description: Unique surrogate identifier for the candidate availability and preference record. Primary key for this data product.

Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Available-candidate-by-job-category matching is a core daily staffing operation. Recruiters run 'show me available candidates for job category X' reports. Linking availability to job_category enables
Reference to the candidate whose availability and placement preferences this record describes. Links to the candidate master profile in the Candidate domain.

Current placement availability state of the candidate. available indicates actively seeking work; on_assignment indicates currently placed; bench indicates between assignments but still engaged; unavailable indicates not seeking work; do_not_contact indicates candidate has opted out of outreach. Critical for bench tracking and redeployment workflows in Bullhorn ATS.
The date through which the candidate has indicated availability, if bounded. Null indicates open-ended availability. Useful for temporary or project-based candidates with known commitment windows.

The earliest date on which the candidate is available to begin a new assignment or placement. Used by recruiters for job order matching and dispatch scheduling. Sourced from Bullhorn ATS candidate record.

Date on which the candidates current availability status was last confirmed by a recruiter or by the candidate directly. Distinct from preference_last_updated_date; specifically tracks confirmation of the availability_status field.

Indicates whether the candidate has been informed of or is aware of the client bill rate associated with their placement. True = candidate is aware of bill rate; False = candidate has not been informed. Relevant for co-employment risk management and transparency compliance.

Maximum hourly or annual pay rate the candidate is targeting, expressed in USD. Represents the upper bound of the candidates compensation expectation. Used alongside desired_pay_rate_min for range-based matching.

Minimum hourly or annual pay rate the candidate is willing to accept, expressed in USD. Used by recruiters to qualify candidates against job order pay rate budgets and to calculate spread and gross margin. Confidential compensation data.

Indicates whether the candidate has been flagged as ineligible for placement (e.g., due to performance issues, compliance violations, or client restrictions). True = do not place; False = eligible for placement. Critical compliance and risk management control.

Reason code or description explaining why the candidate has been flagged as do-not-place. Populated only when do_not_place_flag is True. Examples: client_restriction, compliance_violation, performance_issue, voluntary_withdrawal.
City within which the candidate is willing to accept placements. Used for geographic matching in job order dispatch and sourcing outreach workflows.
Maximum commute radius in miles from the candidates home location that they are willing to travel for an assignment. Used in proximity-based job order matching and sourcing outreach.
U.S. state (two-letter USPS abbreviation) within which the candidate is willing to accept placements. Supports state-level labor law compliance checks (e.g., SUTA, state wage-hour laws).

Date on which a recruiter last made contact with the candidate to confirm or update their availability status. Used to identify stale availability records and trigger re-engagement outreach. Supports candidate engagement analytics.

Free-text recruiter notes regarding the candidates availability, special circumstances, or placement preferences not captured in structured fields (e.g., Candidate available after current project ends mid-month; prefers no weekends).

Number of days of notice the candidate requires before starting a new assignment or must give to their current employer. Used to calculate realistic Time to Start (TTS) for job order fulfillment planning.

Indicates whether the candidate is willing to accept per diem compensation arrangements (daily allowances for travel, lodging, meals). True = willing to accept per diem; False = not interested. Relevant for travel nursing, field services, and project-based placements.

Indicates whether the candidate is open to a Temp-to-Perm (contract-to-hire) arrangement where a temporary assignment may convert to a permanent position. True = open to conversion; False = not interested in conversion. Drives conversion fee eligibility tracking.

Indicates whether the candidates desired pay rate is expressed as an hourly rate, annual salary, or daily per diem rate. Required to normalize compensation comparisons across job orders with different pay structures.

Date on which the candidate last updated their placement preferences (job titles, industries, pay rate, work arrangement, etc.). Used to assess preference data freshness and trigger re-engagement when preferences are stale.

Candidates stated preference for the size of the client organization they wish to work with. Supports matching candidates to client accounts by headcount tier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: startup|small|mid_market|enterprise — promote to reference product if additional tiers are needed]

Candidates stated preference for the type of engagement. temp = temporary/contract; contract_to_hire = Contract-to-Hire (CTH); direct_placement = Direct Hire/Permanent; fte = Full-Time Equivalent salaried role; per_diem = per diem/daily rate engagement. Drives job order matching logic in Bullhorn ATS.
Maximum number of hours per week the candidate is willing to work. Prevents over-scheduling and supports overtime (OT) management. Relevant for FLSA compliance and ACA hour-tracking thresholds.
Minimum number of hours per week the candidate is willing to work. Used to qualify candidates for part-time versus full-time job orders. Supports FLSA classification analysis.

Comma-separated list of industries the candidate prefers to work in (e.g., Healthcare, Technology, Finance). Used to match candidates to client accounts by industry vertical. Aligns with SIC/NAICS industry classification.

Comma-separated list of job titles the candidate is actively seeking. Used for keyword-based job order matching and proactive sourcing outreach in Bullhorn ATS. Examples: Software Engineer, Senior Developer, Tech Lead.

Comma-separated list of specific cities, metro areas, or regions the candidate prefers for placement (e.g., Austin TX, Dallas TX, Remote). More granular than geo_city/geo_state for multi-location preferences.

Candidates preferred work shift. Used in workforce scheduling and dispatch workflows, particularly for light industrial, healthcare, and manufacturing placements managed through Kronos Workforce Ready.
Candidates stated preference for physical work location arrangement: fully on-site, fully remote, or hybrid. Used to filter and match job orders by work model in ATS and VMS platforms.

Free-text or comma-separated description of the candidates preferred workplace culture attributes (e.g., collaborative, fast-paced, diverse, mission-driven). Used in qualitative matching for direct hire and FTE placements.
Timestamp when this availability record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR/CCPA data retention compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Timestamp when this availability record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for identifying recently updated availability records for recruiter workflows.

Indicates whether this candidate has been flagged for proactive redeployment outreach before their current assignment ends. True = flagged for redeployment; False = not flagged. Supports bench management and redeployment workflows to reduce fall-off rate.
Target date by which the recruiter aims to redeploy the candidate into a new assignment. Typically set relative to the current assignment end date. Drives redeployment pipeline reporting in Power BI.
Identifies the operational system of record from which this availability record originated. Supports data lineage and master data management in the Silver Layer. Primary source is Bullhorn ATS/CRM.

The native identifier of this availability record in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn candidate ID or availability record ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.

The channel through which the candidates current availability record was captured or updated (e.g., recruiter_call, job_board, referral, linkedin, career_site, vms_portal). Supports sourcing channel attribution analytics in Power BI.
Maximum percentage of work time the candidate is willing to spend traveling (0–100). Used to qualify candidates for roles with travel requirements. Null if willing_to_travel is False.

Indicates whether the candidate is open to relocating for a permanent or long-term placement opportunity. True = willing to relocate; False = not willing. Relevant for direct hire and FTE placement matching.
Indicates whether the candidate is willing to travel for work assignments. True = willing to travel; False = not willing. Used in conjunction with travel_percentage_max for travel-intensive roles.

Candidates preferred worker classification for engagement: W-2 (employee of staffing firm), 1099 (independent contractor), EOR (Employer of Record), or C2C (Corp-to-Corp). Critical for IRS worker classification compliance and co-employment risk management.
Unique surrogate identifier for each candidate education record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the education data product within the candidate domain.
Reference to the candidate who holds this education record. Links the education history to the candidate master profile in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM candidate entity.

Foreign key linking to candidate.resume. Business justification: Education records are commonly parsed from a candidate's resume. Adding resume_id to education establishes a provenance link from each structured education entry back to the source resume version. Thi

Name of the regional or national accreditation body that accredits the institution (e.g., HLC, SACSCOC, ABET, ACEN for nursing programs). Used to validate institutional legitimacy for compliance-sensitive placements in healthcare, government, and defense sectors.

Specific academic concentration, specialization, or track within the major (e.g., Data Analytics within a Computer Science major, Corporate Finance within an MBA). Provides additional granularity for advanced qualification matching on specialized job orders.

Timestamp when this education record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and CCPA data governance obligations.

Name of the accredited foreign credential evaluation organization that assessed the international degree (e.g., World Education Services (WES), Educational Credential Evaluators (ECE)). Required for OFCCP and I-9 compliance documentation.

Status of the foreign credential evaluation process for internationally-earned degrees. Applicable only when is_international_credential is True. Required for OFCCP compliance on federal contractor placements and I-9 employment eligibility workflows.

Current lifecycle status of the education credential. Tracks whether the credential is active and valid, expired, revoked by the institution, or pending issuance. Drives compliance checks in the credentialing management workflow.

Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the completeness and reliability of this education record, calculated based on field population rates and verification status. Used by Power BI Analytics dashboards to surface low-quality candidate profiles for recruiter review.

Full verbatim name of the degree or certificate as it appears on the official credential document (e.g., Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering, AWS Certified Solutions Architect). Preserves the exact credential title for client-facing submittals and compliance documentation.

Type of academic credential or degree earned or pursued by the candidate. Core qualification field used in job order matching for roles requiring minimum education levels. Broad academic discipline or field in which the degree or certificate was awarded (e.g., Computer Science, Nursing, Business Administration). Used for skills taxonomy mapping and job order qualification matching in the ATS.

Candidates reported Grade Point Average (GPA) on a 4.0 scale, if provided. Optional field sourced from resume parsing or candidate self-submission. Some client job orders specify minimum GPA thresholds for entry-level placements.
The maximum GPA scale used by the institution (e.g., 4.0, 5.0, 10.0). Required to normalize GPA values across institutions with different grading scales for accurate qualification comparison.

Actual or expected date the candidate graduated or completed the degree or certificate program. Null if the candidate did not complete the program. Used for tenure calculations and qualification date-gating on job orders.

Four-digit calendar year of graduation or program completion. Stored separately from graduation_date to support cases where only the year is known (e.g., resume-parsed data). Used for cohort analytics and workforce planning.
Academic honors designation awarded at graduation (e.g., Cum Laude, Magna Cum Laude, Summa Cum Laude). Used for premium candidate profiling and client-facing talent quality reporting.
City where the educational institution is located. Used for geographic context in credential verification and compliance reporting.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the educational institution is located. Supports international credential evaluation and EEOC/OFCCP reporting.

Full legal name of the educational institution attended by the candidate (e.g., University of Michigan, DeVry Institute, Coursera). Used for qualification matching and credential verification workflows.
Two-letter US state abbreviation (or equivalent province/territory code) where the educational institution is located. Used for regional compliance and credential verification routing.

Categorical classification of the educational institution. Drives qualification matching logic for job orders requiring specific institution types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: university|community_college|trade_school|bootcamp|online_platform|high_school|vocational|seminary|other — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the institution holds recognized regional or national accreditation (True). Many client job orders, particularly in healthcare, government, and defense, require degrees from accredited institutions. Drives qualification gate logic in the ATS.

Indicates whether the candidate completed and received the degree or certificate (True) or attended without completing (False). Critical for qualification matching — incomplete degrees may not satisfy job order minimum education requirements.

Soft-delete flag indicating whether this education record has been logically deleted (True) from the active candidate profile without physical removal. Supports GDPR right-to-erasure workflows and CCPA deletion request processing while preserving audit history.

Flags this education record as the candidates highest level of academic achievement (True). Used by ATS qualification matching logic to quickly identify the most advanced credential without scanning all education records.

Indicates whether the education credential was earned at a non-US institution (True) and therefore may require foreign credential evaluation. Triggers the international credential evaluation workflow for EEOC and OFCCP compliance on federal contracts.

Indicates whether the education record has been independently verified by a third-party credential verification service (True) or is based solely on candidate self-reported data (False). Critical for compliance on job orders requiring verified credentials.

Primary academic concentration or major declared by the candidate within their degree program (e.g., Software Engineering, Accounting, Registered Nursing). Provides granular qualification detail beyond field of study for precise job order matching.
Secondary academic concentration or minor declared by the candidate within their degree program. Supplementary qualification data used for advanced candidate matching and talent pool analytics.

Free-text field for recruiter or system-generated notes related to this education record (e.g., discrepancies found during verification, candidate clarifications, special circumstances). Supports recruiter workflow in Bullhorn ATS.

The channel or method through which this education record was originally captured. Tracks whether data originated from automated resume parsing, candidate self-entry via portal, recruiter manual entry in Bullhorn, or a background check report. Supports data quality scoring and sourcing analytics. The operational system of record from which this education record was ingested into the Silver Layer. The native record identifier for this education entry in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn education record ID, SAP SuccessFactors education history ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and operational systems.
Date the candidate began attending the educational institution or program. Used to calculate duration of study and to validate enrollment periods during credential verification.

Title of the candidates thesis or dissertation for graduate-level degrees (Masters or Doctorate). Used for highly specialized technical placements where research expertise is a qualification criterion.

Timestamp of the most recent modification to this education record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and SOC 2 Type II audit compliance.

Date on which the education credential was verified by the designated verification source. Used for compliance audit trails and to determine if re-verification is required based on credential age policies.

External reference or case number assigned by the credential verification provider (e.g., Sterling BGC order ID, National Student Clearinghouse transaction ID). Enables traceability back to the source verification record for audit and dispute resolution.

The source or method used to verify the education credential. Identifies whether verification was performed via Sterling Background Check, National Student Clearinghouse, direct institution contact, or is candidate self-reported. Required for FCRA compliance documentation.

Externally-visible business identifier for this pay rate agreement, used in payroll handoff documents, offer letters, and TempWorks Payroll integration. Format: PRA- followed by 8-12 digits.

Current lifecycle state of the pay rate agreement. Draft indicates initial capture; pending_approval awaits manager sign-off; approved is authorized but not yet effective; active is currently in force; expired has passed its end date; cancelled was voided before activation.

The current approval workflow status for this pay rate agreement. Pending awaits review; approved indicates authorization by the designated approver; rejected requires renegotiation; escalated has been referred to senior management for exception handling.
The date and time at which the pay rate agreement was formally approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved. Used for SLA tracking on approval turnaround time.

The employer burden rate percentage applied on top of the base pay rate to account for employer-side costs including FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation (Workers Comp), and benefits. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 18.50 for 18.50%). Used in gross margin and spread calculations.
The date and time when this pay rate agreement record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting SOC 2 Type II compliance and data lineage tracking.
The date on which this pay rate agreement becomes binding and active. Pay calculations in TempWorks Payroll use this date to determine which rate applies for a given pay period.

The type of staffing engagement for which this pay rate agreement applies. Temp (temporary), contract, temp-to-perm (Contract-to-Hire), direct hire (permanent placement), or SOW (Statement of Work) project-based engagement. Drives payroll and billing treatment.

The date on which this pay rate agreement ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended agreements. When populated, triggers a rate review or renewal workflow. Aligns with assignment end date or contract term.

business_glossary_term: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Exemption Status
description: Indicates whether the candidate is classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Non-exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay; exempt workers are not. Critical for OT rate applicability and payroll compliance.

The multiplier applied to the base pay rate for hours worked on designated holidays (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half, 2.0 for double time). Null if no special holiday rate applies. Used in TempWorks Payroll holiday pay calculations.

Indicates whether the pay rate in this agreement is open to further negotiation by the candidate or recruiter. True if negotiable; False if the rate is fixed (e.g., set by a VMS program or client rate card). Supports recruiter workflow guidance.

The date and time when this pay rate agreement record was most recently modified. Audit trail field supporting change tracking, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and Silver layer incremental processing in Databricks.

The maximum number of hours per week the candidate is authorized to work under this agreement before requiring manager or client approval for additional hours. Supports overtime cost control and ACA compliance monitoring.

The minimum number of hours per week or pay period guaranteed to the candidate under this agreement, regardless of actual hours worked. Common in certain contract arrangements. Null if no minimum guarantee applies.

The date on which the pay rate was negotiated and agreed between the recruiter and the candidate. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this agreement. Distinct from the effective date when the rate becomes active.

Free-text field for recruiter or manager notes related to this pay rate agreement, such as special negotiation context, candidate expectations, client constraints, or exception justifications. Supports operational context for payroll and compliance teams.

The number of hours worked in a week (or day, depending on state law) after which overtime rates apply. Defaults to 40 hours per week per FLSA but may be lower for certain states (e.g., 8 hours/day in California). Drives OT rate application in TempWorks Payroll.

Indicates whether this pay rate agreement is subject to prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act, Service Contract Act, or applicable state prevailing wage laws (common for government and public sector contracts). True if prevailing wage rules apply.

The government-mandated prevailing wage rate applicable to this engagement when prevailing_wage_flag is True. The base_pay_rate must be equal to or greater than this value to maintain compliance. Null if prevailing wage does not apply.

Free-text or coded explanation for why this pay rate agreement was created or modified (e.g., initial placement, merit increase, market adjustment, assignment extension, client rate change, cost-of-living adjustment). Supports audit and analytics.

The operational system of record from which this pay rate agreement originated. Bullhorn for ATS-driven agreements; TempWorks for payroll-initiated rates; Beeline for VMS-driven rate cards; Manual for agreements entered directly into the data platform.

The native identifier of this pay rate agreement record in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn placement pay rate ID, TempWorks pay rate record ID). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and integration traceability.
Two-letter US state code where the candidate performs work under this agreement. Determines applicable state wage laws, SUTA rates, workers comp rates, and state income tax withholding requirements.

Unique system-generated identifier for the candidate profile record. Primary key and anchor for all child entities in the candidate domain. MASTER_PARTY role — all downstream candidate domain products resolve to this record via FK.

Primary street address line for the candidates current residence. Used for tax jurisdiction determination (SUTA, FUTA, state income tax withholding), workers compensation policy assignment, and I-9 documentation.
Secondary address line for the candidates residence (apartment, suite, unit number). Supplements address_line1 for complete mailing address used in payroll tax filings and compliance documentation.

External candidate identifier assigned by the source Applicant Tracking System (ATS), e.g., Bullhorn candidate record ID. Used for cross-system reconciliation and deduplication between the lakehouse and the operational ATS.

Attribution model applied to assign sourcing credit for this candidate. First-touch = credit to initial channel; Last-touch = credit to final channel before application; Multi-touch = distributed credit; Linear = equal credit across all touchpoints. Drives cost-per-source and channel ROI calculations.

Earliest date the candidate is available to begin a new assignment or placement. Critical for Time to Fill (TTF) and Time to Start (TTS) KPI calculations and for matching candidates to job orders with specific start date requirements.
Candidates date of birth in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for age verification, I-9 eligibility, background check identity matching, and EEOC reporting. Stored masked or access-controlled per GDPR/CCPA.

Current lifecycle status of the candidate in the ATS talent pool. Active = available and engaged; Placed = currently on assignment; On Bench = unassigned but available for redeployment; Inactive = not currently pursuing opportunities; Do Not Use = flagged for exclusion; Pending Onboarding = offer accepted, onboarding in progress.

Classification of the candidates engagement model with the staffing firm. W-2 = employee on firms payroll; 1099 = independent contractor (IC); EOR = employer of record arrangement; C2C = corp-to-corp; PEO = professional employer organization. Drives payroll tax treatment, workers comp assignment, and co-employment risk assessment.

business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Consent Flag
description: Indicates whether the candidate has acknowledged CCPA privacy rights disclosure and provided consent for data processing. Required for California residents. Supports CCPA opt-out and data deletion request workflows.
City of the candidates current residential address. Used for geographic talent pool analytics, commute radius matching, and state/local tax jurisdiction determination.

Monetary cost attributed to sourcing this specific candidate, in USD. Includes job board fees, advertising spend, referral bonuses, or RPO campaign costs allocated per candidate. Used for sourcing ROI and cost-per-hire analytics.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the candidates current residential address (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Required for international operations, GDPR applicability determination, and work authorization validation.

Timestamp when the candidate profile record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category. Used for data lineage, SLA measurement, and pipeline age calculations.

Data governance classification level assigned to this candidate profile record. Drives access control policies, data masking rules, and retention schedules. Restricted = highest sensitivity (PII-heavy); Confidential = sensitive business data; Internal = standard operational; Public = no sensitivity.

Indicates whether the candidate has opted out of all recruiter outreach and communications. When True, all automated and manual contact must cease immediately. Enforced per GDPR right to erasure/restriction and CCPA opt-out rights.

Candidates current employment situation at the time of last profile update. Used to prioritize outreach urgency, assess availability, and tailor placement strategy (temp vs. perm vs. contract-to-hire).
Legal given name of the candidate as recorded in the ATS. Used for identity verification, onboarding documentation (I-9, W-2), and candidate communications.

Legal family name (surname) of the candidate as recorded in the ATS. Required for identity verification, payroll tax filings (W-2, 1099), and compliance documentation.

Indicates whether this credential instance currently satisfies placement eligibility requirements. Computed and stored as an operational flag to enable fast eligibility gate checks in the Bullhorn ATS without real-time recalculation. True = eligible; False = ineligible.

Indicates whether this is the primary active instance of this credential type for the candidate when multiple instances exist (e.g., multi-state licenses, renewed credentials with overlapping validity). True = primary instance used for eligibility checks.

The date on which the issuing authority originally issued or granted this credential to the candidate. Used to calculate credential age, validity windows, and continuing education unit (CEU) cycle start dates.

The name of the organization, government body, or professional association that issued this credential (e.g., State of California Board of Registered Nursing, OSHA Training Institute, PMI, DOT). Used for primary source verification routing.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country in which this credential was issued. Supports international staffing operations and GDPR/CCPA compliance for cross-border credential data.

The U.S. state (two-letter USPS abbreviation) or country subdivision in which this credential was issued. Critical for multi-state license portability analysis and compact license determinations (e.g., Nurse Licensure Compact).

The manufacturer lot number of the vaccine administered for immunization credentials. Applicable only when credential_category = immunization. Required for healthcare facility compliance documentation and adverse event tracking.

The updated expiration date assigned to this credential instance upon successful renewal approval. Populated when renewal_status = approved. Replaces the prior expiration_date for ongoing validity tracking.

Free-text field for credentialing coordinators to capture supplemental context about this credential instance — e.g., adjudication rationale for BGC consider results, special conditions attached to a license, or renewal exception approvals.

Indicates whether a polygraph examination was completed as part of the security clearance investigation. Applicable only when credential_category = security_clearance. Required for TS/SCI and certain agency-specific clearances.

The date on which the candidate or staffing coordinator initiated the renewal application with the issuing authority. Marks the start of the renewal lifecycle and is used to calculate renewal processing lead time.
The name of the organization or body responsible for processing the credential renewal (may differ from the original issuing authority in cases of delegated renewal processing).
The monetary fee charged by the issuing authority for processing the credential renewal. Used for cost tracking, candidate reimbursement workflows, and workforce program budgeting.
Indicates which party is responsible for paying the credential renewal fee. Drives accounts payable routing and candidate reimbursement processing in Oracle NetSuite ERP.

Current state of the credential renewal lifecycle for this instance. Drives renewal workflow routing and compliance alerting. Pending_documentation indicates the candidate must supply additional materials before the renewal authority can process the application.

The date on which the completed renewal application and supporting documentation were formally submitted to the issuing authority. Distinct from renewal_application_date (initiation) — this is the official submission date.

The originating system from which this credential record was ingested or created. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and reconciliation between Sterling BGC, manual uploads, and third-party registries.

The quantitative titer test result indicating immunity level for immunization credentials (e.g., 1:8 positive for Hepatitis B). Applicable only when credential_category = immunization. Required for healthcare staffing placements in clinical settings.
The timestamp when this credential instance record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for audit trail compliance.

The name or system identifier of the person or automated process that performed the most recent verification of this credential. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements for credentialing compliance reviews.

The date on which this credential package version was formally approved by the compliance officer or designated authority. Required for audit trail and change management documentation.

Pipe-delimited list of required BGC components for this package (e.g., criminal_check|employment_verification|education_verification|mvr|oig_exclusion|sex_offender_registry|credit_check). Drives the BGC order configuration in Sterling.

The Sterling Background Check package code that defines the specific BGC components required (e.g., criminal check, employment verification, education verification, MVR, OIG exclusion). Maps directly to a Sterling-configured screening package.

Indicates whether E-Verify electronic employment eligibility confirmation is required for this credential package. Mandatory for federal contractors and certain state-regulated placements.

The date on which this credential package version expires and is no longer valid for new placements. Null for open-ended packages. Supports version lifecycle management and ensures compliance with updated client or regulatory requirements.

description: Indicates whether I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification is required as part of this credential package. Typically true for all packages but may be explicitly flagged for compliance audit purposes.
Indicates whether proof of immunization records is required as part of this credential package. Primarily applicable to healthcare staffing verticals (e.g., flu shot, hepatitis B, MMR, TB test).

The staffing industry vertical this credential package is designed to serve (e.g., healthcare, information_technology, light_industrial). Drives vertical-specific credentialing workflows and compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|light_industrial|finance|government|skilled_trades|education|other — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the drug screen component of this package must comply with U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations (49 CFR Part 40). Applicable for transportation, logistics, and safety-sensitive positions.
Indicates whether professional license verification is a mandatory component of this credential package. When true, the required_license_types field specifies which license types must be verified.

Indicates whether an Office of Inspector General (OIG) exclusion list check is required. Mandatory for healthcare placements to ensure candidates are not excluded from federally funded programs (Medicare/Medicaid).

Code or identifier referencing the onboarding checklist template in SAP SuccessFactors or the ATS that is automatically assigned to candidates when this credential package is applied. Ensures consistent onboarding task generation.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this credential package across systems (e.g., HC-TRV-NURSE-V2, FED-IT-CLR-V1, LI-WH-STD-V3). Used for cross-system reference in Bullhorn, Sterling, and Beeline VMS.

Detailed narrative description of the credential package, including its intended use case, applicable roles, and any special compliance notes. Used by recruiters and compliance teams to understand the full scope of requirements.

Human-readable name of the credential package (e.g., Healthcare Travel Nurse Package, Federal Government IT Clearance Package, Light Industrial Warehouse Package). Used in candidate-facing and recruiter-facing workflows.

Current lifecycle state of the credential package. Active packages are available for assignment to candidates. Superseded packages have been replaced by a newer version. Archived packages are retained for historical compliance reference.

Integer version number of this credential package definition, incremented each time the package requirements are materially changed. Enables change tracking and ensures candidates are evaluated against the correct version at time of placement.
Indicates whether a pre-placement physical examination is required as part of this credential package. Common for healthcare, light industrial, and safety-sensitive positions.

The type of placement engagement this credential package applies to: temporary, contract, temp-to-perm (Contract-to-Hire), direct hire, or Statement of Work (SOW). Different placement types may require different credentialing depth.
The number of days before credential expiration that the renewal process should be initiated. Used to trigger automated renewal alerts and recruiter tasks in the ATS (e.g., 30 days before expiry).

Pipe-delimited list of skills assessment or competency test codes required by this package (e.g., MS_OFFICE_BASIC|TYPING_40WPM|FORKLIFT_WRITTEN|CLINICAL_SKILLS_RN). Drives assessment assignment in the ATS onboarding workflow.
Pipe-delimited list of required immunizations or health screenings for this credential package (e.g., FLU|HEP_B|MMR|VARICELLA|TB_TEST|COVID_VAX). Null if immunization_required is false.

Pipe-delimited list of professional license types that must be verified as part of this credential package (e.g., RN|LPN|CNA|CDL_A|PMP|CISSP). Drives license verification workflows in Sterling and the credentialing team.

Pipe-delimited list of training course codes or LMS module identifiers that must be completed as part of this credential package (e.g., OSHA_10|HIPAA_PRIVACY|BLOODBORNE_PATHOGENS|FORKLIFT_CERT). Maps to SAP SuccessFactors LMS course catalog.
The minimum government security clearance level required by this credential package (e.g., Public Trust, Secret, Top Secret, TS/SCI). Null if security_clearance_required is false.
Indicates whether a government security clearance is required as part of this credential package. Applicable to federal government IT and defense staffing placements.

Indicates whether a formal skills assessment or competency test is required as part of this credential package. When true, the required_assessment_codes field specifies which assessments must be passed.
Indicates whether completion of specific training courses or modules is a mandatory component of this credential package. When true, required_training_codes specifies the training requirements.
The timestamp when this credential package record was last modified. Tracks changes to package requirements over time and supports version control and compliance audit trails.

Reference to the Vendor Management System (VMS) program under which this credential requirement is enforced. Applicable for Managed Service Provider (MSP) and contingent workforce programs managed through Beeline VMS where program-level credentialing rules apply.

Maximum number of days before a credentials expiration date that it is still considered acceptable for placement. For example, a value of 30 means a credential expiring within 30 days of the assignment start date is not acceptable. Drives automated expiration alerts and gap analysis.

Indicates whether this credential must be verified and on file before the candidates assignment start date. When True, the credential is a pre-placement gate. When False, the credential may be obtained during or after onboarding within a grace period.
Date on which this credential requirement was formally approved by the designated approver. Null for requirements that do not require explicit approval. Supports compliance audit trails.

Minimum passing score (as a percentage) required on a skills assessment to satisfy this credential requirement. Applicable when credential_category is ASSESSMENT. Null when no minimum score threshold applies or when the credential is pass/fail without a numeric score.

The specific Background Check (BGC) package code from Sterling Background Check that must be ordered for this requirement. Applicable when credential_category is BGC. Maps to the client_package_code used in the bgc_order product to ensure the correct screening scope is ordered.

Reference to the specific clause, section, or exhibit in the clients Master Service Agreement (MSA) or Statement of Work (SOW) that mandates this credential requirement. Supports compliance audit trails and contract-driven credentialing enforcement. Confidential as it references contract terms.

Indicates whether this requirement record represents a client-specific override of the default credential requirement for this placement type or job order. When True, the client_account_id is the source of the override rule, superseding the standard requirement.
Timestamp when this credential requirement record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trails, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.

High-level category classifying the type of credential required. Distinguishes professional licenses, certifications, Background Check (BGC) packages, drug screen panels, training completions, skills assessments, and security clearances. Enables category-level reporting and gap analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LICENSE|CERTIFICATION|BGC|DRUG_SCREEN|TRAINING|ASSESSMENT|CLEARANCE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Specifies the drug screening panel required (e.g., 5-Panel, 10-Panel, DOT, Hair Follicle). Applicable when credential_category is DRUG_SCREEN. Drives the correct test order through the screening vendor.

Date on which this credential requirement becomes enforceable. Requirements with a future effective date are staged for activation. Supports versioning of credentialing rules when client contracts or regulatory mandates change.
Indicates whether E-Verify employment eligibility verification is required for placements under this requirement. Applicable for federal contractor positions and states with mandatory E-Verify laws.

Date on which this credential requirement record ceases to be enforceable. Null indicates the requirement is open-ended with no planned end date. Used to manage time-bound client contract requirements or regulatory mandates with sunset provisions.

Indicates whether a gap in this credential (missing or expired) will block a candidate submittal in the Applicant Tracking System (ATS). When True, the ATS prevents submission until the credential is satisfied. When False, a warning is raised but submission is permitted.

Number of days after assignment start within which a non-pre-placement credential must be obtained and verified. Applicable only when applies_to_assignment_start is False. Enables phased onboarding for credentials that can be completed post-start.

Indicates whether a completed I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification form is required before placement under this requirement. Standard for all US-based placements but may be explicitly flagged for audit and compliance tracking purposes.

The staffing industry vertical for which this credential requirement applies. Healthcare may require Joint Commission credentials; Government may require security clearances; Skilled Trades may require OSHA certifications. Enables vertical-specific credentialing rule enforcement.

Indicates whether this credential is a hard requirement (mandatory) or a preferred qualification. When True, a candidate cannot be submitted or placed without satisfying this credential. When False, the credential is preferred but not blocking.

Name of the organization or regulatory body that issues or governs this credential (e.g., State Board of Nursing, OSHA, CompTIA, PMI, DOT). Used for primary source verification routing and compliance documentation.

Two-letter US state code indicating the jurisdiction in which the professional license must be valid. Applicable for state-issued licenses such as RN, LPN, CPA, or contractor licenses. Null when the credential is federally issued or not state-specific.

Maximum age in days that a credential result remains valid from its completion or issue date. For example, a BGC result may only be valid for 365 days. After this period, a new credential must be obtained. Null indicates no age restriction.

Indicates whether an Office of Inspector General (OIG) exclusion list check is required as part of this credential requirement. Mandatory for healthcare staffing placements to ensure candidates are not excluded from federally funded programs.

Classification of the placement type to which this credential requirement applies when not tied to a specific job order. Supports requirement templates across placement categories such as Contract, Temp-to-Perm (Contract-to-Hire), Direct Hire, Statement of Work (SOW), or Per Diem engagements.

Number of days before credential expiration that a renewal notification should be triggered. Enables proactive credentialing management to prevent assignment disruptions due to lapsed credentials. Applicable only when renewal_required is True.

Indicates whether this credential requires periodic renewal. When True, the system tracks expiration and triggers renewal workflows. When False, the credential is a one-time requirement (e.g., a completed training module with no expiry).

Detailed narrative description of the credential requirement including scope, acceptable documentation, and any special instructions for recruiters or candidates. Supports recruiter guidance and candidate communication during the onboarding process.

Human-readable name or label for this credential requirement as displayed in the ATS, VMS, or credentialing portal. Examples: RN License - CA, OSHA 10 Certification, 7-Year Criminal Background Check, 10-Panel Drug Screen. Used for recruiter-facing display and reporting.

Current lifecycle state of this credential requirement record. ACTIVE requirements are enforced during candidate submission and onboarding. SUPERSEDED records have been replaced by a newer version. DRAFT records are pending approval before enforcement.

Required federal security clearance level for government staffing placements. Applicable when industry_vertical is GOVERNMENT. Values align with US federal clearance tiers: Public Trust, Secret, Top Secret, and Top Secret/Sensitive Compartmented Information (TS/SCI).

Operational system of record from which this credential requirement originated. Bullhorn ATS/CRM for job-order-level requirements, Beeline VMS for program-level requirements, SAP SuccessFactors for onboarding-driven requirements, Sterling for BGC package mandates, or MANUAL for requirements entered directly.

The native identifier of this credential requirement record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn requirement ID, Beeline compliance rule ID). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and bi-directional sync with source systems.

Identifier for the specific training course or Learning Management System (LMS) module required when credential_category is TRAINING. Maps to the course catalog in SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System. Examples include OSHA 10, OSHA 30, HIPAA Awareness, or client-specific safety training.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this credential requirement record. Used for change detection, incremental data loads, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.

Required level of verification rigor for this credential. PRIMARY_SOURCE requires direct confirmation from the issuing authority (e.g., state licensing board). SELF_REPORTED accepts candidate attestation. THIRD_PARTY requires an independent verification vendor such as Sterling. EMPLOYER_VERIFIED requires confirmation from a prior employer.

Sequential version number of this credential requirement record. Incremented each time the requirement is modified. Supports audit trails and historical analysis of how credentialing rules have evolved over time for a given job order or placement type.

Indicates whether a client or compliance officer can grant a waiver for this credential requirement on a case-by-case basis. When True, a waiver workflow is available in the ATS. When False, no exceptions are permitted regardless of circumstances.

The organizational role level required to approve a waiver for this credential requirement. Applicable only when waiver_allowed is True. Drives the waiver approval workflow routing in the ATS and compliance management system.

Type of organization or entity that issues or validates this credential (e.g., government agency, professional association, third-party vendor).

Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context about this credential type that does not fit in other structured fields.
Typical number of days required to complete verification or obtain results for this credential type. Null if processing time is not tracked or highly variable.
Number of days before expiration when renewal reminders should be triggered for candidates holding this credential type. Null if no reminder is configured.

Indicates whether this credential type has an expiration date and requires periodic renewal (True) or is valid indefinitely once obtained (False).

The date the candidate formally initiated a dispute of the background check results with the screening vendor or consumer reporting agency, as permitted under FCRA. Null if no dispute was filed.
The date the candidates dispute was resolved by the screening vendor. Null if no dispute was filed or if dispute is still pending.

Result of the drug screening component. negative = no substances detected; positive = prohibited substance detected; dilute = specimen did not meet concentration thresholds; cancelled = specimen could not be tested.
Result of the education credential verification component, confirming degrees, diplomas, and certifications as reported by the candidate.
Result of the employment history verification component, confirming prior employer details, dates of employment, and job titles as reported by the candidate.
The date on which the background check results expire and a new check may be required per client, regulatory, or company policy. Supports credential expiration tracking and renewal workflows.
The date the final adverse action decision was communicated to the candidate, following the mandatory waiting period after the pre-adverse action notice. Null if adverse action was not finalized.

The staffing industry vertical for which this background check was ordered. Different verticals have distinct credentialing and screening mandates (e.g., healthcare requires OIG exclusion checks; government requires security clearance checks).
Result of the motor vehicle record check component, ordered for roles requiring driving. Evaluates license validity, violations, and suspensions.
Free-text field for recruiter, compliance officer, or adjudicator notes related to this background check order, such as special instructions, exception approvals, or adjudication rationale.

business_glossary_term: Office of Inspector General (OIG) Exclusion Check Result
description: Result of the OIG exclusion list check, required for healthcare staffing placements to ensure the candidate is not excluded from participation in federal healthcare programs (Medicare, Medicaid).
The date and time the background check order was placed with the screening vendor. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for this background check order, used in communications with the candidate, client, and screening vendor.

Current lifecycle status of the background check order. ordered = submitted to vendor; in_progress = vendor is processing; completed = results received; disputed = candidate has filed a dispute; cancelled = order voided before completion.
The internal Staffing Hr user (recruiter, coordinator, or compliance officer) who initiated and submitted the background check order. Used for audit trail and accountability.

The consolidated result returned by the screening vendor summarizing all checks in the package. clear = all checks passed; consider = one or more checks returned findings requiring review; suspended = check could not be completed.

The actual time in minutes the candidate spent completing the assessment, derived from start and end timestamps. Used for proctoring compliance and assessment integrity monitoring.

Indicates whether this assessment record represents the most recent and active result for the candidate on this skill. Set to false for superseded attempts. Supports efficient querying of current credential status without full history scans.
Indicates whether the assessment was administered under supervised proctoring conditions (in-person or remote). Affects result credibility weighting and client acceptance of assessment outcomes.
The language in which the assessment was administered, using ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language codes (e.g., EN, ES, FR). Supports multilingual workforce programs and EEOC non-discrimination compliance.

The maximum number of times a candidate is permitted to attempt this assessment before a waiting period or permanent disqualification applies. Enforced per assessment provider policy or client mandate.
The highest achievable score on this assessment instrument. Required to compute score percentage and normalize results across different assessment providers and versions.
The minimum score required to achieve a passing result on this assessment. May be set by the staffing firm, client, or assessment provider. Used to determine pass/fail result status.
Indicates whether the pass_threshold value is expressed as a raw score, a percentage, or a percentile rank. Required to correctly interpret and apply the threshold in result determination logic.

The candidates performance relative to the normative population, expressed as a percentile (0-100). Indicates what percentage of the reference population scored at or below this candidate. Supports QoS (Quality of Submission) candidate ranking.
Qualitative proficiency tier assigned to the candidate based on their assessment score. Used for candidate ranking, job matching, and QoS reporting in recruiter workflows.

The external assessment product code or catalog identifier assigned by the assessment provider (e.g., Criteria Corp test ID, Wonderlic WPT-Q). Enables cross-referencing with provider platforms and normative databases.
Classification of the assessment provider as internal (staffing firm-administered), third-party vendor, client-mandated, or industry body. Affects scoring normalization and result validity rules.
The candidates actual numeric score as returned by the assessment instrument before normalization or scaling. Represents the principal quantitative result of the assessment event.

Free-text notes entered by the recruiter or assessor regarding the assessment outcome, candidate performance observations, or any anomalies noted during administration. Supports qualitative context for QoS reporting.

The official outcome of the skills assessment for the candidate. Determines candidate eligibility for submittal to job orders requiring this skill qualification. Core field for QoS (Quality of Submission) tracking.
The date on which the assessment result was reviewed and approved by the designated reviewer. Supports credentialing workflow audit trails and compliance documentation.

Name of the internal recruiter, credentialing specialist, or assessor who reviewed and validated the assessment result. Applicable for practical evaluations and trade skills tests requiring human judgment.
The candidates score expressed as a percentage of the maximum possible score (raw_score / max_possible_score * 100). Enables standardized comparison across assessments with different scales.

The operational system of record from which this skills assessment record originated (e.g., Bullhorn ATS/CRM, SAP SuccessFactors, Sterling, internal platform). Supports data lineage and Silver layer reconciliation.

The native identifier of this assessment record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn credential ID, SAP SuccessFactors assessment ID). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the system of record.

The industry vertical or staffing practice area for which this assessment is relevant. The date by which the candidates work authorization must be re-verified to maintain placement eligibility. Typically set to the authorized_end_date or a configurable number of days prior. Null if reverification_required is False. Drives proactive expiration alerts and compliance workflows.

Indicates whether the candidates work authorization requires re-verification before or upon expiration (e.g., EAD renewals, OPT extensions, TN renewals). Indicates whether the candidate requires employer sponsorship (e.g., H-1B petition, PERM labor certification) to maintain or obtain work authorization. The timestamp when this work authorization record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale records, support incremental data loads in the Databricks Silver layer, and maintain audit trail integrity for immigration compliance reviews. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The official case number assigned by the United States Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) for the candidates immigration petition or application (e.g., I-129 H-1B petition receipt number). Format: three-letter service center code followed by 10 digits (e.g., EAC2312345678). Used to track petition status and employer sponsorship obligations.

The date on which the work authorization documents were verified by the staffing firm. Used to establish the verification timeline for I-9 compliance audits and to calculate re-verification due dates for time-limited authorizations.

The method used to verify the candidates work authorization documents. document_review = physical or digital document inspection; e_verify = electronic verification via DHS E-Verify system; manual = manual internal review; third_party_vendor = verification performed by a credentialing vendor such as Sterling. The name or user identifier of the Staffing Hr compliance officer, recruiter, or authorized representative who performed the work authorization verification. Supports audit trail requirements and accountability for I-9 and immigration compliance.

The specific visa category or classification code as issued by the relevant immigration authority (e.g., H-1B, L-1A, L-1B, O-1A, O-1B, TN-1, TN-2, F-1 OPT, J-1). Used in split-credit scenarios where multiple recruiters share commission. Sum of commission splits across all eligible assignments for a placement typically equals 1.00 (100%). Confidential business data.

The target number of candidate submittals this team member is expected to deliver for this requisition. Used for performance tracking and workload planning. May be set based on historical conversion rates, client requirements, or internal service level agreements (SLAs).

Qualitative performance rating for this team member on this specific assignment, typically assessed at assignment closure or during periodic reviews. Reflects quality of submittals, client feedback, time-to-fill performance, and overall contribution to order fulfillment. Used for coaching, development, and performance management.

The number of successful placements this team member has achieved for this requisition. Typically 0 or 1 for single-headcount orders, but may be higher for multi-headcount requisitions. Used for commission calculations and performance attribution.

Boolean indicator (True/False) identifying whether this team member is the primary or lead owner of the requisition. True indicates the main point of accountability; False indicates a supporting or secondary role. Only one assignment per requisition should typically have this flag set to True.

The number of times this requisition has been reassigned to different team members prior to this current assignment. High reassignment counts may indicate order difficulty, client issues, or internal capacity challenges. Used for operational analytics and process improvement.

Free-text or semi-structured field indicating the primary sourcing channels or strategies this team member is focusing on for this requisition (e.g., LinkedIn Recruiter, internal database, employee referrals, job boards, vendor management system (VMS) talent pools). The number of calendar days from assignment start date to the first candidate submittal delivered by this team member for this requisition. Key performance indicator (KPI) for recruiter responsiveness and sourcing efficiency. Null if no submittals have been made yet.

The percentage of workload or credit allocated to this team member for this requisition, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.50 for 50%, 1.00 for 100%). Used in split-credit scenarios where multiple recruiters share responsibility for a single order. Sum of workload weights across all active assignments for a requisition typically equals 1.00 (100%). Critical for commission splits, performance attribution, and capacity planning.

Defines the scope at which this burden rate is applied: enterprise_wide (default for all placements), client_specific (negotiated rate for a specific client account), assignment_specific (applied to a single placement or assignment), or branch_specific (applicable to a specific branch or division).
Date on which this burden rate structure was formally approved by the authorized approver. Required for audit trail and SOC 2 change management compliance.

Employer-paid benefits cost expressed as a percentage of gross wages included in the burden rate. Covers health insurance, dental, vision, 401(k) match, and other statutory or voluntary benefits provided to workers. Varies by worker classification and benefit plan enrollment.

Code identifying the staffing branch or division to which this burden rate applies when applicability_scope is branch_specific. Supports multi-branch operations where burden structures differ by geographic market or business unit.

Date from which this burden rate structure becomes effective and is applied to payroll cost calculations, spread analysis, and bill rate margin validation. Aligns with payroll period start dates.

Type of staffing engagement to which this burden rate applies. Temporary and contract placements typically carry higher WC and benefits burden; direct hire placements may carry only a placement fee structure. Temp-to-perm engagements may have conversion fee implications.

Date on which this burden rate structure ceases to be effective. Null indicates an open-ended rate with no scheduled expiration. Used to enforce rate versioning and ensure correct rate application during payroll processing.

business_glossary_term: Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) Employer Rate
description: Employers share of FICA tax expressed as a percentage of gross wages (e.g., 7.6500 for 7.65%). Comprises Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) employer contributions. Applied to gross pay up to applicable wage bases per IRS regulations.

Federal Unemployment Tax Act employer tax rate expressed as a percentage of gross wages (e.g., 0.6000 for 0.60% net after state credit). Applied to the first $7,000 of each employees wages per calendar year per IRS regulations.

General liability insurance cost expressed as a percentage of gross payroll included in the employer burden structure. Covers third-party bodily injury and property damage claims arising from staffing operations.

Indicates whether this burden rate applies to an Employer of Record (EOR) engagement model where the staffing firm acts as the legal employer of record for co-employment or client-directed workforce arrangements. EOR models carry distinct burden and compliance obligations.

Indicates whether this burden rate applies to a Professional Employer Organization (PEO) co-employment model where the PEO assumes employer tax and benefits obligations. PEO burden structures differ from standard staffing burden rates.

Target markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate, incorporating the total burden and desired gross margin. Used in spread analysis and client billing validation. Expressed as a percentage of pay rate (e.g., 45.00 = 45% markup).

Free-text field for payroll analysts or finance staff to document special conditions, exceptions, carrier negotiation details, or regulatory basis for this burden rate configuration. Supports audit documentation and knowledge transfer.

Internal overhead allocation expressed as a percentage of gross wages included in the burden rate. Covers branch operating costs, recruiter commissions, back-office administration, and other indirect costs attributable to workforce deployment.
Maximum hourly pay rate threshold (in USD) at which this burden rate structure is applicable. Defines the upper bound of the pay rate band for this burden rate. Null indicates no upper limit.

Minimum hourly pay rate threshold (in USD) at which this burden rate structure is applicable. Used to define pay rate banding for tiered burden rate structures where different pay levels carry different overhead or benefits loads.
Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this burden rate structure (e.g., BR-CA-WC-5403-2024). Used in billing systems, VMS integrations, and rate card references.
Human-readable descriptive name for the burden rate structure (e.g., California Light Industrial WC Class 5403 2024). Used in reporting, dashboards, and analyst-facing tools.

Origin of the burden rate values indicating how the rate was established: manual (entered by payroll/finance staff), system_calculated (derived by TempWorks Payroll engine), carrier_provided (supplied by WC or GL insurance carrier), or state_mandated (set by state regulatory authority).

Classification of the burden rate structure indicating its scope of applicability: standard (default enterprise-wide), client_specific (negotiated for a client), assignment_specific (tied to a single placement), state_override (state-level regulatory override), or wc_class_specific (Workers Compensation class-driven rate).

Two-letter U.S. state code (ISO 3166-2:US) identifying the state jurisdiction to which this burden rate applies. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance, SUTA rate assignment, and state-specific Workers Compensation rate overrides.

State Unemployment Tax Act employer contribution rate expressed as a percentage of gross wages for the applicable state jurisdiction. Varies by state experience rating, industry classification, and annual state-assigned rate notices. Applied to state-specific taxable wage bases.

business_glossary_term: State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) Taxable Wage Base
description: Maximum annual gross wages per employee subject to SUTA tax in the applicable state jurisdiction (e.g., $7,000 in some states, $56,500 in others). Required for accurate SUTA cost modeling and burden rate calculation.

Target gross margin percentage for placements using this burden rate structure, representing the desired spread (bill rate minus total employer cost) as a percentage of bill rate. Used in profitability reporting and bill rate validation.

Aggregate employer burden rate expressed as a percentage of gross wages, representing the sum of all burden components (FICA employer share, FUTA, SUTA, WC, general liability, benefits load, and overhead). Used to compute total employer cost per worker hour and validate bill rate margins. Note: this is a stored configuration value, not a real-time computed aggregate.
Timestamp when this burden rate record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and SOC 2 audit trail requirements.

Sequential version number for this burden rate record, incremented each time the rate structure is updated. Supports rate versioning and historical rate lookups for payroll audits and retroactive adjustments.

business_glossary_term: Workers Compensation (WC) Base Rate per $100 of Payroll
description: Workers Compensation insurance premium base rate expressed as dollars per $100 of gross payroll for the applicable NCCI class code and state jurisdiction (e.g., 2.3500 = $2.35 per $100). This is the primary WC cost driver in the burden rate structure.
Human-readable description of the NCCI Workers Compensation class code (e.g., Carpentry — Construction, Clerical Office Employees). Used in reporting, audits, and WC policy documentation.

Experience modification factor (e-mod) applied to the WC base rate to adjust premiums based on the employers actual loss history relative to industry average. Values below 1.0 indicate better-than-average loss history; above 1.0 indicates worse. Sourced from WC carrier or NCCI.

Risk hazard category assigned to the WC NCCI class code indicating the relative injury risk level of the job duties. Used for internal risk segmentation, pricing decisions, and WC carrier negotiations.

business_glossary_term: Workers Compensation (WC) National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI) Class Code
description: Four-digit NCCI Workers Compensation classification code identifying the job duty category for WC premium calculation (e.g., 5403 for Carpentry, 8810 for Clerical Office). Drives WC base rate per $100 of payroll and is the primary driver of WC cost in staffing operations.
