False for per diem reimbursements, expense reimbursements under accountable plans, and certain non-cash fringe benefits. Critical for accurate payroll tax calculation and IRS Form 941 filing.

Indicates whether this earnings line represents overtime pay as defined under FLSA (Fair Labor Standards Act) — hours worked beyond 40 in a workweek compensated at a minimum of 1.5x the regular rate. Used for FLSA compliance reporting, overtime cost analytics, and client billing reconciliation where OT bill rates differ from straight-time rates.

Indicates whether this earning component is subject to federal and state income tax withholding. True for wages, OT, DT, holiday pay, bonuses, and commissions. False for qualifying per diem allowances within IRS GSA limits, accountable plan expense reimbursements, and certain fringe benefits. Drives FICA, FUTA, SUTA, and income tax withholding calculations.

Classification code identifying the labor category or job classification for this earnings line (e.g., LIGHT_IND for light industrial, IT_PROF for IT professional, CLERICAL, HEALTHCARE). Used for workforce cost analytics, bill rate reconciliation, and compliance with prevailing wage determinations under DOL regulations. Aligns with timesheet.labor_category.

Sequential line number ordering this earnings component within the parent pay stub. Satisfies LINE_SEQUENCE minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role. Used for display ordering on pay stubs and for deterministic processing order during gross pay calculation.

Current processing status of this earnings line within the payroll workflow. Pending = awaiting payroll approval; Approved = authorized for payment; Processed = included in a completed payroll run; Voided = cancelled/reversed; Adjusted = corrected via an adjustment entry. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role.

The pay rate multiplier applied to the base pay rate for premium pay earnings. Standard values: 1.0000 for regular pay, 1.5000 for OT (overtime), 2.0000 for DT (double time). May reflect CBA (collective bargaining agreement) or client-specific premium rates. Used to validate gross_amount calculation and support FLSA compliance audits.

The frequency at which the worker is paid, determining the pay period structure for this earnings line. Weekly is most common for temporary staffing workers. Biweekly and semimonthly are common for permanent placements. Drives payroll processing schedule in TempWorks Payroll and tax withholding table selection.

Last calendar date of the pay period to which this earnings line applies. Defines the closing boundary of the work period being compensated. Used in conjunction with pay_period_start_date for FLSA overtime workweek calculations and state wage statement compliance.

First calendar date of the pay period to which this earnings line applies. Combined with pay_period_end_date, defines the work period being compensated. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance, FLSA overtime calculation, and tax period allocation.

The hourly, daily, or unit rate applied to this earnings line to compute the gross amount. Represents the workers agreed compensation rate for this earning type (e.g., straight-time rate, OT rate at 1.5x, DT rate at 2x). Sourced from the placement rate agreement in Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Confidential as it contains worker compensation data.

The daily per diem rate applied for this earnings line when earning_type is per_diem. Compared against GSA (General Services Administration) published rates to determine taxability — amounts within GSA limits are non-taxable; excess amounts are taxable wages. Null for non-per-diem earning types.

Classification of per diem allowance type when earning_type is per_diem. Lodging = accommodation allowance; Meals and Incidentals (M&IE) = daily meal and incidental expense allowance; Combined = single daily rate covering both; None = not a per diem line. GSA per diem rates determine taxability thresholds. Null or none for non-per-diem earning types.

Indicates whether this earnings line is subject to prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act, Service Contract Act, or state prevailing wage laws. True for government contract assignments requiring certified payroll reporting. Triggers additional compliance reporting obligations to DOL and OFCCP.

Code identifying the work shift associated with this earnings line (e.g., DAY, EVE for evening, NIGHT, WKND for weekend). Used to apply shift differential pay rates and to reconcile earnings with Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling data. Relevant for shift_differential earning types.

The native record identifier from the originating payroll system (TempWorks Payroll) for this earnings line. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record. Used for audit trails, data lineage, and dispute resolution.

The calendar tax year (YYYY) to which this earnings line is attributed for W-2 or 1099 reporting purposes. Determined by the check_date. Critical for year-end tax form preparation, IRS reporting, and multi-year payroll analytics. Enables accurate W-2 box 1 (wages) and 1099-NEC box 1 (nonemployee compensation) aggregation.

Timestamp when this earnings line record was most recently modified. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED minimum category. Tracks payroll corrections, status changes, and retroactive adjustments. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Two-letter US state code where the work was performed for this earnings line. Drives state income tax withholding, SUTA (State Unemployment Tax Act) employer contributions, state-specific overtime rules, and workers compensation premium allocation. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance in staffing operations where workers may be deployed across multiple states.

Tax and employment classification of the worker for this earnings line. W-2 Temp = temporary employee on agency payroll; W-2 Perm = permanent placed employee; 1099 IC (Independent Contractor) = self-employed contractor; EOR (Employer of Record) = worker employed through EOR arrangement. Determines tax withholding obligations, FICA applicability, FUTA/SUTA liability, and W-2 vs 1099 year-end tax form issuance.

NCCI (National Council on Compensation Insurance) workers compensation class code applicable to this earnings line. Determines the workers comp insurance premium rate applied to the gross wages. Critical for accurate workers comp cost allocation, experience modification factor calculations, and compliance with state workers compensation insurance requirements.
Unique system-generated identifier for the pay rate record. Primary key for the pay_rate data product in the payroll domain.
Reference to the worker assignment this pay rate applies to. Supports multi-rate scenarios where a worker may have different rates across concurrent assignments.

Foreign key linking to payroll.burden_rate. Business justification: Pay rate structures should reference the master burden rate definition for accurate cost modeling and margin calculation. pay_rate currently has burden_rate_pct: DECIMAL(7,4) which is a denormalized v
Reference to the client account for which the worker is placed. Used to validate rate alignment with client rate cards and MSA terms.

Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Pay rates are governed by MSA-negotiated rate schedules; critical for billing compliance, margin calculation, and audit trail. Staffing operations require tracing worker pay rates to governing client

Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Pay rates must align with contracted rate cards to ensure margin integrity and prevent billing disputes. Staffing operations require direct linkage for rate validation, markup verification, and client

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Premium pay rates for licensed/certified workers (RN, CDL, PE) are validated against the specific active credential instance. 'Rate Validation by Active Credential Instance' is a real staffing payroll

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Rate cards are tied to credential packages; different pay rates for workers with advanced certifications or security clearances. Healthcare staffing commonly pays premium rates for specialty certifica

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Rate approval workflows validate that credential requirements are satisfied before activating pay rates; prevents paying workers who lack mandatory client-required credentials. Common in healthcare/IT

Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire placements have negotiated salary rates that drive payroll processing. Links placement deal terms to payroll rate structure for accurate salary administration and cost tracking.

Foreign key linking to recruitment.hiring_decision. Business justification: Pay rate records implement the bill/pay rates authorized by the hiring decision. Payroll auditors and rate dispute resolution processes require direct traceability from the active pay_rate record to t

Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC agreement specifies payment_rate and payment_structure that directly govern pay rate setup for IC contractors. Staffing HR pay rate configuration for IC workers must reference the IC agreement to v

Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Pay rates are benchmarked and structured by job category for market rate analysis, pay grade assignment, and pricing consistency across clients. Staffing firms maintain category-level pay rate tables

Foreign key linking to timesheet.labor_category. Business justification: Staffing firms structure pay rates by labor category/skill level for rate card management and client billing. This link supports rate card configuration, prevailing wage compliance, and ensures pay ra

Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Pay rates frequently vary by work location due to metro differentials, cost-of-living adjustments, and site-specific client agreements. Essential for accurate rate management and pricing by location i

Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP managed programs govern markup caps and rate schedules (markup_cap_pct, target_ttf_days). Pay rate approval in MSP engagements requires tracing rates to the managed program for billing compliance

Foreign key linking to recruitment.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Pay rates are established during onboarding process. Tracking which onboarding engagement set the rate supports audit trails, rate dispute resolution, and compliance verification that rates were prope
Reference to the job order (requisition) associated with this pay rate. Enables rate traceability back to the originating client requisition.

Foreign key linking to joborder.position_requirement. Business justification: Staffing firms set pay rates directly against position-level requirements (pay_rate_min/max defined per position). Multi-position orders require position-level rate traceability for pay compliance aud
Reference to the worker/candidate whose compensation this pay rate governs. Links pay rate to the individual worker record.

Foreign key linking to vendor.rate_agreement. Business justification: rate_agreement defines negotiated markup, pay rate bounds, and spread floors/ceilings with a supplier. pay_rate must reference the governing rate_agreement to enforce rate compliance, flag exceptions,

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW/IC engagements have distinct pay rates (milestone, hourly IC rates). Payroll must compute and audit pay for SOW workers against the specific engagement terms. A staffing payroll expert would expec

Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs define engagement-specific terms including pay rate structures and worker classifications. Staffing firms must link pay rates to SOWs for scope compliance, change order management, and SOW-level

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Pay rates must meet state-specific minimum wage, prevailing wage, and overtime rules. Staffing firms link rate records to the governing state compliance rule to validate rates against jurisdictional m

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Staffing agencies use subcontractor vendors to fulfill placements when internal candidate pools are insufficient. Pay rates must track which vendor supplied the worker for vendor invoice reconciliatio

Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: pay_rate governs what a worker is paid; vendor_rate_card defines the supplier-negotiated pay/bill rates. Linking them enables rate card compliance auditing — verifying applied pay rates match the nego

Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: VMS programs enforce markup and bill-rate caps (markup_cap_pct, bill_rate_cap_currency). Pay rate approval workflows must validate rates against VMS program caps. Staffing operations experts expect pa

External rate identifier assigned by the Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, when the rate is VMS-driven. Used for reconciliation between TempWorks Payroll and Beeline VMS rate management. Populated only when rate_source = vms_driven.

Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Pay rates require validated wage/hour determinations for FLSA exemption status, prevailing wage compliance, and minimum wage validation. Staffing firms must document compliant rate approval before ass

Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: H1B/LCA prevailing wage compliance requires pay rates to be validated against work authorization type — staffing firms sponsoring visas must document 'Pay Rate Compliance by Work Authorization' for DO

Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: Pay rates vary by work location due to prevailing wage laws, state minimum wage, and shift differentials. pay_rate has location_id (→client.location) but placement.work_location carries workers_comp_c

Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Pay rates are directly determined by worker classification (W-2 vs. 1099, FLSA exempt vs. non-exempt). Staffing firms must link rate records to the governing classification determination for FLSA comp

Current workflow state of the pay rate record. Pending = awaiting manager or compliance approval; approved = active and payroll-eligible; rejected = denied; expired = past effective end date. Controls whether the rate can be used in payroll processing.

Name or identifier of the manager, recruiter, or compliance officer who approved this pay rate. Provides audit trail for rate authorization in compliance with internal controls and OFCCP requirements.
Date and time when the pay rate was formally approved. Part of the rate lifecycle audit trail. Required for SOC 2 Type II compliance and internal payroll controls.

The rate charged to the client for each unit of the workers time (typically per hour). Stored alongside the pay rate to enable spread and gross margin calculation. The spread (bill_rate minus regular_pay_rate) is the primary revenue metric in staffing. Sourced from the client rate card or VMS.

Date after which the clients conversion fee obligation is waived if the worker is converted to a permanent employee. Typically tied to a minimum number of hours worked or weeks on assignment per the MSA terms. Null if no conversion fee waiver applies.

Date and time when the pay rate record was first created in TempWorks Payroll. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOC 2 Type II compliance and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary rate fields are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency payroll for international staffing operations.

Multiplier applied to the regular pay rate to calculate the double-time (DT) pay rate. Typically 2.0x, applicable in states such as California for hours over 12 in a day or on the 7th consecutive day. Applied by TempWorks Payroll for DT hours.

The date after which this pay rate is no longer active. Nullable for open-ended rates. When populated, payroll processing after this date must use a successor rate record. Supports rate versioning and assignment extensions.
The date from which this pay rate becomes active and applicable to payroll processing. Defines the beginning of the rates binding period. Payroll transactions on or after this date use this rate.

business_glossary_term: Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) Exempt Flag
description: Indicates whether the worker is exempt from FICA (Social Security and Medicare) withholding for this assignment. True for certain visa holders (e.g., F-1, J-1 students) or specific worker classifications. Drives TempWorks Payroll tax withholding logic.

business_glossary_term: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Exempt Flag
description: Indicates whether the worker is classified as exempt from FLSA overtime requirements (True = exempt salaried; False = non-exempt hourly). Determines whether OT and DT multipliers apply. Critical for wage and hour compliance and DOL audit defense.

Indicates whether this pay rate is a blended rate covering multiple job duties or skill levels within a single assignment. True = blended rate applies; False = single-role rate. Common in SOW engagements and multi-skill temporary placements.

The percentage markup applied over the regular pay rate to arrive at the bill rate. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.45 = 45% markup). Used for pricing analysis, margin reporting, and VMS rate negotiations. Markup = (bill_rate - regular_pay_rate) / regular_pay_rate.

Maximum number of hours per week or pay period at the regular pay rate before overtime (OT) rates apply. Typically 40 hours per week under FLSA, but may be lower per client contract or state law (e.g., California daily OT threshold). Drives OT calculation trigger in TempWorks Payroll.

Minimum number of hours per pay period the worker is guaranteed to be paid, regardless of actual hours worked. Common in certain staffing contracts and SOW engagements. Impacts gross pay floor calculation in TempWorks Payroll.

Multiplier applied to the regular pay rate to calculate the overtime (OT) pay rate. Standard FLSA minimum is 1.5x for hours over 40 per week. May be higher per client contract or state law. Applied by TempWorks Payroll for OT hours on timesheets.
Frequency at which the worker is paid. Determines payroll cycle alignment in TempWorks Payroll. Weekly is most common for temporary staffing; bi-weekly and semi-monthly for salaried placements.

Internal compensation band or grade classification assigned to this pay rate (e.g., G1, G2, Band A). Used for workforce analytics, compensation equity analysis, and EEOC/OFCCP reporting on pay equity across worker populations.

Daily non-taxable allowance paid to the worker for meals, lodging, and incidentals when working away from their tax home. Expressed in currency per day. Must comply with IRS per diem guidelines to maintain non-taxable status. Tracked separately from regular pay for tax reporting.

business_glossary_term: Paid Time Off (PTO) Accrual Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether the worker accrues Paid Time Off (PTO) under this pay rate configuration. Eligibility may depend on assignment duration, worker classification, or client program rules. Drives PTO accrual processing in TempWorks Payroll.

Rate at which PTO hours accrue per hour worked or per pay period, expressed in hours. For example, 0.0385 hours of PTO per hour worked equates to approximately 1 week of PTO per year. Populated only when pto_accrual_eligible = True.
Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this pay rate configuration within TempWorks Payroll and Beeline VMS. Used for rate card lookups and VMS-driven rate matching.

Free-text notes documenting the rationale, context, or special conditions behind the negotiated pay rate. May include references to candidate counter-offers, market data justifications, or client-specific rate exceptions. Supports recruiter and account manager context for future rate reviews.

Origin of the pay rate determination. Negotiated = individually agreed with worker; vms_driven = rate set by Vendor Management System (VMS) such as Beeline; standard = internal standard rate table; client_rate_card = derived from client-specific rate card agreement.

Classification of how the worker is compensated. Hourly = per hour worked; salary = fixed periodic amount; piece_rate = per unit produced; daily = per day worked. Drives gross pay calculation logic in TempWorks Payroll.

The base pay rate for the worker expressed in the applicable rate_type unit (e.g., dollars per hour for hourly workers, dollars per day for daily workers). This is the principal quantitative compensation fact. Used as the basis for gross pay calculation in TempWorks Payroll.

Additional pay rate amount added to the regular pay rate for workers assigned to non-standard shifts (e.g., evening, overnight, weekend). Expressed as a flat dollar amount per hour or as a percentage add-on depending on the shift_differential_type field.
Indicates whether the shift_differential_rate is a flat dollar amount added per hour or a percentage of the regular pay rate. Determines how TempWorks Payroll calculates the effective shift pay.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this pay rate record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and conflict resolution in the Databricks Silver Layer when rates are ingested from multiple upstream systems.

Two-letter US state code where the worker performs services under this pay rate. Determines applicable SUTA rate, state income tax withholding rules, state-specific overtime laws, and workers comp jurisdiction. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance.

business_glossary_term: State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) State Code
description: Two-letter state code designating the state to which SUTA (State Unemployment Tax Act) taxes are remitted for this worker. May differ from work state_code in multi-state scenarios or when the workers base state differs from the work location state.

Indicates whether the worker under this pay rate is eligible for conversion from temporary to permanent (temp-to-perm or contract-to-hire) placement. Affects conversion fee applicability and rate renegotiation triggers upon conversion.

Date and time when the pay rate record was last modified. Tracks rate amendments, approval status changes, and effective date adjustments. Required for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.

NCCI or state-specific workers compensation classification code associated with the job duties for this pay rate. Determines the workers comp insurance premium rate applied to the workers wages. Critical for accurate burden rate calculation and insurance compliance.

Unique system-generated identifier for each payroll processing cycle (pay run) executed through TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the primary key and authoritative anchor for all payroll transactions within a given cycle.

Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: pay_run has pay_period_start_date/end_date as plain columns but no FK to the authoritative pay_period record. Linking pay_run.pay_period_id → timesheet.pay_period is fundamental to payroll period reco
Date and time when the payroll run was formally approved by the authorized approver. Captured for audit trail, SLA compliance, and payroll processing timeline analytics.
Staffing branch or cost center code associated with this payroll run. Used for branch-level payroll cost allocation, P&L reporting, and operational analytics in Power BI.
Date printed on physical payroll checks or used as the effective date for direct deposit transactions. May differ from pay_date when banking processing windows shift the settlement date.

Identifier for the legal employing entity or EOR entity under which this payroll run is processed. Supports multi-entity payroll operations and co-employment models where Staffing Hr acts as the employer of record.
Date and time when this pay run record was first created in TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the audit record creation timestamp for data lineage and SOC 2 Type II compliance.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll run. Typically USD for domestic operations; supports multi-currency for international EOR engagements.

Primary method used to disburse net pay to workers in this run. Direct deposit (ACH) is standard; check and pay card are alternatives for workers without bank accounts; wire for special high-value payments.
Date on which this payroll runs journal entries were posted to the Oracle NetSuite ERP General Ledger. Marks the accounting period in which payroll costs are recognized.
Number of 1099 independent contractors (ICs) included in this payroll run. Used for IRS 1099-NEC filing thresholds and worker classification compliance monitoring.
Indicates whether this payroll run spans workers in more than one US state, triggering multi-state tax withholding, SUTA apportionment, and additional compliance review requirements.
Scheduled date on which worker net pay is disbursed via direct deposit, check, or pay card. Determines the tax deposit due date per IRS deposit schedule rules.
Frequency at which workers are paid within this run. Drives pay period date calculations and tax withholding schedules per IRS Publication 15 annualization tables.
Comma-delimited list of two-letter US state codes (e.g., CA,TX,NY) representing all states in which workers were paid in this run. Drives multi-state tax filing obligations and SUTA rate application.
Date and time when this payroll run was finalized and posted to the General Ledger in Oracle NetSuite ERP, making it immutable for the accounting period.
Date and time when TempWorks Payroll completed the payroll calculation and tax computation processing for this run. Used for payroll operations SLA monitoring and processing time analytics.
Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the pay run, used in reconciliation, audit trails, and payroll operations communications. Format: PR-YYYY-MM-NNNN.

Current lifecycle state of the payroll run within TempWorks Payroll workflow. Progresses from open (data entry) through processing, approved, and posted (finalized to GL). Voided indicates the run was reversed.

Classification of the payroll run indicating its operational purpose. Regular runs cover standard pay periods; off-cycle handles out-of-band payments; adjustment corrects prior-period errors; void-and-reissue replaces a voided run; supplemental covers bonuses or special payments.
Calendar quarter (1–4) to which this payroll run belongs, used for IRS Form 941 quarterly payroll tax filing and FUTA/SUTA quarterly reporting.
Calendar year to which this payroll runs earnings and withholdings are attributed for IRS W-2 and 1099 tax reporting purposes. Critical for year-end tax form generation.

Sum of all voluntary and involuntary employee deductions (benefits, garnishments, 401k, health insurance premiums, etc.) withheld from worker gross pay in this run. Reconciles gross-to-net pay calculations.
Sum of double time (DT) earnings for all workers in this run, applicable in states such as California where DT is mandated for hours exceeding 12 in a workday or 8 on the seventh consecutive day.

Sum of all employer-side payroll costs beyond gross wages in this run, including employer FICA, FUTA, SUTA, workers comp premiums, and employer-paid benefits. Used to calculate the burden rate and total cost of workforce deployment.
Sum of employee-side Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) withholdings (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) across all workers in this run. Reported on IRS Form 941.

Sum of employer-side Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) matching contributions (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) for all W-2 workers in this run. Included in total employer burden calculation.

business_glossary_term: Total Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) Tax
description: Sum of Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) employer tax accrued in this payroll run, calculated at 6.0% on the first $7,000 of each employees wages (net of SUTA credit). Filed annually on IRS Form 940.

Sum of all gross earnings for all workers in this payroll run before any deductions or tax withholdings. Includes regular pay, overtime (OT), double time (DT), per diem, and other earnings components.

Sum of net pay disbursed to all workers in this run after all tax withholdings and deductions. Represents the total cash outflow for worker compensation and is the primary reconciliation figure against bank ACH/check totals.

Sum of overtime (OT) premium earnings for all workers in this run, calculated at 1.5x the regular rate for hours exceeding 40 per workweek per FLSA. Supports OT cost monitoring and client billing reconciliation.
Sum of per diem allowances paid to workers in this run for travel, lodging, and meal reimbursements. Per diem amounts within IRS GSA rates are non-taxable; amounts above are taxable wages.
Sum of paid time off (PTO) earnings disbursed in this payroll run, including vacation, sick, and holiday pay. Supports PTO accrual reconciliation and state-mandated paid leave compliance.

Sum of straight-time (regular rate) earnings for all workers in this run, excluding overtime, double time, and premium pay components. Used for pay rate analytics and FLSA regular rate of pay calculations.

business_glossary_term: Total State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) Tax
description: Sum of State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) employer tax accrued across all applicable states in this payroll run. Rates and wage bases vary by state; critical for multi-state payroll compliance.

Sum of all employee-side tax withholdings across all workers in this run, including federal income tax, state income tax, local taxes, and employee FICA (Social Security and Medicare). Used for IRS Form 941 and state tax deposit reconciliation.

Sum of workers compensation insurance premiums accrued for all workers in this payroll run, calculated based on payroll amounts and applicable workers comp class codes and rates. Supports insurance carrier reporting and burden rate analysis.
Date and time when this pay run record was last modified in TempWorks Payroll or the Silver Layer. Supports change data capture, data lineage, and audit trail requirements.
Business justification or reason code for voiding this payroll run. Required when run_status = voided to support audit compliance and payroll error tracking.
Date and time when this payroll run was voided, if applicable. Populated only for runs with run_status = voided. Supports payroll reversal audit trail and GL correction tracking.
Number of W-2 (Wage and Tax Statement) employees included in this payroll run. Distinguishes co-employed temporary workers from independent contractors for tax and compliance reporting.
Total number of workers (W-2 employees and 1099 independent contractors) included in this payroll run. Used for headcount reporting, payroll audit reconciliation, and workforce analytics.

Unique system-generated identifier for each individual pay stub record. Primary key for the pay_stub data product. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — serves as the anchor for all gross-to-net pay disbursement details for a single worker in a single pay run.

Reference to the placement assignment for which this pay stub was generated. Ties earnings to the specific client engagement, job order, and work location for labor cost allocation and billing reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Pay stubs must reference governing MSA for audit trail, co-employment determination, and compliance verification. Required for client audits, dispute resolution, and demonstrating adherence to contrac

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Payroll processing validates credential status before issuing payment; compliance holds block pay_stub generation when mandatory credentials expire. Staffing firms routinely hold paychecks until BGC/l

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Pay stubs must be validated against the credential package governing the assignment — staffing payroll audits run 'Pay Stubs by Credential Package' to verify workers paid at premium rates hold the req

Foreign key linking to timesheet.direct_deposit_setup. Business justification: pay_stub records ACH disbursement but has no FK to the direct_deposit_setup that governed it. Linking pay_stub.direct_deposit_setup_id → timesheet.direct_deposit_setup enables ACH return resolution an

Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire employees receive pay stubs when staffing firm handles payroll during transition or under PEO arrangements. Links pay records to permanent placement for payroll continuity and compliance.

Foreign key linking to compliance.everify_case. Business justification: E-Verify case status must be resolved before continuing payroll for new hires at federal contractor clients. TNC (Tentative Non-Confirmation) cases require payroll suspension until resolution - federa

Foreign key linking to compliance.i9_verification. Business justification: Every pay stub requires valid I-9 work authorization verification before payment issuance. Staffing firms cannot pay workers without documented work eligibility - core compliance gate for payroll proc

Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC contractor payment processing requires referencing the IC agreement to validate payment_structure, payment_terms_net_days, and form_1099_reporting_flag when generating pay stubs. Staffing payroll t

Foreign key linking to recruitment.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Payroll validation requires verifying worker completed onboarding before issuing first paycheck. Links pay stub to the onboarding engagement that authorized payment. Critical for compliance audits and

Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Pay stubs must link to job orders for client billing reconciliation, revenue recognition by order, and job-level margin analysis. Essential for staffing P&L reporting and invoice validation against co

Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: pay_stub has pay_period_start_date/end_date as plain columns with no FK to the authoritative pay_period record. Linking pay_stub.pay_period_id → timesheet.pay_period normalizes period reference, enabl

Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_rate. Business justification: A pay stub is generated based on a specific pay rate master record. Adding pay_rate_id to pay_stub creates a traceable link from the transactional pay statement back to the authoritative rate definiti
Reference to the payroll run batch that produced this pay stub. Enables grouping of all stubs processed in the same payroll cycle and supports reconciliation against the payroll run header.

Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Staffing payroll gates pay stub issuance on compliance clearance (I-9, BGC, drug screen). Payroll auditors reference the placement_compliance_check record to confirm a worker was legally authorized to

Reference to the worker (candidate/employee) for whom this pay stub was generated. Links to the candidate or worker master record in the staffing system. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: W-2 workers on SOW engagements receive pay stubs. Links payroll record to SOW engagement for project cost tracking, client billing validation, and worker payment documentation.

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: When workers are vendor-supplied (subcontractor model), pay stubs must track the originating vendor for cost allocation, vendor invoice validation, and dispute resolution. Accounts payable reconciliat

Foreign key linking to timesheet.timesheet. Business justification: Pay stubs are generated directly from approved timesheets in staffing operations. This link enables traceability from time worked to payment issued, critical for payroll audit, dispute resolution, and

Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Pay stub generation requires validated wage/hour determinations to ensure compliant overtime calculations, salary basis tests, and prevailing wage compliance. Staffing payroll systems validate each pa

Foreign key linking to payroll.wc_policy. Business justification: A pay stub captures workers_comp_premium (the actual WC premium charged on this pay cycle) and workers_comp_code (the NCCI class code). Adding wc_policy_id links the pay stub to the governing WC polic

Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: Work authorization validity at each pay date is a direct payroll compliance requirement — staffing firms must confirm active work authorization before disbursing pay. 'Payroll Compliance: Work Authori

Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: Per diem eligibility and multi-state tax withholding on pay stubs depend on the specific work location. pay_stub carries work_state_code as a denormalized field; a direct FK to work_location enables p

Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Pay stub processing depends on correct worker classification (W-2 vs 1099) to determine tax withholding obligations, FICA applicability, and reporting requirements. Classification drives payroll tax t

Type of bank account designated for direct deposit disbursement. checking or savings as required by NACHA ACH transaction codes (PPD). Determines the ACH SEC code used in the payment file. Null for check and pay card disbursements.

Unique 15-digit NACHA ACH trace number assigned to the direct deposit transaction for this pay stub. Null for check and pay card disbursements. Used for ACH payment tracing, return item resolution, and bank reconciliation.

Sequential check number printed on the physical paycheck when pay_delivery_method is check. Null for direct deposit and pay card disbursements. Used for check reconciliation, stop-payment requests, and positive pay bank file matching.

Timestamp when this pay stub record was first created in the payroll system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for audit trails and payroll processing SLA measurement.

Dollar amount or percentage value of net pay allocated to this account in a split-deposit arrangement. Populated when deposit_allocation_type is fixed_amount or percentage. Null when deposit_allocation_type is full_net.

Specifies how net pay is allocated to this bank account in a split-deposit configuration. full_net = entire net pay to one account; fixed_amount = a specific dollar amount; percentage = a defined percentage of net pay. Supports multi-account direct deposit configurations.

Numeric sequence (1 = highest priority) determining the order in which net pay is allocated across multiple bank accounts in a split-deposit configuration. Ensures correct disbursement sequencing when a worker has designated multiple accounts.

Dollar amount of earnings for double-time hours (dt_hours × pay_rate × 2.0). Applicable in states with double-time mandates. Stored as a discrete earnings component for gross pay audit and state compliance reporting.

Total double-time hours worked during the pay period, subject to the 2x pay multiplier. Applicable in states such as California with daily double-time thresholds (e.g., hours beyond 12 in a workday). Required for accurate gross pay calculation in multi-state payroll.

Dollar amount of federal income tax withheld from the workers wages for this pay period based on W-4 elections and IRS withholding tables. Required for W-2 reporting (Box 2) and federal tax deposit obligations.

business_glossary_term: Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) Taxable Wages
description: Portion of the workers gross wages subject to Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) employer tax for this pay period, up to the $7,000 annual wage base. Required for IRS Form 940 filing and employer burden rate reporting.

Total pre-deduction, pre-tax earnings for the pay period. Sum of regular_earnings, ot_earnings, dt_earnings, per_diem_amount, and any other pay components. Represents the gross-base amount in the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction. Used as the basis for all tax withholding calculations.

Employee share of Medicare (HI) tax withheld under FICA at the applicable rate (1.45%, plus 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax for high earners). Required for W-2 reporting (Box 6) and FICA deposit compliance.

Final take-home amount disbursed to the worker after all deductions and tax withholdings (gross_pay − total_deductions − total_tax_withheld). Represents the net total in the MONETARY_TRIPLET. The amount actually paid via direct deposit, check, or pay card.

Dollar amount of earnings for overtime hours (ot_hours × pay_rate × 1.5). Stored separately from regular earnings to support FLSA compliance reporting, overtime cost analytics, and client billing reconciliation.

Total overtime hours worked during the pay period, subject to the 1.5x pay multiplier under FLSA. Includes hours exceeding the standard weekly threshold or daily OT thresholds per applicable state law. Critical for gross pay calculation and FLSA compliance reporting.

The actual date on which payment was or is scheduled to be disbursed to the worker. Represents the principal business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). Used for cash flow reporting, tax period assignment, and worker payment scheduling.

Method by which net pay is disbursed to the worker. direct_deposit = ACH electronic transfer to bank account; check = physical paper check; pay_card = prepaid debit card for unbanked workers. Determines downstream disbursement routing logic.

Frequency at which the worker is paid, determining the pay period cadence for this stub. Drives payroll scheduling, tax withholding annualization, and benefit deduction timing. Most staffing workers are paid weekly or biweekly.

The workers base hourly or unit pay rate applied during this pay period, expressed in USD. Represents the agreed compensation rate from the placement rate card. Used to calculate regular earnings and validate gross pay. Classified confidential as worker compensation data.

Classification of the pay rate basis for this stub. hourly = standard time-based; salary = fixed periodic amount; per_diem = daily allowance-based; piece_rate = unit-of-output based. Determines gross pay calculation method and FLSA overtime applicability.

Non-taxable per diem allowance paid to the worker for travel, meals, or lodging during the pay period. Sourced from approved per diem claims. Tracked separately from taxable wages for IRS accountable plan compliance and gross pay composition.

Status of the NACHA prenote (zero-dollar test transaction) process for this bank account. pending = prenote initiated; sent = prenote transmitted to bank; verified = bank confirmed account valid; failed = account validation failed. Governs whether live ACH disbursement is permitted.

Workers remaining PTO accrual balance as of this pay stubs pay period end date, expressed in hours. Snapshot value captured at time of pay run for worker statement display and leave liability reporting.

Number of PTO hours applied to this pay stub for the pay period. Represents paid leave hours that contribute to gross pay but are not worked hours. Required for PTO accrual balance management and FLSA leave tracking.

Dollar amount of earnings for regular straight-time hours (regular_hours × pay_rate). Core component of gross pay. Stored as a raw earnings component to support gross-to-net audit trail and payroll reconciliation.

Total straight-time hours worked by the worker during the pay period, up to the overtime threshold (typically 40 hours per week under FLSA). Sourced from approved timesheet records in Kronos Workforce Ready. Used to calculate regular earnings.
Employee share of Social Security (OASDI) tax withheld under FICA at the applicable rate (6.2% up to the annual wage base). Required for W-2 reporting (Box 4) and FICA tax deposit compliance.

Dollar amount of state income tax withheld for the primary work state during this pay period. Supports multi-state payroll compliance and state W-2 / equivalent reporting. May be zero for states with no income tax.

Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this pay stub as printed on the workers pay statement (e.g., PS-2024-00045231). Used for worker inquiries, audit trails, and payroll reconciliation. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Current lifecycle state of the pay stub within the payroll processing workflow. draft = in calculation; calculated = gross-to-net complete pending approval; approved = authorized for disbursement; issued = payment released to worker; voided = cancelled before disbursement; reversed = post-issuance correction applied. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.

business_glossary_term: State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) Taxable Wages
description: Portion of the workers gross wages subject to State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) employer tax for this pay period, up to the applicable state wage base. Required for state unemployment tax filing and workforce cost burden rate calculation.

Workers federal income tax filing status as declared on IRS Form W-4, used to determine the correct withholding amount for this pay stub. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|married_filing_jointly|married_filing_separately|head_of_household|exempt|qualifying_surviving_spouse — promote to reference product]

Aggregate dollar amount of all pre-tax and post-tax deductions applied to this pay stub, including benefits premiums, garnishments, voluntary deductions, and advances. Represents the adjustment component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Supports gross-to-net reconciliation.

Aggregate dollar amount of all federal, state, and local taxes withheld from the workers gross pay for this pay period. Includes FICA (Social Security and Medicare), federal income tax, and applicable state/local income taxes. Key component of gross-to-net calculation.

Timestamp of the most recent modification to this pay stub record. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks corrections, reversals, and status transitions for audit compliance.

Indicates whether this pay stub is associated with a W-2 employee (True) versus a 1099 independent contractor (False). Drives tax withholding applicability, FICA obligations, and year-end tax form generation routing in TempWorks Payroll.

NCCI or state-specific workers compensation classification code assigned to the worker for this pay period based on job duties and work location. Drives workers comp premium calculation and is required for policy compliance and audit.

Employer-paid workers compensation insurance premium allocated to this pay stub for the pay period, calculated as (workers_comp_rate × payroll_basis). Represents a key component of the employer burden rate and total labor cost.

Cumulative gross pay earned by the worker from the start of the calendar year through this pay stubs pay period end date. Displayed on the workers pay statement and used for W-2 wage box population, FICA wage base tracking, and annual compensation reporting.

Cumulative net pay disbursed to the worker from the start of the calendar year through this pay stubs pay period end date. Displayed on the workers pay statement for annual earnings visibility and financial planning.

Cumulative total of all taxes withheld from the workers pay from the start of the calendar year through this pay stubs pay period end date. Displayed on the pay statement and used for W-2 Box 2/4/6 population and tax deposit reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: In co-employment and EOR arrangements, workers comp policies may be client-specific or shared across clients. Essential for risk management, premium allocation, and determining coverage responsibilit

Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Workers comp policies are MSA-level insurance obligations; MSAs specify minimum coverage requirements. Staffing firms must link WC policies to MSAs for compliance verification, certificate of insuran

Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Workers' comp policies are assigned by NCCI class code which maps directly to job category. Staffing firms assign WC policies per job category for premium calculation and audit compliance. wc_class_co

Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Workers comp policies are jurisdiction-specific and tied to specific work locations. Required for accurate premium calculation, state compliance, and tracking which client locations are covered under

Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: In EOR and co-employment MSP programs (co_employment_flag, eor_flag on wc_policy), workers' comp policies are program-level constructs. Program-level WC insurance compliance reporting and premium allo

Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_run. Business justification: Workers compensation premium calculations are tied to specific payroll runs. wc_policy currently has pay_run_reference: STRING which is a denormalized reference. Adding structured FK pay_run_id → pay_

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Workers' comp policies are governed by state-specific rules covering mandatory coverage thresholds, rate schedules, and exemptions. Staffing firms link WC policies to the governing state compliance ru

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Workers comp policies for vendor-supplied workers are often held by the vendor, not the staffing agency. Certificate of insurance (COI) tracking, premium audit, and liability determination require li

Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor agreement specifies workers_comp_minimum_usd as a contractual requirement for supplier workers. WC policy compliance validation for supplier-provided workers requires referencing the vendor agr

Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: Workers compensation policies are issued per work location and jurisdiction. Each site has specific WC class codes and rates. Links policy coverage to physical work location.

The dollar amount of premium adjustment resulting from the most recent carrier audit, representing the difference between estimated premiums paid and actual earned premium based on audited payroll. Positive values indicate additional premium owed; negative values indicate a return premium credit.

The frequency at which the insurance carrier conducts a premium audit to reconcile estimated premiums against actual payroll. Staffing firms with high payroll volume often negotiate more frequent audits to reduce year-end true-up exposure.

business_glossary_term: Workers Compensation (WC) Base Rate per $100 of Payroll
description: The manual (base) WC premium rate expressed as a dollar amount per $100 of covered payroll for the associated class code and state jurisdiction, before application of the experience modification factor (EMR). Sourced from the applicable state rating bureau or NCCI.

The WC premium cost expressed as a decimal rate applied to gross payroll, representing the WC component of the total employer burden rate. Used in bill rate calculations, spread analysis, and gross margin reporting. Derived from the modified rate and historical payroll mix for this class code.

business_glossary_term: Insurance Carrier National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) Code
description: The five-digit NAIC company code assigned to the insurance carrier by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners. Used for regulatory identification and carrier verification in multi-state WC compliance.
The legal name of the insurance carrier or underwriter providing the workers compensation coverage under this policy. Used for carrier reporting, premium remittance, and audit correspondence.

The internal cost allocation code used to attribute WC premium costs to specific client accounts or job orders. Enables client-level WC cost reporting, profitability analysis, and accurate bill rate construction inclusive of WC burden.

Indicates whether this WC policy record involves a co-employment arrangement where both the staffing firm and the client share employer responsibilities. Affects WC coverage determination and premium allocation between parties.

The calculated WC premium dollar amount for the premium period, computed as (payroll_subject_to_wc / 100) multiplied by the modified_rate_per_100. Represents the accrued WC cost for the period and is used in burden rate application and client cost allocation.
The timestamp when this workers compensation policy record was first created in the payroll system. Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.

business_glossary_term: Workers Compensation (WC) Deductible Amount Per Claim
description: The per-claim deductible amount the employer is responsible for funding before the carrier pays WC benefits, applicable to large deductible programs. Zero for guaranteed cost policies. Impacts cash flow planning and loss reserve requirements.

The initial deposit premium paid to the carrier at policy inception, typically a percentage of the estimated annual premium. Represents the upfront cash outlay and is reconciled against actual earned premium at audit.
The date on which the workers compensation insurance policy becomes active and coverage begins. Used to determine coverage applicability for worker assignments and premium period calculations.

The aggregate policy limit of employers liability coverage for bodily injury by disease. Distinct from the per-accident limit and the per-employee disease limit. Used for risk management reporting and MSA compliance verification.

The per-occurrence limit of employers liability coverage (Part B of the WC policy) for bodily injury by accident. Standard limits are $100,000, $500,000, or $1,000,000. Used for risk management and client contract compliance.

Indicates whether this WC policy record applies to workers placed under an Employer of Record (EOR) arrangement, where the staffing firm acts as the legal employer for WC purposes. Drives co-employment risk management and premium allocation logic.

The estimated total annual WC premium for this policy as projected at policy inception, based on estimated payroll and applicable rates. Used for budgeting, cash flow planning, and comparison against actual accrued premiums during the audit cycle.

The experience modification rate (EMR) assigned by NCCI or the applicable state rating bureau, reflecting the employers actual loss history relative to expected losses for the class. A factor below 1.0 indicates better-than-average loss experience; above 1.0 indicates worse. Applied to the base rate to compute the modified premium rate.
The date on which the workers compensation insurance policy expires and coverage ends. Triggers renewal workflow and is used to validate coverage during assignment management and payroll processing.

business_glossary_term: Last Workers Compensation (WC) Premium Audit Date
description: The date of the most recently completed premium audit conducted by the insurance carrier. Used to track audit cycle compliance, identify overdue audits, and reconcile estimated versus actual premium balances.

For loss-sensitive policies, identifies the specific program structure. Retrospective rated programs adjust final premium based on actual losses; large deductible programs require the employer to fund losses up to a deductible threshold. Null or none for guaranteed cost policies.

The minimum premium amount guaranteed to the carrier regardless of actual payroll volume, as stipulated in the policy terms. Relevant for low-volume periods and ensures the carrier recovers minimum administrative costs.

business_glossary_term: Workers Compensation (WC) Modified Rate per $100 of Payroll
description: The effective WC premium rate per $100 of payroll after applying the experience modification factor (EMR) to the base rate. This is the rate used in actual premium calculations for payroll runs. Computed as base_rate_per_100 multiplied by experience_mod_factor.

business_glossary_term: Next Scheduled Workers Compensation (WC) Premium Audit Date
description: The scheduled date for the next premium audit by the insurance carrier. Used for audit preparation planning, payroll record retention compliance, and proactive reconciliation of estimated versus actual premiums.

business_glossary_term: Payroll Amount Subject to Workers Compensation (WC)
description: The gross payroll dollar amount for the premium period that is subject to workers compensation premium calculation for this class code and state jurisdiction. Excludes non-covered remuneration per NCCI rules (e.g., tips, certain overtime premiums). Used as the basis for premium computation.

Free-text field for operational notes, underwriting conditions, endorsements, or special program terms associated with this WC policy record. Used by payroll and risk management teams to document carrier-specific requirements or exceptions.

The externally-assigned policy number issued by the insurance carrier that uniquely identifies this workers compensation insurance policy. Used for carrier reporting, premium audit reconciliation, and regulatory filings.

Current lifecycle state of the workers compensation policy. Active policies are in force and covering workers; expired policies have passed their expiration date; cancelled policies were terminated before expiration; pending policies are bound but not yet effective.

Classification of the WC insurance policy structure. Guaranteed cost policies have fixed premiums; loss-sensitive policies (e.g., retrospective rated, large deductible) have premiums that vary based on actual loss experience. Drives premium calculation methodology and financial risk exposure.

The four-digit calendar or fiscal year of the workers compensation policy period. Used to group premium transactions for annual audit reconciliation, loss run reporting, and year-over-year cost trend analysis.

The basis on which WC premiums are calculated. In staffing, the standard basis is payroll (gross remuneration subject to WC), but some specialty programs may use flat or per-capita bases. Determines how payroll_subject_to_wc is computed.

business_glossary_term: Workers Compensation (WC) Premium Period End Date
description: The end date of the payroll period for which this WC premium transaction was calculated. Together with premium_period_start_date, defines the coverage window for the premium accrual.

business_glossary_term: Workers Compensation (WC) Premium Period Start Date
description: The start date of the payroll period for which this WC premium transaction was calculated. Defines the coverage window for the premium accrual and aligns with the pay run cycle.

The schedule rating credit (negative value) or debit (positive value) percentage applied to the manual premium, reflecting underwriter judgment on risk characteristics not captured by the experience modification factor. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., -0.10 for a 10% credit).

The two-letter US state code representing the state jurisdiction under which this workers compensation policy is filed and governed. Critical for multi-state WC compliance, SUTA alignment, and state-specific rate application for temporary and contract workers.

The timestamp when this workers compensation policy record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

business_glossary_term: Workers Compensation (WC) Classification Code Source Bureau
description: Identifies the rating bureau or authority that issued the WC class code and rates for this policy record. Most states use NCCI; California uses WCIRB; a few states have independent bureaus. Critical for multi-state compliance and rate validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCCI|WCIRB|NYBCOMP|DELAWARE|INDIANA|MICHIGAN|MINNESOTA|OTHER — promote to reference product]

The cumulative workers compensation premium accrued from the start of the policy year through the current premium period for this class code and state jurisdiction. Used for premium audit reconciliation, carrier reporting, and financial forecasting of WC costs.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Tax filings are subject to compliance audits by IRS, state tax authorities, and DOL. Audit scope includes payroll tax returns, deposit schedules, and withholding accuracy - audit trail links filings t

Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: In EOR staffing arrangements (eor_arrangement_flag on tax_filing), tax filings are made on behalf of client accounts. Regulatory compliance and EOR cost allocation reporting require tracing tax filing

Reference to the original payroll_tax_filing_id that this amended filing corrects. Null for original (non-amended) filings. Enables amendment chain tracking for IRS audit readiness.

Reference to the payroll run or payroll batch that generated the tax liabilities captured in this filing. Links the tax filing back to the source payroll processing event in TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE (to a MASTER_AGREEMENT/processing entity) for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Tax filings must comply with state-specific rules governing filing deadlines, deposit schedules, and rate requirements. Staffing firms link tax filings to the governing state compliance rule for state

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: In EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements, the supplier is the legal employer who files payroll taxes under their EIN. tax_filing must reference the EOR supplier for regulatory attribution, multi-EIN t

Indicates whether this record represents an amended or corrected tax filing (True) versus an original filing (False). Amended filings (e.g., Form 941-X) correct errors in previously submitted returns and are tracked separately for audit purposes.

Remaining tax balance owed to the tax authority after applying all deposits and credits (tax_liability_amount minus deposit_amount minus prior_deposit_amount). A positive value indicates underpayment; zero or negative indicates overpayment or credit.

Confirmation or acknowledgment number returned by the tax authority (e.g., EFTPS trace number, state portal confirmation) upon successful receipt of the tax deposit or filed return. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.
Timestamp when this tax filing record was first created in TempWorks Payroll and ingested into the Silver Layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this filing record (e.g., USD). Supports international operations and EOR arrangements. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Actual dollar amount deposited to the tax authority for this filing period. May differ from tax_liability_amount due to prior deposits, adjustments, or credits. Net amount remitted. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Actual date on which the tax deposit was transmitted to the tax authority via EFTPS or state equivalent. Used to determine timeliness and calculate any late deposit penalties. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Method used to transmit the tax deposit to the tax authority. EFTPS = IRS Electronic Federal Tax Payment System (mandatory for most employers); ACH = Automated Clearing House bank transfer for state deposits; check = paper check (rare); state_portal = state-specific online payment portal.

Statutory deadline by which this tax deposit or return must be submitted to the tax authority to avoid penalties. Determined by IRS deposit schedule rules (semi-weekly or monthly) or statutory filing deadlines for annual/quarterly returns.

IRS-assigned Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the legal entity filing this return. Required on all federal and state payroll tax filings. In EOR (Employer of Record) and co-employment models, this may reflect the EOR entitys EIN rather than the clients EIN.

Indicates whether this tax filing is processed under an Employer of Record (EOR) or co-employment arrangement (True), where the staffing firm acts as the legal employer for tax purposes on behalf of a client. Affects EIN used and liability allocation.

Total federal income tax withheld from worker wages and remitted to the IRS for this filing period. Reported on IRS Form 941 Line 3. Applies to W-2 employees; not applicable for 1099 independent contractors.

business_glossary_term: Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) Employee Tax Amount
description: Employee share of FICA taxes (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) withheld from worker paychecks and remitted as part of this filing. Applicable for W-2 employees; not applicable for 1099 independent contractors.

business_glossary_term: Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) Employer Tax Amount
description: Employer matching share of FICA taxes (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) owed by the staffing firm or EOR entity as the employer of record. Equal to the employee FICA amount under standard rules.

Exact date and time when the tax return was electronically submitted to the tax authority. Provides precise audit trail for compliance and IRS/DOL audit readiness. Null if return has not yet been filed.

Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this tax filing or deposit transaction, used for reconciliation with the tax authority and TempWorks Payroll records. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Frequency classification of the tax filing period. Determines deposit schedule (e.g., semi-weekly vs. monthly depositor rules under IRS Publication 15) and aligns with the form type (e.g., quarterly for 941, annual for 940).

Current workflow state of the payroll tax filing or deposit obligation. pending = liability identified but not yet deposited; deposited = funds transferred to tax authority; filed = return submitted; amended = corrected filing submitted; voided = filing cancelled; rejected = filing rejected by authority. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.

The IRS or state tax form type associated with this filing obligation. Common values include: 941 (Employers Quarterly Federal Tax Return), 940 (Federal Unemployment Tax Act annual return), 944 (annual federal tax return for small employers), W-2 (Wage and Tax Statement), 1099-NEC (Non-Employee Compensation), state SUTA return, state income tax withholding return. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 941|940|944|W-2|1099-NEC|SUTA|state_income_tax|local_income_tax — promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) Tax Amount
description: Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) tax liability for this filing period. Calculated at 6.0% on the first $7,000 of each employees wages, net of SUTA credit (typically reducing effective rate to 0.6%). Reported on IRS Form 940.
Dollar amount of interest charged by the tax authority on late or underpaid tax deposits. Accrues from the deposit due date to the actual payment date. Zero if no interest assessed.

Full legal name of the employer entity as registered with the IRS and state tax authorities for this filing. Supports multi-entity payroll structures including EOR, PEO, and co-employment arrangements.

Free-text notes or comments entered by payroll staff regarding this tax filing, such as reasons for amendments, penalty dispute details, or special handling instructions. Supports operational context for compliance reviews.
Amount overpaid to the tax authority for this filing period. May be applied as a credit to the next period or requested as a refund. Null or zero if no overpayment exists.
Last date of the payroll period covered by this tax filing. Defines the end of the wage and tax liability window for this return.
First date of the payroll period covered by this tax filing. Combined with payroll_period_end_date, defines the wage and tax liability window for this return.
Dollar amount of IRS or state-assessed penalties for late deposit, late filing, or underpayment of payroll taxes. Zero if no penalties assessed. Critical for compliance monitoring and SLA tracking.

Total tax deposits made in prior periods within the same filing period that are applied against the current tax liability. Used for reconciliation between cumulative deposits and the final return balance due.

Two-letter U.S. state or territory code (ISO 3166-2:US) for the jurisdiction of this filing. Null for federal-level filings. Critical for multi-state payroll tax compliance across all worker assignment states.

Total state income tax withheld from worker wages and remitted to the state tax authority for this filing period. Applicable for states with income tax; null for states without state income tax (e.g., TX, FL, WA).

business_glossary_term: State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) Tax Amount
description: State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) tax liability for this filing period. Rate and wage base vary by state and employer experience rating. Critical for multi-state staffing operations where workers are assigned across multiple states.

Jurisdictional level of the tax authority receiving this filing: federal (IRS/DOL), state (state revenue or unemployment agency), or local (city/county tax authority). Supports multi-state payroll compliance reporting and audit readiness.

Name of the federal, state, or local tax authority to which this filing or deposit is submitted (e.g., IRS, California EDD, New York State DTF, City of Philadelphia Revenue Department). Used for multi-jurisdiction payroll compliance tracking.

Total tax liability computed for this filing period based on taxable wages and applicable tax rates. Represents the gross amount owed to the tax authority before any prior deposits or credits are applied. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.
Calendar quarter (1–4) to which this filing applies. Applicable for quarterly filings such as IRS Form 941 and quarterly SUTA returns. Null for non-quarterly filing types.
Four-digit calendar year to which this tax filing applies (e.g., 2024). Used for annual reconciliation, W-2/1099 processing, and IRS/DOL audit readiness.

Aggregate gross taxable wages subject to the specific tax type covered by this filing for the filing period. Basis for computing the total tax liability. Excludes non-taxable fringe benefits and pre-tax deductions where applicable.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this tax filing record, including status changes, amendments, or deposit updates. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Classification of workers covered by this filing: W-2 for employees subject to withholding and employer taxes, 1099 for independent contractors (IC) subject only to self-employment tax reporting, or both for filings covering mixed worker populations. Critical for IRS worker classification compliance.

Number of workers (W-2 employees or 1099 contractors) included in this tax filing period. Reported on IRS Form 941 Line 1 as the number of employees who received wages. Supports headcount analytics and workforce planning.
Unique surrogate primary key for each annual tax form record (W-2, W-2c, 1099-NEC, 1099-MISC) generated in TempWorks Payroll. Globally unique across all form types and tax years.
