Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: W-2 and 1099 tax forms for visa-sponsored workers require work authorization details — nonresident alien treatment, treaty exemptions, and LCA compliance all depend on visa category. 'Tax Form Complia

Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: W-2 vs. 1099-NEC tax form type is directly determined by worker classification. Staffing firms must link tax forms to the governing classification determination for IRS misclassification audit defense
Date and time when this tax form record was first created in TempWorks Payroll during the year-end tax form generation process. Supports audit trail and SOC 2 compliance.
Date the workers copy of the tax form was distributed (mailed, electronically delivered, or handed in person). Must be on or before January 31 of the year following the tax year per IRS requirements.

Method used to distribute the workers copy of the tax form. Mail: physical paper mailed to worker address. Electronic: delivered via secure online portal (requires worker consent). In-person: handed directly to worker.
Date and time when the IRS FIRE system or SSA ACCUWAGE system accepted the e-file submission for this tax form. Null if not yet filed or if paper-filed. Critical for IRS filing deadline compliance.

IRS or SSA-assigned submission identifier returned upon successful e-file transmission. For W-2s: SSA ACCUWAGE submission ID. For 1099s: IRS FIRE system submission ID. Used to track filing status and retrieve acknowledgements.

Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the staffing company or EOR entity issuing the tax form. Appears in Box c of W-2 and Payer TIN field of 1099 forms. Required for IRS e-filing and state tax agency submissions.

Current lifecycle state of the tax form. Draft: form generated but not yet submitted to IRS/SSA. Filed: successfully submitted. Corrected: a W-2c or amended 1099 has been issued. Voided: form cancelled. Accepted: IRS/SSA e-file acknowledgement received.

Discriminator identifying the IRS tax form type. W-2 for W-2 employees and temporary workers; W-2c for corrected W-2s; 1099-NEC for independent contractor (IC) nonemployee compensation; 1099-MISC for miscellaneous income. Drives form-specific field population and filing rules.

True if this record represents a corrected tax form (W-2c or amended 1099). The corrected checkbox is checked on the physical form. Links to the original form via original_form_id for correction history tracking.
Date and time when this tax form record was most recently modified (e.g., correction applied, filing status updated, distribution recorded). Supports audit trail and change tracking.

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 19 Local Income Tax Withheld
description: W-2 Box 19: Total local income tax withheld from the workers wages. Reported to the applicable local tax authority. Null if no local tax applies.

W-2 Box 18: Total wages subject to local income tax withholding (e.g., city or county taxes). Applicable in jurisdictions such as New York City, Philadelphia, or Ohio municipalities. Null if no local tax applies.
1099-MISC Box 3: Other income payments not reportable in other boxes, such as prizes, awards, or taxable per diem amounts exceeding IRS limits. Null for W-2 and 1099-NEC form types.

1099-MISC Box 1: Rent payments of $600 or more paid to a landlord or property owner. Applicable when staffing company pays office or facility rent to a non-corporate payee. Null for W-2 and 1099-NEC form types.

1099-NEC Box 1: Total nonemployee compensation paid to an independent contractor (IC) during the tax year. Includes fees, commissions, prizes, and awards for services rendered. Null for W-2 form types. Threshold: $600 or more triggers filing requirement.

business_glossary_term: 1099-NEC Box 4 Federal Income Tax Withheld (Backup Withholding)
description: 1099-NEC Box 4: Federal income tax withheld under backup withholding rules (24% rate) when the contractor failed to provide a valid TIN or IRS notified backup withholding is required. Null for W-2 form types.

True if this tax form was submitted to the IRS/SSA via paper rather than electronic filing. Staffing companies filing 10 or more forms are required to e-file per IRS regulations. Tracks compliance with mandatory e-file thresholds.

Payers Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) as reported on 1099 forms. Typically the same as the employer EIN but may differ for EOR or co-employment arrangements where a third-party PEO is the payer of record.

Type of TIN used for the recipient/worker on this tax form. SSN for W-2 employees and individual contractors; EIN for contractors operating as a business entity; ITIN for foreign workers without an SSN. Drives IRS validation rules.

Two-letter US state abbreviation for the primary state of employment reported on this tax form (W-2 Box 15 state code). Drives state tax agency filing and multi-state payroll compliance. For workers with wages in multiple states, the primary state is recorded here.
State-assigned employer identification number used for state income tax withholding reporting (W-2 Box 15). Required for state e-filing submissions. Format varies by state.

Current status of the state tax agency filing for this form. Not_required: state has no income tax or filing not required. Pending: prepared but not yet submitted. Filed: submitted to state agency. Accepted: state agency confirmed receipt. Supports multi-state payroll compliance tracking.

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 17 State Income Tax Withheld
description: W-2 Box 17: Total state income tax withheld from the workers wages. Reported to the applicable state tax agency. Null for 1099 form types or states with no income tax.

W-2 Box 16: Total wages subject to state income tax withholding. May differ from Box 1 federal wages due to state-specific pre-tax exclusions. Supports multi-state payroll compliance. Null for 1099 form types.
The calendar tax year (e.g., 2024) for which this form reports wages, compensation, and withholdings. Drives IRS filing deadlines and year-end reconciliation cycles.

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 12 Code A Amount (Uncollected SS Tax on Tips)
description: W-2 Box 12 Code A: Uncollected Social Security tax on tips. One of up to four Box 12 entries capturing pre-tax benefit and special compensation amounts. Null if not applicable.

Pipe-delimited list of IRS Box 12 codes reported on this W-2 (e.g., D|DD|W). Code D = 401(k) elective deferrals; Code DD = employer-sponsored health coverage cost; Code W = HSA employer contributions; Code C = taxable cost of group-term life insurance. Supports multi-code scenarios common in staffing. Null for 1099 form types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|D|DD|E|F|G|H|J|K|L|M|N|P|Q|R|S|T|V|W|Y|Z|AA|BB|DD|EE|FF|GG|HH — promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 12 Total Pre-Tax Benefit Amount
description: Aggregate dollar amount across all Box 12 codes reported on this W-2. Used for year-end reconciliation of pre-tax benefit deductions (401k, HSA, health coverage). Null for 1099 form types.

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 2 Federal Income Tax Withheld
description: W-2 Box 2: Total federal income tax withheld from the workers wages during the tax year. Reported to IRS and used by worker for federal tax return filing. Null for 1099 form types.
W-2 Box 6: Employees share of Medicare (FICA) tax withheld. Equals 1.45% of Box 5 wages, plus 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax for high earners. Null for 1099 form types.

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 5 Medicare Wages and Tips
description: W-2 Box 5: Total wages and tips subject to Medicare (FICA) tax. No wage base cap applies. Includes wages above the Social Security wage base. Null for 1099 form types.

W-2 Box 14: Employer-reported informational amounts such as state disability insurance (SDI) withheld, union dues, educational assistance, or other items not captured in other boxes. Null for 1099 form types.

W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker was an active participant in an employer-sponsored retirement plan (401k, SIMPLE IRA, etc.) during the tax year. Affects IRA deductibility on the workers personal return. Null for 1099 form types.

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 4 Social Security Tax Withheld
description: W-2 Box 4: Employees share of Social Security (FICA) tax withheld. Equals 6.2% of Box 3 wages up to the wage base. Null for 1099 form types.
W-2 Box 3: Total wages subject to Social Security (FICA) tax, capped at the annual Social Security wage base limit. Null for 1099 form types.
W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker is classified as a statutory employee (e.g., certain drivers, traveling salespeople). Affects self-employment tax treatment. Null for 1099 form types.

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 13 Third-Party Sick Pay Indicator
description: W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker received third-party sick pay (e.g., from an insurance carrier) that is included in wages. Common in staffing for workers on short-term disability. Null for 1099 form types.

business_glossary_term: W-2 Box 1 Wages, Tips, Other Compensation
description: W-2 Box 1: Total taxable wages, tips, and other compensation subject to federal income tax withholding. Excludes pre-tax benefit deductions (401k, health insurance). Null for 1099 form types.
City of the workers mailing address as printed on the tax form. Used for form distribution and state tax filing jurisdiction determination.
First line of the workers mailing address as printed on the tax form for distribution. Required for paper form mailing and IRS/SSA filing. Appears in Box f of W-2 and Recipient Address of 1099.
Two-letter US state code of the workers mailing address. Drives state tax filing jurisdiction and multi-state payroll compliance rules.
ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code of the workers mailing address as printed on the tax form.

True if the workers copy of the tax form (Copy B for W-2, Copy B for 1099) has been distributed to the worker by the IRS deadline (January 31). Tracks compliance with worker distribution requirements.
Full legal name of the worker or independent contractor as it appears on the tax form (W-2 Box e or 1099 Recipient Name). Must match SSA records for W-2 employees to avoid name/SSN mismatch errors.

Workers Social Security Number (SSN) as reported on the tax form. Appears in Box a of W-2 and Recipient TIN field of 1099 forms. Masked in most operational views; full value required only for IRS e-filing and SSA submission. Governed by FCRA and IRS regulations.

Two-letter state code for the state under which workers compensation (Workers Comp) insurance was carried for this worker during the tax year. Relevant for W-2 employees on temporary assignments. Used in year-end workers comp audit reconciliation.
Primary key for tax_line

Reference to the original tax line record that this adjustment line corrects or reverses. Null for original (non-adjustment) lines. Enables audit trail linking between original and corrected tax line records.
Reference to the payroll processing run (batch) in which this tax line was computed. Supports reconciliation of all tax lines within a single payroll cycle.

Reference to the parent pay stub (paycheck) that this tax line belongs to. Establishes the header/detail relationship between the pay stub and its individual tax line items. TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category.
Reference to the worker (W-2 employee or 1099 independent contractor) for whom this tax line is computed. TRANSACTION_LINE PARTY_REFERENCE category.

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Tax lines are computed under state-specific tax rules (rates, wage bases, deposit schedules, SUTA experience rates). Staffing firms link tax lines to the governing state compliance rule for state tax

Foreign key linking to payroll.tax_filing. Business justification: Tax line records (individual tax obligations per pay stub) feed into aggregate tax filings (941, state quarterly filings). Adding FK tax_filing_id → tax_filing.payroll_tax_filing_id links each tax_lin

Foreign key linking to payroll.tax_withholding_election. Business justification: Tax withholding execution (tax_line) should reference the workers tax withholding election (W-4 form data) that drove the calculation. tax_line currently has denormalized attributes filing_status, al

Indicates whether this tax line represents the Additional Medicare Tax (0.9% surtax) applicable to high-earning employees whose wages exceed the IRS threshold ($200,000 for single filers). Employer does not match this surtax.

Indicates whether this tax line is an adjustment or correction to a previously processed tax line (e.g., retroactive rate change, prior-period correction, amended return adjustment). When True, adjustment_reason should be populated.
Free-text description of the reason for a tax line adjustment when adjustment_flag is True (e.g., Retroactive SUTA rate change Q1 2024, W-4 correction applied, Prior period underpayment correction).

The date on which the paycheck or direct deposit was issued to the worker. Determines the tax deposit liability date and the calendar year to which the tax obligation is attributed for W-2 reporting purposes.

The calculated tax dollar amount for this line in the current pay period (taxable_wage_base × tax_rate, or flat amount for flat-rate taxes). Core value used in gross-to-net calculation and payroll tax remittance. TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category.
Timestamp when this tax line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements. TRANSACTION_LINE RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.

The employers IRS-assigned federal tax deposit schedule for this tax type (monthly, semi-weekly, or next-day for large deposits). Determines the tax_deposit_liability_date. State deposit schedules may differ.

business_glossary_term: Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) State Credit Rate
description: The FUTA state tax credit rate (up to 5.4%) applied to reduce the gross FUTA rate of 6.0% when the employer has paid SUTA taxes in a credit-reduction state. Applies only to FUTA tax type lines.
Sequential ordering number of this tax line within the parent pay stub. Enables deterministic ordering and display of all tax line items on a pay stub. TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.
The last calendar date of the pay period to which this tax line applies. Together with pay_period_start_date, defines the exact payroll period for tax obligation accrual and filing.
The first calendar date of the pay period to which this tax line applies. Used for period-over-period tax reporting, quarterly filing (Form 941/940), and annual W-2 reconciliation.

Current status of the tax remittance for this line to the applicable tax authority. Tracks whether the tax has been deposited on time, is pending, was remitted late (triggering penalty exposure), has been adjusted, or voided. TRANSACTION_LINE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.

The native record identifier from TempWorks Payroll (the system of record) for this tax line. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational payroll system.

business_glossary_term: State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) Experience Rate
description: The employers SUTA experience-rated tax rate for the applicable state, as assigned by the state unemployment agency based on the companys claims history. Applies only to SUTA tax type lines. Drives burden rate calculations.

business_glossary_term: State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) State Code
description: Two-letter state code identifying the state to which SUTA taxes are remitted for this worker. May differ from the workers work state in multi-state assignments. Applies only to SUTA tax type lines.
Level of government authority imposing this tax obligation. Supports multi-state payroll compliance and jurisdiction-level tax filing and reporting.

The date by which the employer must remit this tax to the applicable tax authority (IRS, state, or local). Determined by the employers deposit schedule (monthly or semi-weekly for federal; varies by state). Critical for avoiding late deposit penalties.

Indicates whether the worker is exempt from this specific tax type (e.g., FICA exemption for certain visa holders, FUTA exemption for specific worker categories). When True, computed_amount is zero and the exemption basis should be documented.
Reason or basis for the tax exemption when tax_exempt_flag is True (e.g., F-1 Visa FICA Exemption, W-4 Exempt Election, Agricultural Worker FUTA Exemption). Required for compliance audit trail.

business_glossary_term: Tax Jurisdiction Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) Code
description: Five-digit FIPS code uniquely identifying the county or county-equivalent jurisdiction for this tax line. Enables precise multi-state and local tax jurisdiction mapping and filing.
Name of the local taxing jurisdiction (city, county, municipality, school district) for local/city tax lines. Null for federal and state-level taxes. Supports local tax filing compliance.
Two-letter U.S. state code (USPS abbreviation) for the state in which this tax obligation applies. Null for federal-only taxes. Critical for multi-state payroll tax filing and SUTA rate management.

Calendar quarter (1–4) in which this tax line falls, derived from the check date. Used for quarterly payroll tax filing (IRS Form 941, FUTA Form 940, and state equivalents) and quarterly reconciliation.

The effective tax rate (as a decimal fraction, e.g., 0.062000 for 6.2%) applied to the taxable wage base to compute the tax amount for this line. For flat-dollar taxes, this may be zero with the flat amount captured in computed_amount.

Discriminator indicating whether this tax line represents an employee-side withholding (deducted from worker gross pay) or an employer-side tax obligation (borne by the staffing company). Critical for gross-to-net calculation and burden rate validation. TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category (discriminator).

Standardized code identifying the specific type of tax obligation on this line (e.g., FIT, SIT, FICA_SS_EE, FICA_MED_EE, FICA_SS_ER, FICA_MED_ER, FUTA, SUTA, LOCAL, ADD_MED, SUPP). Used for tax filing, reporting, and reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIT|SIT|LOCAL|FICA_SS_EE|FICA_MED_EE|FICA_SS_ER|FICA_MED_ER|FUTA|SUTA|ADD_MED|SUPP — promote to reference product]
Human-readable name of the tax type (e.g., Federal Income Tax, California State Income Tax, FICA Social Security Employee, Federal Unemployment Tax). Displayed on pay stubs and tax reports.
Calendar year (e.g., 2024) to which this tax line is attributed for annual tax reporting, W-2/1099 issuance, and year-end reconciliation.

The portion of the workers gross wages subject to this specific tax in the current pay period, after applying any applicable wage base caps (e.g., Social Security wage base, FUTA/SUTA wage base limits). TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.
Timestamp when this tax line record was last modified. Tracks adjustments, corrections, and status changes for audit trail and data lineage purposes. TRANSACTION_LINE RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.

The annual taxable wage base ceiling for this tax type (e.g., $168,600 for Social Security in 2024; $7,000 for FUTA). Once the workers YTD wages exceed this limit, no further tax is assessed for this tax type in the calendar year.

Indicates whether the worker has reached the annual taxable wage base ceiling for this tax type in the current calendar year. When True, no further tax is assessed for this tax type until the next calendar year reset.

Method used to calculate the tax withholding for this line: percentage of taxable wages, flat dollar amount, IRS tax table lookup, supplemental flat rate, or exempt (no withholding). Drives the calculation logic applied in TempWorks Payroll.

Indicates whether the worker is classified as a W-2 employee (subject to withholding) or a 1099 independent contractor (IC) for this tax line. Drives which tax obligations apply and which annual tax form is issued. Aligns with IRS worker classification rules.

Cumulative tax amount withheld or assessed for this worker under this tax type from the start of the calendar year through and including this pay period. Critical for quarterly (941/940) and annual (W-2/1099) tax reconciliation and filing.

Cumulative taxable wages for this worker under this tax type from the start of the calendar year through and including this pay period. Used to enforce annual wage base limits and for W-2/1099 annual reconciliation.
Unique surrogate identifier for each tax withholding election record. Primary key for the tax_withholding_election data product in the payroll domain.
Reference to the active placement or assignment associated with this withholding election, enabling assignment-level tax configuration for multi-assignment workers.

Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire employees submit W-4 elections when staffing firm processes payroll under PEO or transition arrangements. Links tax elections to permanent placement for withholding compliance.

Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC agreement's worker_classification_basis directly governs withholding election setup — true IC workers are typically exempt from withholding. Staffing payroll teams must reference the IC agreement w

Foreign key linking to recruitment.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Tax withholding elections are collected during onboarding. Linking to onboarding engagement supports IRS compliance verification that W-4/state tax forms were obtained before first paycheck, required
Reference to the worker (W-2 employee or 1099 independent contractor) whose tax withholding elections are captured in this record. Links to the candidate or worker master record.

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW/IC workers have distinct withholding elections (backup withholding for 1099, W-2 for EOR SOW). Tax withholding elections must reference the SOW engagement to determine correct tax treatment. IRS b

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Withholding elections must comply with state-specific rules (mandatory withholding states, flat rate states, exemption eligibility). Staffing firms link withholding elections to the governing state co
Reference to the newer tax_withholding_election record that replaced this one. Populated when election_status = superseded. Enables forward-chaining of the election history for audit trails.

Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Tax withholding elections (W-4) are only applicable to W-2 employees, not independent contractors. Worker classification determines withholding eligibility and form type - misclassified workers trigge

Number of withholding allowances claimed by the worker on pre-2020 W-4 forms. Each allowance reduces the amount of federal income tax withheld. Applicable only for W-4 forms prior to the 2020 redesign. Null for 2020+ W-4 forms which use the step-based approach.

Username or employee ID of the payroll supervisor or manager who reviewed and approved this withholding election entry. Required for IRS lock-in letter overrides and exempt status elections as part of internal controls.
Timestamp when this withholding election record was first created in the payroll system. Supports audit trail, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and IRS record retention requirements for withholding forms.
Dollar amount of itemized or other deductions entered in Step 4b of the 2020+ W-4. Reduces withholding by accounting for deductions that exceed the standard deduction. Null for pre-2020 W-4 forms.

Dollar amount of qualifying dependent credits entered in Step 3 of the 2020+ redesigned W-4. Reduces the amount of income subject to withholding. Includes child tax credit ($2,000 per qualifying child under 17) and other dependent credits ($500 per other dependent). Null for pre-2020 W-4 forms.

Reference identifier for the physical or electronic withholding form document stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM or SAP SuccessFactors document repository). Enables retrieval of the original signed form for audit and compliance verification.

Date on which this withholding election becomes effective and begins driving tax withholding calculations in pay runs. Aligns with IRS requirement that new W-4 elections take effect no later than the start of the first payroll period ending on or after the 30th day after the form is received.

Free-text notes entered by payroll staff regarding this withholding election. May include reasons for IRS lock-in overrides, documentation of verbal instructions, or notes on unusual withholding configurations.
Externally-visible business identifier for this withholding election record, used for audit trails, payroll processing references, and worker communications (e.g., TWE-2024-00123).

The channel or method through which the withholding election was submitted. Paper form = physical W-4 or state form submitted; Electronic self-service = worker submitted via online portal (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors ESS); Employer default = system default applied when no form received; IRS lock-in = IRS-mandated withholding rate overriding worker election; Verbal instruction = documented verbal request.

Current lifecycle state of this withholding election. Active = currently driving withholding calculations; Superseded = replaced by a newer election; Pending = submitted but not yet effective; Cancelled = voided before taking effect; Expired = past the expiration date.

Sequential version number for withholding elections for the same worker and jurisdiction. Increments each time the worker submits a new withholding form. Enables tracking of election history and supersession chain for audit and compliance purposes.

Username or employee ID of the payroll staff member or system process that entered this withholding election into TempWorks Payroll. Supports audit trail requirements for payroll compliance and SOC 2 Type II controls.

Date on which this withholding election expires and is no longer valid. Applicable for exempt-from-withholding elections which expire annually (February 15 of the following year per IRS rules), or for state-specific elections with defined validity periods.

The workers federal or state tax filing status as declared on the withholding form. Drives the applicable standard deduction and tax bracket used in withholding calculations. Values align with IRS filing status categories.

Fixed percentage rate applied to gross wages for income tax withholding when the withholding method is percentage or flat_amount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2200 = 22%). Used for supplemental wage withholding at the IRS flat supplemental rate or for state flat-rate withholding.

The specific tax withholding form type submitted by the worker. Examples include W-4 (federal), W-4P (pension), state equivalents (e.g., CA DE-4, NY IT-2104, IL W-4, TX no state income tax). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: W-4|W-4P|W-4S|W-4V|DE-4|IT-2104|IL-W-4|VA-4|NC-4|GA-G-4 — promote to reference product]
The maximum number of withholding allowances permitted by the IRS lock-in letter, overriding the workers own W-4 election. Employer must not allow the worker to claim more allowances than this value.

The filing status mandated by the IRS lock-in letter, overriding the workers own W-4 election. Populated only when an IRS lock-in letter is in effect. Employer must use this status for withholding calculations.

Date of the IRS lock-in letter received by the employer mandating a specific withholding rate for this worker. When present, the employer must withhold at the IRS-specified rate regardless of the workers W-4 elections. Null if no lock-in letter applies.

Indicates whether the worker has claimed exemption from federal or state income tax withholding. True = worker claims exempt status and no income tax will be withheld. Exempt status expires February 15 each year and must be renewed. Employer must retain the exempt W-4 and notify IRS if required.
Indicates whether the worker has claimed exemption from state income tax withholding on the state withholding form. Exempt criteria and renewal requirements vary by state.

Indicates whether this election applies to federal, state, or local tax withholding. Federal elections are governed by IRS W-4; state elections by equivalent state withholding forms; local elections by municipality-specific forms.

Name of the local taxing jurisdiction (city, county, or municipality) for local income tax withholding elections. Applicable in states with local income taxes such as Ohio, Pennsylvania, Kentucky, and New York City.

Indicates whether the worker checked the Step 2 multiple jobs checkbox on the 2020+ W-4, signaling they hold multiple concurrent jobs or their spouse also works. When true, withholding is calculated using the higher withholding rate tables to prevent under-withholding.

Indicates whether the worker is a nonresident alien (NRA) for U.S. tax purposes. NRA workers are subject to special withholding rules under IRS Publication 15 and must follow specific W-4 instructions (cannot claim exempt, must add an additional amount to wages for withholding calculation). Relevant for staffing firms placing international workers.

The pay frequency associated with this withholding election, used to annualize wages for withholding table lookups. Must match the workers actual pay frequency in the pay run. Drives the correct withholding amount per period.

Date the withholding election form was received by the employer or payroll department. Used to determine the required effective date per IRS rules (must take effect no later than the first payroll period ending on or after the 30th day after receipt).

Additional flat dollar amount the worker requests to be withheld for state income tax beyond the calculated state withholding. Applied per pay period in addition to the standard state withholding calculation.

Number of withholding allowances claimed on the state withholding form. Many states still use an allowance-based system independent of the federal W-4 redesign (e.g., California DE-4, New York IT-2104). Null for states with no income tax or states that have adopted the federal W-4.

Two-letter USPS state abbreviation identifying the state jurisdiction for state or local withholding elections. Null for federal elections. Supports multi-state payroll compliance across all states where Staffing Hr places workers.

The workers filing status as declared on the state withholding form, which may differ from the federal filing status. State-specific values vary by jurisdiction (e.g., California uses single, married, head of household with different withholding tables). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|married|married_higher_rate|head_of_household|exempt — promote to reference product]

Method used to withhold income tax on supplemental wages (bonuses, commissions, overtime, per diem above IRS threshold). Flat rate = mandatory 22% federal flat rate for supplemental wages under $1M; Aggregate = combined with regular wages using withholding tables; Optional flat = employer elects flat rate when not mandatory.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country with which the worker claims a U.S. tax treaty benefit, potentially reducing or eliminating withholding on certain income types. Applicable for nonresident alien workers claiming treaty exemptions via Form 8233 or W-8BEN.
Timestamp when this withholding election record was last modified. Tracks changes to election data for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Databricks Silver Layer.

The tax year version of the W-4 or equivalent withholding form submitted by the worker. Critical because the 2020 redesigned W-4 eliminated allowances and introduced a new step-based structure. Pre-2020 forms use allowances; 2020+ forms use Step 3 dependent credits and Step 4 adjustments.

The calculation method used to compute income tax withholding for this worker. Percentage method uses IRS Publication 15-T tables; Wage bracket method uses bracket tables; Flat amount applies a fixed dollar amount; Annualized projects annual income for withholding; Cumulative adjusts for year-to-date withholding.

Classification of the worker for tax withholding purposes. W-2 = employee subject to income tax withholding and FICA; 1099 = independent contractor (IC) not subject to employer withholding; EOR = Employer of Record arrangement where the EOR entity handles withholding. Drives whether withholding elections are applicable.

Timestamp when this document collection record was first created in the system.
Human-readable name or title of the document collected (e.g., Master Service Agreement Acknowledgment, Non-Disclosure Agreement, W-4 Federal Withholding).

Current lifecycle status of the document in the collection and signing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|sent|viewed|signed|completed|declined|expired|voided — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Type of document collected during onboarding or offboarding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: msa_acknowledgment|nda|non_compete|handbook_acknowledgment|safety_policy|drug_screen_consent|bgc_authorization|fcra_disclosure|offer_letter|assignment_confirmation|w4|state_withholding|w9|1099_setup|i9|direct_deposit_form|emergency_contact_form|equipment_acknowledgment|confidentiality_agreement|code_of_conduct|pto_policy|benefits_enrollment|cobra_notice|final_paycheck_acknowledgment|exit_interview|property_return_form — promote to reference product]
Version identifier of the document template or form used (e.g., v2.1, 2024-Q1), ensuring compliance with the correct policy or legal version.
Date from which the document becomes effective or binding, particularly relevant for tax forms (W-4, state withholding) and policy acknowledgments.
Indicates whether the worker claimed exempt status from federal or state withholding on the tax form (True = exempt, False = not exempt). Applicable to W-4 and state withholding document types.

Date the worker submitted the completed form to the employer or staffing agency. May differ from signed_date for forms that require additional processing or manual submission.
Indicates whether the document was signed electronically via DocuSign or similar platform (True) or via wet signature/manual process (False).
Indicates whether this document is mandatory for the worker to complete onboarding or offboarding (True) or optional (False).

Reason the document was rejected or declined, if applicable (e.g., incomplete information, incorrect form version, illegible signature).
Date the document was reviewed and approved by HR or compliance staff.
Free-text notes or comments entered by the reviewer regarding the document review, any issues identified, or follow-up actions required.
Date the document was sent to the worker for review or signature.
Date the worker signed or acknowledged the document, completing the signature workflow.
IP address from which the worker signed the document electronically, captured for audit and compliance purposes.
System or platform from which the document was originated or collected (e.g., DocuSign CLM, SAP SuccessFactors, TempWorks Payroll, manual upload).
Reference to the physical or digital storage location where the signed document is archived (e.g., cloud storage URI, document management system path, file share location).

Indicates whether this document supersedes or replaces a previously submitted document of the same type (True) or is a new submission (False). Particularly relevant for tax forms (W-4, state withholding) when workers update their withholding elections.
Tax year for which the tax form document (W-4, state withholding, W-9, 1099 setup) is applicable. Null for non-tax documents.
Timestamp when this document collection record was last modified or updated.
Date the worker first viewed or opened the document.

Current lifecycle status of the checklist template: active (in use), inactive (temporarily disabled), draft (under development), or archived (retired but retained for historical reference).
Indicates whether completion of this checklist requires formal approval or sign-off before the worker can start assignment (True) or is informational only (False).

Business-friendly name of the task checklist template (e.g., Healthcare Temp Onboarding, IT Contractor Onboarding, Light Industrial Assignment End).

Category of the checklist template indicating the business process it supports: onboarding (new worker start), offboarding (assignment end), redeployment (worker reassignment), or extension (assignment continuation).
Indicates whether this checklist template is customized for a specific client account (True) or is a general template applicable across multiple clients (False).
Indicates whether this checklist is driven by regulatory or compliance requirements (True) such as I-9, E-Verify, background checks, or is purely operational (False).
Date and time when this checklist template version was first created in the system.
Business division or vertical this checklist is designed for (e.g., Healthcare, IT, Light Industrial, Professional). Nullable if checklist is division-agnostic.
Date from which this checklist template version becomes active and available for use in creating task assignments.
Expected number of business days required to complete all tasks in this checklist under normal circumstances. Used for planning and Service Level Agreement (SLA) management.
Date after which this checklist template version is no longer active. Nullable for templates with indefinite validity.
Indicates whether completion of this checklist is mandatory (True) for the associated placement type and scenario, or optional (False).
Username or identifier of the user who last modified this checklist template version.
Date and time when this checklist template version was last modified.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context about this checklist template that does not fit structured fields.
Business role or department responsible for maintaining and updating this checklist template (e.g., Onboarding Team, Compliance Department, Credentialing Team).

Indicates whether this checklist includes worker orientation or onboarding training as a required task.
Service Level Agreement (SLA) target in hours for completing all tasks in this checklist from initiation. Used for performance measurement and client commitments.
Detailed description of the checklist template purpose, scope, and intended use cases. Provides context for users selecting appropriate templates.
Total number of individual tasks defined within this checklist template. Used for progress tracking and workload estimation.
Version number of this checklist template. Incremented when template is revised to track changes over time and support audit trails.
Username or identifier of the user who created this checklist template version.

Unique system-generated identifier for each worker assignment record. Primary key of the assignment data product and the SSOT for active worker assignment status in the placement domain. Serves as the backbone of the Bullhorn ATS placement record.

Reference to the Master Service Agreement governing the terms and conditions of this assignment, including rate terms, conversion fees, and liability provisions. Sourced from DocuSign CLM.

Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: Assignments are governed by a specific pay period cycle that determines timesheet submission deadlines, payroll cutoff dates, and billing cycles. Staffing operations managers use assignment→pay_period

Reference to the candidate (worker) placed on this assignment. Links the assignment to the candidate master record in the Bullhorn ATS candidate entity.

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Assignments are fulfilled by staffing suppliers who provide the contingent worker. Core operational link for supplier performance tracking, invoice reconciliation, VMS routing, and MSP program managem

Foreign key linking to candidate.talent_pool. Business justification: Talent pool sourcing attribution: assignments sourced from bench or redeployment pools must be tracked for pool ROI reporting, fill-rate analytics by pool type, and MSP/VMS program performance dashboa

Foreign key linking to vendor.tier_classification. Business justification: VMS programs route assignments to suppliers based on tier classification (preferred/tier 1/tier 2). Direct link enables tier-based workflow automation, allocation enforcement, and supplier performance

Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Assignments are executed under a vendor agreement governing supplier markup caps, compliance standards, and worker classification terms. Vendor agreement compliance auditing for active assignments req

Foreign key linking to joborder.vms_order. Business justification: In MSP/VMS-managed programs, each assignment originates from a specific vms_order record. This link enables VMS reconciliation reporting — matching VMS platform order records to resulting assignments

Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: VMS-sourced assignments must reference the VMS program for bill rate cap enforcement, VMS fee calculation, markup cap compliance, and timesheet approval cycle routing. assignment.vms_req_number is a d

Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: I-9/E-Verify compliance requires each active assignment be linked to the worker's verified work authorization record. assignment.i9_verified is a boolean only — a proper FK enables I-9 audit reports,
Reference to the specific client work location where the worker is physically assigned. Supports workforce scheduling, dispatch, and geographic workforce analytics.

The date the assignment actually ended, which may differ from the scheduled end date due to early termination, extension, or conversion. Null if the assignment is still active. Used for fall-off rate and tenure analytics.

Current lifecycle state of the assignment. Active = worker currently on assignment; Ended = assignment completed or terminated; Extended = original end date extended; Converted = temp-to-perm or CTH conversion completed; Cancelled = assignment cancelled before start or during placement.

Classification of the assignment engagement model. Temp = short-term temporary placement; Contract = fixed-duration contract; Contract-to-Hire (CTH) = contract with intent to convert; Temp-to-Perm = temporary placement with conversion pathway; Direct Hire = permanent placement.

Current status of the background check (BGC) for this assignment. Clear = passed; Adverse = failed or flagged; Waived = client waived requirement. Sourced from Sterling Background Check. Required for FCRA compliance and client credentialing.

The hourly or unit rate charged to the client for the workers services. Core financial field used in invoicing, gross margin calculation, and rate card compliance. Expressed in USD per hour unless otherwise noted.
The externally-known placement record identifier from the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system of record. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability back to the source ATS.

The date on which a temp-to-perm or contract-to-hire worker was formally converted to a permanent employee of the client. Null if no conversion has occurred. Triggers conversion fee billing and assignment status change to converted.

The fee charged to the client upon conversion of a temp-to-perm or contract-to-hire worker to a permanent employee. Applicable only for CTH and temp-to-perm assignment types. Governed by the clients MSA terms.
The clients internal cost center or department code to which the workers costs are allocated. Used for client billing, PO matching, and workforce cost allocation reporting in the clients ERP system.

The timestamp when the assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Time to Fill (TTF) / Time to Start (TTS) KPI calculations. Conforms to ISO 8601 format.
The clients department or business unit where the worker is placed. Supports workforce analytics, headcount reporting by department, and client billing allocation.

Current status of the pre-placement drug screening for this assignment. Negative = passed; Positive = failed; Waived = client waived requirement. Sourced from Sterling Background Check. Required for client compliance and OSHA safety standards.

The reason code for why the assignment ended. Used for fall-off rate analysis, quality of service reporting, and client relationship management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: assignment_completed|client_terminated|worker_resigned|converted_to_perm|cancelled|no_show — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]

The number of times this assignments end date has been extended. Used to track assignment longevity, client satisfaction, and redeployment planning. Incremented each time a new scheduled end date is set beyond the prior one.

Workers FLSA overtime exemption status for this assignment. Non-Exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay; Exempt workers are not. Critical for payroll compliance, DOL audit readiness, and co-employment risk management.

Indicates whether the workers I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification form has been completed and verified for this assignment. Required by the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) before work commencement.
Indicates whether this assignment was created to backfill a position vacated by a previous worker. Used for workforce analytics, fall-off rate tracking, and client relationship management.

Indicates whether the worker is eligible for redeployment to another assignment upon completion of this one. Used by recruiters to proactively manage bench workers and reduce time-to-fill on new requisitions.
Indicates whether the worker performs the assignment remotely (True) or on-site at the client location (False). Affects workers comp classification, state tax nexus, and workforce scheduling.
The job title or position title for the role the worker is filling on this assignment. Used for workforce reporting, job classification, and EEOC/OFCCP compliance reporting.
The timestamp when the assignment record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance. Conforms to ISO 8601 format.
The percentage markup applied over the pay rate to arrive at the bill rate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.45 = 45%). Used for rate card compliance, margin analysis, and client pricing governance.

Current status of the workers onboarding process for this assignment. Tracks completion of I-9, E-Verify, background check, drug screen, and orientation steps required before the worker can begin. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding.

The hourly bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours worked by the placed worker. Typically 1.5x the standard bill rate per FLSA requirements. Required for accurate client invoicing and OT cost tracking.
The hourly pay rate paid to the worker for overtime hours. Typically 1.5x the standard pay rate per FLSA requirements. Required for payroll processing and FLSA compliance auditing.

The hourly or unit rate paid to the worker for services rendered. Core financial field used in payroll processing, spread calculation, and markup analysis. Expressed in USD per hour unless otherwise noted.

Indicates whether the worker is eligible to receive per diem allowances (meals, lodging, incidentals) for this assignment. Typically applies to travel assignments or placements away from the workers tax home. Affects payroll tax treatment.
The client-issued purchase order number authorizing spend for this assignment. Required for invoice matching and accounts payable processing at the client. Sourced from Beeline VMS or client ERP.
The originally contracted or currently scheduled end date of the assignment. Used for proactive redeployment planning, extension tracking, and workforce bench management.
The standard number of hours per week the worker is scheduled to work on this assignment. Used for FTE calculation, workforce planning, and prorated billing. Typically 40 for full-time placements.

The gross spread per hour calculated as bill rate minus pay rate. Represents the staffing firms gross revenue per hour before burden costs. Key profitability metric tracked at the assignment level per ASA industry standards.
The actual date the worker began the assignment at the client work location. Used for tenure calculations, billing cycle initiation, and Time to Start (TTS) KPI measurement.

business_glossary_term: Worker Classification (W-2 / 1099 / Corp-to-Corp / Employer of Record)
description: Tax and employment classification of the worker for this assignment. W-2 = employee of the staffing firm; 1099 = independent contractor; Corp-to-Corp = contractor through their own entity; EOR = employer of record arrangement. Drives payroll tax treatment and IRS compliance.

The NCCI or state-specific workers compensation class code assigned to this placement based on the job duties performed. Determines the workers comp insurance premium rate applied to the assignment. Required for accurate burden rate calculation and insurance compliance.
Unique surrogate identifier for each assignment-compliance requirement fulfillment record

Foreign key linking to the worker assignment record that must satisfy this compliance requirement

Foreign key linking to the specific compliance requirement that must be fulfilled for this assignment
Foreign key to compliance.compliance_requirement.compliance_requirement_id

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Individual assignment compliance requirements are often mandated by specific state rules (state BGC timing laws, state drug testing statutes, state-mandated training). Compliance teams need to trace e
The date on which this compliance requirement was successfully fulfilled for this assignment. Used to calculate re-verification due dates and track compliance history.
System timestamp when this assignment-compliance requirement record was created, typically at assignment activation when requirements are evaluated and assigned

The date on which this completed requirement expires for this assignment and must be re-verified. Calculated from completion_date plus the requirements re_verification_interval_days. Null if requirement does not expire.
System timestamp of the most recent update to this assignment-compliance fulfillment record, tracking status changes, completions, and re-verifications

The date by which this requirement must be re-verified for this assignment to maintain compliance. Typically set before expiration_date to allow processing time. Drives operational workflows and alerts.

Current status of this compliance requirement for this specific assignment: pending (not yet started), in_progress (documentation being collected), completed (requirement satisfied), waived (exception granted), expired (completed but now expired and needs re-verification), failed (requirement not met), not_applicable (requirement determined not to apply to this assignment)

The method used to verify fulfillment of this requirement for this assignment: document_review (manual review of uploaded documents), background_check (third-party BGC vendor), drug_test (lab results), training_completion (LMS integration), credential_verification (license/certification check), client_attestation (client confirmation), system_integration (automated verification via API)
Free-text notes captured by the verifying user regarding the fulfillment of this requirement for this assignment. May include document names, vendor confirmation numbers, or special circumstances.
Indicates whether a waiver or exception was granted for this requirement on this assignment, allowing the assignment to proceed despite non-completion
Business justification for granting a waiver for this requirement on this assignment. Required when waiver_flag is true. Captures approval authority and business rationale.
Unique surrogate primary key for each assignment extension event record in the placement domain. Globally unique identifier for this transactional record.

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Extension approval workflows must verify worker credentials remain valid through the new end date. This link enables compliance gates on extension requests — particularly critical for expirable creden

Foreign key linking to recruitment.hiring_decision. Business justification: Extensions with rate changes require a new or amended hiring decision record for client re-authorization. Compliance and billing teams trace each extension to the authorizing hiring decision for rate-

Reference to the job order (requisition) under which the assignment extension is being processed. Links the extension to the client demand record.

Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: Assignment extensions take effect at a pay period boundary to ensure revised rates and new end dates are applied correctly in payroll and billing. Payroll operations require assignment_extension→pay_p

Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Assignment extensions require compliance re-verification (credential expiration checks, work authorization reverification, updated BGC if client-required). Compliance teams run a placement_compliance_
Reference to the parent temporary or contract assignment record that is being extended. Core linkage to the placement assignment lifecycle.
Reference to the candidate (worker) whose assignment is being extended. Enables fall-off rate monitoring and redeployment planning at the worker level.

Foreign key linking to vendor.rate_agreement. Business justification: Extension rate approval workflows in MSP programs require validating revised_bill_rate and revised_pay_rate against the supplier's negotiated rate agreement, including markup caps, rate lock periods,

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Extensions need direct supplier link for supplier scorecard metrics (extension approval rate, time-to-extend). While inherited from parent assignment, direct link enables supplier performance reportin
The business date on which the extension request was formally approved or rejected by the designated approver. Used for SLA measurement and approval cycle time reporting.

The native record identifier assigned to this extension event in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
Email address of the client-side manager or supervisor who authorized the extension. Used for extension confirmation communications and audit trail.
Name of the client-side manager or supervisor who authorized the extension request. Captured as a denormalized field from the extension workflow for audit and reporting purposes.
Indicates whether the worker becomes eligible for temp-to-perm conversion upon completion of the extended assignment period. Triggers conversion fee tracking and direct hire workflow.
The timestamp when this extension record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Provides the audit trail entry point for the extension lifecycle.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the bill rate and pay rate fields on this extension record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency staffing operations.
The business date on which the approved extension was formally executed and the new end date was applied to the assignment record. Marks the completion of the extension lifecycle.

Number of calendar days added to the assignment by this extension event, calculated as new_end_date minus prior_end_date. Stored as a business field to support workforce planning and analytics without requiring date arithmetic at query time.
Externally-known business identifier for this extension event, generated by the Bullhorn assignment extension workflow. Used for cross-system reference and client communication.

Standardized reason code categorizing why the assignment extension was requested. Supports analytics on extension drivers and workforce planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: project_continuation|headcount_backfill|client_request|worker_request|sow_extension|other — promote to reference product]

Free-text narrative providing additional context or justification for the extension beyond the standardized reason code. Captured from the client or staffing coordinator during the extension request workflow.

Ordinal position of this extension event within the full extension history of the assignment (e.g., 1 = first extension, 2 = second extension). Enables tracking of cumulative extensions per assignment for fall-off rate and redeployment analytics.
Current lifecycle state of the extension request within the approval and execution workflow. Drives operational actions such as rate renegotiation, onboarding updates, and redeployment planning.
Indicates whether this extension event has been flagged as at risk of fall-off (early termination before the new end date). Supports fall-off rate monitoring and proactive account management.
Indicates whether this extension is managed under a Managed Service Provider (MSP) program, requiring VMS workflow compliance and additional approval routing.
The revised assignment end date established by this extension event. Becomes the active end date on the parent assignment record upon execution. Drives redeployment planning and workforce scheduling.
The hourly bill rate charged to the client that was in effect on the assignment immediately before this extension event. Stored for rate change audit and spread analysis.
The assignment end date that was in effect immediately before this extension event was applied. Critical for auditing the extension history and calculating the duration of each extension increment.
The hourly pay rate paid to the worker that was in effect on the assignment immediately before this extension event. Stored for rate change audit and gross margin analysis.

Indicates whether this extension event included a renegotiation of the bill rate or pay rate. True when either revised_bill_rate or revised_pay_rate differs from the prior rate. Enables quick filtering of rate-change extensions for financial impact analysis.

Indicates whether the worker has been flagged for redeployment planning as part of this extension review. True when the extension is not approved or when the new end date triggers a redeployment workflow.
Free-text explanation provided by the approver when an extension request is rejected. Populated only when extension_status is rejected. Supports process improvement and client communication.
The business date on which the extension request was formally submitted by the client or staffing coordinator. Used to measure lead time and SLA compliance for extension processing.
The renegotiated hourly bill rate to be charged to the client effective upon execution of this extension. Null if the bill rate was not renegotiated as part of this extension event.
The renegotiated hourly pay rate to be paid to the worker effective upon execution of this extension. Null if the pay rate was not renegotiated as part of this extension event.

business_glossary_term: Prior Spread (Bill Rate minus Pay Rate)
description: The gross spread (bill rate minus pay rate) in effect on the assignment before this extension event. Stored as a business field to support margin trend analysis across extension history without requiring rate arithmetic at query time.

business_glossary_term: Revised Spread (Bill Rate minus Pay Rate)
description: The gross spread (revised bill rate minus revised pay rate) applicable after this extension event. Null if no rate change occurred. Supports margin impact analysis for renegotiated extensions.
The timestamp when this extension record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.

The requisition or extension request number assigned by the clients Vendor Management System (VMS) such as Beeline. Required for MSP-managed programs to reconcile extension events between the staffing firms ATS and the clients VMS.
Classification of the work arrangement for the extended assignment period. May differ from the original assignment if the extension includes a change in work modality.
Tax and employment classification of the worker on this assignment at the time of extension. Drives payroll processing, tax withholding, and co-employment compliance requirements.

Unique surrogate identifier for each assignment status history record. Primary key for this audit trail entity. Role: EVENT_LOG — append-only record of immutable status transition events for worker assignments.

Reference to the client account for which this assignment is being fulfilled. Denormalized to enable direct client-level SLA compliance and fall-off rate reporting without joining through the placement or job order records.

Reference to the job order (requisition) associated with this assignment. Supports TTF (Time to Fill) and TTS (Time to Start) SLA tracking by linking status transitions back to the originating job order.
Reference to the parent worker assignment record for which this status transition event was recorded. EVENT_SOURCE_REFERENCE — the assignment entity that emitted this status change event.

Reference to the candidate (worker) whose assignment status changed. Enables candidate-level fall-off rate analysis, redeployment tracking, and SLA compliance reporting across all assignments for a given worker.

Foreign key linking to recruitment.req_pipeline. Business justification: Assignment status transitions such as on-hold or cancelled are reflected in the req pipeline stage. Linking status history to pipeline enables pipeline-stage-to-assignment-status correlation reports u

Reference to the VMS (Vendor Management System) program under which this assignment operates, if applicable. Links to Beeline VMS program configuration for contingent workforce programs. Null for direct-client assignments not managed through a VMS.

The actual elapsed time in hours between the triggering business event and this status transition. Compared against sla_target_hours to determine SLA compliance. Supports TTF and TTS metric calculation in Power BI Analytics dashboards.

Indicates whether this status transition required a formal approval step (e.g., client approval for an extension, manager approval for a conversion). Supports workflow compliance and audit trail requirements.

The date and time when the status transition was formally approved by the approving user. Null when no approval was required. Used to measure approval cycle time for SLA compliance and process efficiency analytics.

One-time sign-on bonus amount offered to the candidate as part of the compensation package. Null if no sign-on bonus is included. Relevant for compensation benchmarking and candidate attraction analytics.

The timestamp when the offer record was last modified. Audit trail field used for change data capture (CDC), Silver layer incremental processing, and compliance audit trails.

The physical work arrangement offered to the candidate: onsite (at client location), remote (fully remote), or hybrid (combination). Relevant for candidate matching, client SLA compliance, and workforce planning.

Indicates whether the staffing firm is contractually obligated to provide a replacement candidate at no additional fee due to the fall-off occurring within the guarantee period.
Current status of the replacement fulfillment obligation for this fall-off event. Tracks whether a replacement has been sourced, placed, or if the obligation was waived or failed.

The gross revenue amount lost or at risk due to this fall-off event, expressed in the billing currency. For direct hire placements, represents the placement fee at risk. For temporary placements, represents estimated lost billing revenue. Critical for financial impact reporting.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this fall-off event record was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver Layer.
The timestamp when this fall-off event record was last modified in the data platform. Audit trail field for change tracking and data governance compliance.

The requisition number from the clients Vendor Management System (VMS) associated with the placement that fell off. Required for VMS-managed programs to report fall-off events back to the clients MSP/VMS platform.
Indicates whether the fall-off occurred within the contractual guarantee period (True) or after it expired (False). Determines replacement obligation and revenue impact applicability.
Classification of the work arrangement for the placement that fell off. Used to analyze fall-off patterns by work modality.

The hourly or unit rate charged to the client for the workers services. Represents the client-facing price component used in invoicing and revenue recognition. Expressed in the applicable currency unit.

The total employer-side cost loading rate applied on top of the pay rate, expressed as a decimal percentage. Encompasses FICA, FUTA, SUTA, workers compensation, and benefits costs. Used in true cost-of-labor calculations.

The one-time fee charged to the client when a temporary or contract-to-hire worker is converted to a permanent employee. Defined in the client MSA and applicable only for temp-to-perm placement types.
The date and time when this rate record was first created in the system. Provides the foundational audit trail entry for data lineage, compliance, and record lifecycle tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary rate fields in this record (e.g., USD, CAD). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements.

The percentage of the placed candidates first-year annual salary charged as the direct hire placement fee. Applicable only for direct hire and permanent placement engagements. Defined in the client MSA.

The hourly bill rate charged to the client for double-time hours, typically applicable on holidays or after extended daily thresholds per client contract or state law. Used in client invoicing for DT hours.

The hourly pay rate for double-time hours, typically 2x the regular pay rate. Applicable per client contract terms or state-mandated double-time rules. Used in payroll processing for DT-eligible hours.
The date on which this rate record becomes active and applicable to payroll and billing calculations. Marks the start of the rates binding period for the associated placement.
The date on which this rate record ceases to be applicable. Null indicates an open-ended rate with no defined end. Used to manage rate versioning, extensions, and contract renewals.

The gross margin expressed as a percentage of the bill rate, calculated as (Spread / Bill Rate). Represents the profitability ratio for this rate record and is a primary KPI for financial reporting and account management.

business_glossary_term: IRS GSA (General Services Administration) Rate Reference
description: The IRS General Services Administration (GSA) per diem rate reference code or location identifier used to validate per diem allowances against federally published rates. Applicable for per_diem rate types to ensure IRS compliance.
The hourly bill rate charged to the client for hours worked on designated holidays. Defined in the client MSA or SOW and used in holiday billing calculations.
The hourly pay rate for hours worked on designated holidays. Defined in the workers assignment terms and used in payroll processing for holiday-worked hours.

Indicates whether this rate record is currently active and eligible for use in payroll and billing calculations. A rate may be inactive due to expiration, supersession by a newer rate version, or manual deactivation.

Indicates whether this rate or per diem allowance is subject to federal and state income tax withholding. Per diem amounts at or below GSA rates are typically non-taxable; amounts exceeding GSA rates are taxable as wages.

The occupational classification code (e.g., DOL SOC code or client-defined job category code) associated with this rate. Drives workers compensation rate assignment, FLSA exemption status, and rate card categorization.

The percentage markup applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.35 = 35%). Used in rate negotiations, client proposals, and margin analysis reporting.

Free-text field for capturing additional context, negotiation history, special conditions, or exceptions associated with this rate record. Used by account managers and recruiters to document rate-specific business decisions.

The hourly bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours worked beyond the standard threshold (typically 40 hours/week under FLSA). Distinct from the standard bill rate to support accurate client invoicing for OT hours.
The hourly pay rate for overtime hours, required to be at least 1.5x the regular pay rate under FLSA. Used in payroll processing for hours exceeding the standard weekly threshold.

The hourly or unit rate paid to the worker for services rendered. Represents the worker-facing compensation component used in payroll processing. Must comply with applicable minimum wage laws under FLSA.
The maximum total per diem budget authorized for this rate record over the assignment period. Used to enforce spending controls and flag overages in per diem reimbursement processing.

Sub-classification of per diem rate type indicating the specific allowance category. Applicable only when rate_type = per_diem. Aligns with IRS GSA per diem schedule categories for tax compliance and reimbursement tracking.

The approved daily monetary allowance for the per diem category (travel, lodging, meals, or incidentals). Represents the maximum reimbursable or taxable daily amount for the applicable per diem category.

The type of placement engagement this rate record supports. Temporary covers standard temp assignments; contract_to_hire (temp-to-perm) includes conversion terms; direct_hire covers permanent placement fees; SOW (Statement of Work) covers project-based engagements.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this rate record within the source system (Bullhorn, Beeline VMS, or TempWorks). Used for cross-system reconciliation and rate card referencing.

The originating system or method from which this rate record was sourced. Bullhorn indicates the ATS rate card; beeline_vms indicates the VMS rate management module; tempworks indicates the payroll per diem configuration; manual indicates a directly entered rate.

Classification of the rate component this record represents. Standard covers regular bill/pay rates; overtime (OT) and double-time (DT) apply to FLSA-governed hours; holiday covers premium holiday pay; per_diem covers travel/lodging/meals/incidentals allowances; burden covers employer-side cost loading. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|overtime|double_time|holiday|per_diem|burden — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]

The method used to calculate and process per diem reimbursements. Actual expense requires receipts; flat_rate pays a fixed daily amount regardless of actual spend; gsa_rate uses the IRS GSA published rate for the work location.

The gross dollar difference between the bill rate and the pay rate (Bill Rate minus Pay Rate). Represents the staffing firms gross revenue per hour before burden costs. A key profitability metric tracked at the placement level.

The time or work unit basis for the bill and pay rates. Hourly is standard for temporary staffing; daily/weekly/monthly may apply to contract or SOW engagements; fixed applies to direct hire placement fees.
The date and time when this rate record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization across systems, and audit compliance.

Primary shift pattern or schedule type supported at this work location. Used by Kronos Workforce Ready for scheduling, overtime rule application, and shift differential pay calculation.

Timestamp when this work location record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Indicates whether this submittal is for a backfill position — replacing a worker who has left or been removed from an assignment. Backfill submittals are tracked separately for workforce planning and client relationship analytics.

Indicates whether the submitted candidate is being redeployed from a previous or ending assignment within the same client or another client. Redeployment tracking is a key metric for bench utilization and workforce retention.

Free-text notes entered by the recruiter at the time of submittal. May include candidate highlights, match rationale, availability details, or special considerations communicated to the client contact.
The date on which the candidate formally accepted the offer for this submittal. Marks the conversion of a submittal to a placement. Used for submittal-to-hire ratio and TTS calculations.

The date on which a formal employment or engagement offer was extended to the candidate following a successful interview. Used for offer-to-acceptance cycle time measurement and TTF milestone tracking.

Classification of the intended engagement type for this submittal. Distinguishes between temporary staffing, contract-to-hire (CTH), direct placement, temp-to-perm, and Statement of Work (SOW) engagements. Drives billing, compliance, and conversion fee logic.

The hourly or periodic bill rate proposed to the client at the time of submittal. Represents the rate charged to the client for the candidates services. Used in spread and gross margin calculations. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

The hourly or periodic pay rate proposed for the candidate at the time of submittal. Represents the compensation offered to the worker. Used in spread and gross margin calculations. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
Free-text notes accompanying the QoS rating, providing qualitative context on why the candidate was rated at that quality level. May include match strengths, gaps, or client-specific considerations.

Numeric quality score assigned to this submittal reflecting how well the candidate profile matches the job order requirements. Scored on a defined scale (e.g., 1.0–5.0). Used as a KPI for recruiter performance, submittal quality benchmarking, and client satisfaction analysis.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the proposed pay rate and bill rate on this submittal (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Indicates the pay and bill rate period basis for this submittal (e.g., hourly, daily, weekly, annual, per diem). Determines how proposed_pay_rate and proposed_bill_rate are interpreted for compensation and billing calculations.

Standardized code indicating the primary reason a submittal was rejected by the client or withdrawn by the candidate. Used for pipeline analytics, fall-off rate analysis, and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: overqualified|underqualified|rate_mismatch|availability|culture_fit|position_filled|no_response|candidate_withdrew — promote to reference product]

The date on which the candidate granted Right to Represent (RTR) authorization to the staffing firm for this submittal. Used for compliance tracking and dispute resolution in cases of duplicate representation claims.

Indicates whether the candidate has granted the staffing firm the Right to Represent (RTR) them for this specific job order and client. A True value confirms the candidate has authorized the submittal, preventing duplicate representation conflicts.

The channel or method through which the candidate provided Right to Represent (RTR) consent (e.g., email, phone, text, verbal, self-service portal). Supports compliance documentation and audit trail for RTR disputes.

description: The date by which the client is contractually required to respond to this submittal per the Master Service Agreement (MSA) or VMS program SLA. Used to flag overdue submittals and measure client SLA compliance.

The date and time the candidate profile was formally submitted to the client contact for the specified job order. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp. Used as the start point for Time-to-Fill (TTF) and Time-to-Start (TTS) KPI calculations. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Human-readable, externally-visible business identifier for this submittal (e.g., SUB-2024-00123). Used in client communications, ATS references, and operational reporting. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

The channel or method through which the candidate submittal was delivered to the client (e.g., ATS portal, VMS portal, email, phone, direct). Used for channel effectiveness analysis and process standardization.

Current workflow state of the candidate submittal in the recruitment pipeline. Tracks progression from initial submission through client review to hiring decision. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Timestamp when this submittal record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the submittal record for audit and change management purposes. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
