Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: In RPO/MSP programs, recruiter assignments are governed by the managed program contract (SLAs, fee structures, routing rules, markup caps). Program-level recruiter performance reporting and SLA compli
Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) that this recruiter is assigned to fill. Central anchor of the recruiter assignment — every assignment must be tied to an open job order.

Reference to a candidate from the bench (unassigned workers) being considered for redeployment into this job order. Supports redeployment-first sourcing strategies that reduce time-to-fill and improve bench utilization.

Foreign key linking to client.program_supplier. Business justification: The program_supplier record governs submission limits, routing order, markup caps, and compliance status for a supplier within a program. Recruiter assignments must reference the program_supplier reco

Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Recruiters are assigned to fill specific SOW positions with defined scope, timelines, and headcount targets. Real business process: workload allocation, SOW delivery accountability, and client commitm

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Recruiter assignments in hybrid delivery models where internal recruiters manage supplier relationships per requisition. Tracks supplier coordination responsibility, enables supplier-recruiter perform

Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Recruiter assignments to supplier-sourced requisitions are governed by the vendor agreement defining exclusivity, markup caps, and performance tiers. Operations and procurement teams require this link

Foreign key linking to vendor.vms_enrollment. Business justification: Recruiter assignments for VMS programs require the supplier to have active enrollment; enrollment record defines portal access, approved categories, and compliance status that govern the recruiter's s

Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: In VMS programs, recruiter assignments are governed by VMS routing rules, submission limits per req, and time-to-submit SLAs. VMS program performance reporting and supplier scorecard generation requir
The actual date on which the job order was filled (placement confirmed). Used to calculate realized Time-to-Fill (TTF) against the target and to measure recruiter performance against SLA commitments.

The actual number of candidate submittals delivered by the recruiter against this job order as of the current date. Compared against target_submittal_count to measure QoS performance and recruiter productivity.
The date on which the recruiting manager formally approved this recruiter assignment. Supports governance, SLA start-date anchoring, and audit trail for assignment authorization.

The calendar date on which the recruiter was formally assigned to the job order or requisition. Used as the start anchor for Time-to-Fill (TTF) and Time-to-Start (TTS) KPI calculations and workload reporting.
The date on which the recruiters active responsibility for the job order ended, either through placement, reassignment, job order closure, or cancellation. Nullable for currently active assignments.

Current lifecycle state of the recruiter assignment. active = recruiter is actively working the req; reassigned = ownership transferred to another recruiter; closed = job order filled or cancelled; on_hold = req paused by client; cancelled = assignment voided before any activity.

Classifies the nature of the recruiters role on this job order. primary = sole owner; backup = secondary coverage; split_desk = shared fee arrangement between two recruiters; rpo_team = Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) team member; msp_coordinator = Managed Service Provider (MSP) program coordinator; overflow = temporary capacity relief assignment.

Indicates whether a backfill assignment is required because the original placed candidate left the role prematurely (fall-off). When True, triggers a new recruiter assignment workflow for the same job order.

The exact date and time when this recruiter assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Indicates the staffing delivery model under which this recruiter assignment operates. direct = standard direct staffing; rpo = Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) engagement; msp = Managed Service Provider (MSP) program; hybrid = combination of RPO and direct delivery.

Indicates whether this assignment has been flagged as at risk of a fall-off (placement cancellation after acceptance). Triggered by early warning signals such as candidate counter-offer, client budget freeze, or extended start date delay. Supports proactive retention and backfill planning.

The ratio of client interviews conducted to offers extended for this assignment. Measures the quality of candidates advanced to interview stage and the recruiters ability to match client expectations. Populated at assignment closure.

Indicates whether this recruiter has exclusive ownership of the job order (True) or whether the req is being worked by multiple recruiters simultaneously (False). Exclusive assignments typically carry higher accountability and commission expectations.

Flags whether this recruiter assignment is part of a formal Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) engagement where the staffing firm is acting as an embedded talent acquisition function for the client. Drives RPO-specific reporting, billing, and SLA tracking.

Free-text field for recruiter or manager notes related to this assignment, such as special client instructions, candidate pipeline status, sourcing challenges, or escalation context. Supports operational communication and audit trail.

A composite numeric score (0.00–100.00) evaluating the recruiters performance on this specific assignment, incorporating QoS (Quality of Submission), TTF adherence, submittal-to-hire ratio, and client satisfaction. Populated post-closure for performance reviews and compensation decisions.

Business priority classification for this recruiter assignment, reflecting urgency of the job order fill. critical = immediate fill required (e.g., client escalation, compliance risk); high = expedited fill; medium = standard SLA; low = flexible timeline. Drives recruiter workload prioritization.

The date on which the recruiter assignment was transferred from one recruiter to another. Used to measure the impact of reassignments on TTF and to track assignment continuity for client relationship management.

Free-text or coded explanation for why this recruiter assignment was transferred to a different recruiter. Common reasons include recruiter departure, workload rebalancing, specialization mismatch, client request, or performance issue. Populated only when assignment_status = reassigned.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Time-to-Fill (TTF) Target Days
description: The contractually or operationally agreed number of calendar days within which the recruiter must fill the job order from the assignment date. Drives TTF KPI measurement and client SLA compliance reporting.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this recruiter assignment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

The native identifier of this record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn assignment ID, Beeline req ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability from the Silver Layer back to the system of record.

The primary candidate sourcing approach the recruiter is employing for this assignment. Supports sourcing effectiveness analytics and cost-per-hire analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|social_media|referral|database_search|headhunting|campus|redeployment|other — promote to reference product]

For split-desk assignments, the percentage of the placement fee or gross margin allocated to this recruiter. Enables accurate commission calculation and split-fee arrangement tracking. Null for non-split assignments. Range: 0.00–100.00.

The ratio of candidate submittals to confirmed hires for this assignment (e.g., 5.00 means 5 submittals per hire). A key QoS and recruiter efficiency metric. Populated at assignment closure. Lower ratios indicate higher submittal quality.

The target date by which the recruiter is expected to fill the job order with a placed candidate. Drives Time-to-Fill (TTF) SLA tracking and recruiter accountability. Set at assignment creation based on client SLA or internal KPI targets.

The planned number of qualified candidate submittals the recruiter is expected to deliver for this job order. Used for workload planning, Quality of Submission (QoS) benchmarking, and SLA compliance tracking against client expectations.

The exact date and time when this recruiter assignment record was most recently modified. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The requisition (Req) number assigned by the clients Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for MSP-managed programs. Required for VMS-driven assignments to correlate internal Bullhorn records with external VMS tracking and rate management.

A numeric weight (e.g., 0.50–3.00) representing the relative complexity or effort burden of this assignment compared to a standard job order. Used in workload balancing algorithms to ensure equitable distribution across the recruiting desk. Higher values indicate more complex or time-intensive requisitions.

Unique surrogate identifier for each candidate pipeline record within a requisition. Primary key for the req_pipeline data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete recruitment pipeline event with a full lifecycle from sourcing through hire or rejection.

Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: Pipeline tracking includes BGC status as a stage gate. Links pipeline record to background check in progress, enabling bottleneck analysis and SLA monitoring. Essential for visibility into credentiali
Reference to the hiring manager or client contact associated with this pipeline record. Used for interview scheduling, hiring decision tracking, and client visibility dashboards.

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Pipeline stages track credential readiness as candidates progress. Links candidates pipeline position to their credential status, supporting readiness reporting and identifying credentialing bottlene

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Pipeline stage management includes credential compliance gates that block stage advancement. Recruiters and ops managers track whether pipeline candidates meet the credential_requirement before advanc
Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) that this pipeline record belongs to. Links the candidate progression to the specific open position being filled.

Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Compliance clearance is a pipeline gate — candidates cannot advance to offer/placement without BGC and credential clearance. The req_pipeline tracks pipeline_stage and pipeline_status but has no FK to

Foreign key linking to joborder.position_requirement. Business justification: Pipeline stage tracking is per headcount slot (position_requirement), not just per order. TTF analytics, bottleneck identification, and workforce planning dashboards require pipeline records linked to
Reference to the candidate whose progression through the recruitment pipeline is being tracked. Core party reference linking pipeline activity to the candidate profile.

Foreign key linking to recruitment.recruiter_assignment. Business justification: The req_pipeline tracks real-time candidate progression for a specific job order. The recruiter_assignment defines who is accountable for filling that job order. Linking req_pipeline to recruiter_assi

Foreign key linking to joborder.sla_commitment. Business justification: Pipeline stage timestamps (stage_entry_timestamp, stage_exit_timestamp, stage_duration_business_days) are measured against SLA commitments (submittal_sla_hours, ttf_target_days). MSP SLA breach detect

Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: req_pipeline tracks stage_duration_business_days, is_bottleneck_flag, and is_aging_alert — all SLA-governed pipeline metrics. Linking to the governing SLA enables real-time pipeline SLA compliance das

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Pipeline tracking by supplier enables VMS program management: allocation enforcement, supplier capacity monitoring, bottleneck identification, and stage-level performance analysis. Essential for MSP o

Foreign key linking to vendor.tier_classification. Business justification: MSP pipeline management routes requisitions by supplier tier; tier_classification governs submission_priority_order and SLA targets per pipeline stage. Reporting on pipeline velocity by tier requires
Native submission or candidate-job record identifier from the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system. Used for data lineage, system reconciliation, and cross-referencing pipeline records with the source system.
Structured or free-text feedback provided by the client or hiring manager following a submittal or interview. Used for QoS improvement, recruiter coaching, and submittal-to-hire ratio analysis.
Timestamp when this pipeline record was first created in the system, typically triggered by the initial sourcing or submittal event in Bullhorn ATS. Audit trail field for data lineage.

Recruitment delivery model under which this pipeline record is managed. Indicates whether the requisition is fulfilled through direct staffing, Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO), Managed Service Provider (MSP), or Vendor Management System (VMS) channel.
Anticipated start date for the candidate if hired, as agreed between the client and the staffing firm. Used for time-to-start (TTS) forecasting and workforce scheduling.
Date the candidate was formally hired or selected for the position. Terminal milestone used to calculate time-to-fill (TTF) and submittal-to-hire ratio KPIs.
Date and time the interview was completed. Distinct from the scheduled timestamp; used to measure interview-to-decision cycle time and pipeline velocity.
Date and time the interview was scheduled with the client or hiring manager. Used for interview scheduling management and pipeline stage duration calculations.
Type of interview conducted or scheduled for the candidate at the interviewing stage of the pipeline. Used for interview scheduling management and hiring manager visibility dashboards.

Indicates whether this pipeline record has exceeded the expected stage duration threshold and triggered an aging alert. Used by recruiters and hiring managers to prioritize action on stalled candidates.

Indicates whether this pipeline record has been flagged as a bottleneck based on stage conversion rate analysis. Used for pipeline health monitoring and TTF forecasting in RPO and MSP delivery models.

Indicates whether this pipeline record has been marked as a priority by the recruiter or account manager, typically for urgent requisitions or high-value client accounts. Drives recruiter workload prioritization.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this pipeline record, refreshed on each stage transition event to maintain current-state accuracy in the Silver Layer.
Date the candidate formally accepted the offer. Used to calculate offer acceptance rate and time-to-start (TTS) metrics.
Bill rate (per hour or per annum) associated with the offer for this pipeline record. Used to calculate spread (bill rate minus pay rate) and gross margin at the pipeline stage.
Date a formal offer was extended to the candidate. Marks the entry into the offered pipeline stage and is used to calculate offer-to-acceptance cycle time.
Pay rate (per hour or per annum) offered to the candidate at the offer stage. Used for offer competitiveness analysis and spread/markup calculations. Confidential business data.

Free-text notes entered by the recruiter or account manager regarding the candidates progression, client feedback, or special circumstances for this pipeline record. Supports recruiter workload management and hiring manager communication.

Externally-known business identifier for this pipeline record, typically sourced from Bullhorn ATS (e.g., SUB-2024-00123). Used in VMS scorecards, recruiter dashboards, and RPO/MSP reporting to uniquely reference a candidate-to-requisition progression record.

Current stage of the candidate within the recruitment pipeline for this requisition. Drives stage conversion rate analytics, bottleneck detection, and time-to-fill (TTF) forecasting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sourced|screened|submitted|interviewing|offered|hired|rejected|withdrawn — promote to reference product]

Lifecycle status of this pipeline record indicating whether the candidates progression is actively being managed, placed on hold, closed due to hire or rejection, or archived for historical reference.

Type of placement being pursued for this pipeline record. Distinguishes between temporary staffing, contract-to-hire (temp-to-perm), and direct/permanent placement, which affects billing, compliance, and conversion fee calculations.

The pipeline stage immediately preceding the current stage for this record. Enables stage transition analysis and supports pipeline velocity reporting without requiring a full history table join. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sourced|screened|submitted|interviewing|offered|hired|rejected|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Quality of Submission (QoS) score assigned to this pipeline record, reflecting how well the submitted candidate matches the job order requirements. Used in VMS scorecard reporting and recruiter performance management.
Basis on which the pay rate and bill rate are expressed for this pipeline record (e.g., hourly, annual, daily, per diem). Required for accurate spread and gross margin calculations.

Reason code or description explaining why a candidate was rejected or withdrew from the pipeline. Used for fall-off rate analysis, QoS (Quality of Submission) improvement, and EEOC adverse action tracking. Null if candidate has not been rejected or withdrawn.

Pipeline stage at which the candidate was rejected or withdrew. Enables stage-level fall-off rate analysis and identification of pipeline bottlenecks. Null if candidate has not been rejected or withdrawn. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sourced|screened|submitted|interviewing|offered|hired|rejected|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date the Right to Represent (RTR) authorization was confirmed by the candidate. Used for compliance auditing and to ensure submittals are made only with valid RTR.

Status of the Right to Represent (RTR) authorization obtained from the candidate before submittal to the client. Tracks whether RTR is pending, confirmed, expired, or not required. Critical for compliance with co-employment and exclusive representation rules.
Date the candidate was first identified and added to the pipeline for this requisition. Marks the start of the recruitment lifecycle for TTF and TTS (Time to Start) calculations.

Channel or method through which the candidate was originally sourced for this requisition (e.g., job board, employee referral, direct sourcing, VMS portal). Used for sourcing effectiveness analytics and RPO campaign performance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|referral|direct_source|social_media|agency|internal_database|vms_portal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Number of business days the candidate has spent or spent in the current pipeline stage. Calculated at each stage transition event refresh. Used for bottleneck identification and SLA compliance monitoring.
Date and time when the candidate entered the current pipeline stage. Used to calculate stage duration in business days and identify aging alerts for time-to-fill (TTF) forecasting.
Date and time when the candidate exited the current pipeline stage and transitioned to the next stage or a terminal state. Null if the candidate is still in the current stage.
Total number of stage transitions this pipeline record has undergone since creation. Used to identify candidates who have been recycled through stages or experienced unusual pipeline paths.

Classification of the worker engagement type for this pipeline record. Distinguishes between W-2 employees, 1099 independent contractors (IC), corp-to-corp arrangements, and full-time equivalents (FTE). Critical for IRS worker classification compliance and burden rate calculations.
Unique system-generated identifier for each absence record. Primary key for the absence_record data product within the timesheet domain.
Identifier of the active placement or assignment during which the absence occurred. Used to associate the absence with the specific client engagement and job order.
Identifier of the client organization at whose worksite the worker was assigned when the absence occurred. Used for client SLA compliance tracking and notification workflows.

Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: absence_record has client_notified and client_notification_timestamp attributes indicating a specific client contact notification process. Staffing coordinators must notify the designated client conta

Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Absences impact client site staffing levels and trigger replacement workflows; site managers need absence visibility for scheduling. Essential for client site headcount management and replacement requ

Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: An absence record falls within a specific pay period for payroll processing purposes. The absence_record currently stores pay_period_start_date and pay_period_end_date as denormalized fields. Adding p

Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_run. Business justification: absence_record has payroll_processed flag but no FK to the pay_run that processed the PTO/FMLA payment. Linking absence_record.pay_run_id → payroll.pay_run enables payroll auditors to trace which pay
Identifier of the contingent worker associated with this absence record. Links to the worker master record in the workforce management system.

Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: FMLA, ADA, and state leave compliance requirements mandate specific documentation, notification timelines, and certification processes for absences. Compliance officers tracking leave requirement fulf

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Absences for SOW workers impact deliverable timelines and engagement billing. Program managers need absence_record→SOW engagement linkage to assess schedule risk, notify clients, and determine if enga

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: State leave laws (FMLA, CFRA, PFML, state-specific paid leave) are modeled in state_compliance_rule. Absence records must reference the governing state rule to enforce documentation deadlines, notific
Identifier of the timesheet record for the pay period in which this absence falls. Links the absence to the authoritative hours record for payroll and billing.

Total calendar duration of the absence expressed in days (may be fractional for partial-day absences). Derived from absence_start_date and absence_end_date but stored for reporting efficiency and workforce availability analytics.
The last calendar date of the absence period. Null for open-ended absences (e.g., ongoing FMLA leave). Combined with absence_start_date to determine total absence duration.
Total number of scheduled work hours missed during the absence period. Used for payroll deduction calculations, PTO balance consumption, and workforce availability reporting.

Free-text notes entered by the worker, manager, or HR coordinator providing additional context for the absence (e.g., nature of illness, family emergency details). Classified as confidential due to potential sensitivity of leave-related information.
Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this absence record (e.g., ABS-2024-00123). Used in worker communications, manager approvals, and leave management correspondence.
The first calendar date of the absence period. Used to calculate absence duration, determine pay period impact, and trigger FMLA tracking windows.

Classification of the absence by leave category as defined in the leave management policy. Drives payroll treatment (paid vs. unpaid), compliance recordkeeping, and workforce availability reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PTO|Sick Leave|FMLA|Holiday|Jury Duty|Bereavement|Unexcused|Personal|Military|Workers Comp — promote to reference product]

Current workflow state of the absence request through the manager approval process. Drives payroll processing eligibility and client notification triggers. Pending = submitted awaiting review; Approved = manager authorized; Denied = rejected; Cancelled = worker withdrew; Under Review = escalated.
Name or system identifier of the manager, supervisor, or automated workflow that approved or denied the absence request. Supports audit trail and accountability for leave decisions.
Date and time when the absence request was approved or denied by the authorized approver. Null if still pending. Used for approval workflow SLA tracking and audit trail.
Date and time when the client was notified of the worker absence. Null if client_notified is False. Used to measure notification SLA compliance against MSA contractual obligations.

Indicates whether the client organization has been notified of this worker absence (True) or not (False). Critical for client SLA compliance tracking — unnotified absences may breach MSA notification obligations.
Date and time when this absence record was first created in the system. Establishes the audit trail start point and is used for data lineage tracking in the Silver layer lakehouse.

Deadline by which the worker must submit required leave documentation. Under FMLA, employers may require documentation within 15 calendar days of the leave request. Drives compliance follow-up workflows.

Indicates whether required supporting documentation (e.g., physician certification for FMLA, jury summons, death certificate for bereavement) has been received and verified (True) or is outstanding (False).

business_glossary_term: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) Hours Used
description: Number of hours counted against the workers annual FMLA entitlement (up to 480 hours / 12 weeks per year). Populated only when fmla_qualifying is True. Required for DOL FMLA recordkeeping compliance.

business_glossary_term: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) Qualifying Indicator
description: Indicates whether this absence qualifies under the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) (True) or not (False). Triggers FMLA tracking, 3-year recordkeeping retention, and DOL compliance workflows.

Indicates whether the absence is compensated (True) or unpaid (False). Determines payroll treatment: paid absences draw from PTO/sick balances or holiday pay; unpaid absences result in payroll deductions.

Indicates whether the absence covers only part of a scheduled workday (True) rather than full days (False). Partial-day absences require hour-level tracking for accurate payroll deduction and FMLA intermittent leave accounting.

Source system identifier for this absence record as assigned by the Kronos Workforce Ready leave management module. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and integration with the Silver layer from the operational system of record.

Standardized reason code from the Kronos leave management module identifying the specific reason for the absence (e.g., FMLA-SELF, FMLA-FAMILY, PSL-CA, JURY, BRVMT-IMM). Supports regulatory reporting and state-specific paid sick leave law compliance.

Indicates whether this absence record has been included in a completed payroll processing run (True) or is still pending payroll (False). Prevents double-processing and confirms the absence has been reflected in worker compensation.

Indicates whether the workers per diem allowance is affected by this absence (True) or not (False). Per diem payments may be suspended or prorated for absent days depending on the assignment contract terms.

business_glossary_term: Paid Time Off (PTO) Balance Before Absence
description: Workers available PTO balance in hours immediately prior to this absence being applied. Provides a point-in-time snapshot for audit, dispute resolution, and worker self-service reporting.
Number of accrued Paid Time Off (PTO) hours deducted from the workers PTO balance to cover this absence. Zero for unpaid absences or absences covered by other leave types (e.g., FMLA, holiday pay).

Indicates whether a replacement worker has been requested to cover this absence (True) or not (False). Triggers workforce scheduling and dispatch workflows to source a backfill worker for the client site.
Total number of hours the worker was originally scheduled to work during the absence period. Provides the baseline against which absence_hours is measured for partial-day absence tracking.

Indicates whether this absence has caused or is at risk of causing a breach of the client SLA (True) — for example, if the absence results in insufficient staffing levels below the contracted headcount threshold. Feeds client SLA compliance reporting.

Code identifying the applicable state-specific paid sick leave or family leave law governing this absence (e.g., CA-HWHFA for California Healthy Workplaces Healthy Families Act, NY-PFL for New York Paid Family Leave). Ensures compliance with state-level leave mandates beyond federal FMLA.

Date and time when the worker or manager submitted the absence request in Kronos Workforce Ready. Establishes the start of the approval workflow and is used for SLA measurement of approval turnaround time.

Indicates whether this absence has triggered an assignment termination workflow (True) in Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Applicable for contingent workers with unexcused absences that breach the no-call/no-show policy threshold.

Indicates whether this absence has been classified as unexcused (True) — i.e., the worker did not provide adequate notice or documentation. Unexcused absences for contingent workers may trigger assignment termination workflows per staffing agency policy.

Date and time when this absence record was last modified. Tracks changes to approval status, documentation receipt, payroll processing, and other lifecycle updates for audit and data quality purposes.

Unique system-generated identifier for each timesheet adjustment record. Serves as the primary key for the timesheet_adjustment entity. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — canonical minimum categories enforced.

Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_stub. Business justification: Timesheet adjustments often trigger pay stub corrections or reissues. Tracking which specific pay stub was affected (vs. just the pay_run) is essential for audit trails, worker communication about cor

Foreign key linking to contract.amendment. Business justification: Retroactive billing and payroll adjustments are frequently triggered by contract amendments (rate changes, scope modifications). Linking adjustments to the causative amendment is required for financia

Foreign key linking to timesheet.approved_hours_summary. Business justification: An adjustment is a post-submission correction to an approved timesheet, and the approved_hours_summary is the aggregated approved record that the adjustment amends. Linking adjustment directly to appr
Reference to the active placement or assignment under which the original timesheet was submitted. Links the adjustment to the specific client engagement, job order, and pay/bill rate agreement.
Identifier of the client organization associated with the placement. Required for client billing reconciliation and invoice adjustment when billable hours are corrected.

Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Rate adjustments reference the applicable rate schedule; retroactive rate changes must cite the amended rate schedule for audit trail and billing correction. Real business process: rate correction and

Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: Timesheet adjustments that reduce billable hours/amounts result in credit memos. Linking adjustment to credit_memo enables the adjustment-to-credit-memo audit trail that staffing AR teams use for bill

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_dispute. Business justification: Timesheet adjustments are frequently triggered by billing disputes. Linking adjustment directly to the causative billing_dispute enables dispute-driven adjustment tracking — a named staffing billing o

Foreign key linking to payroll.earnings_line. Business justification: Payroll adjustments correct specific earnings lines. Linking adjustment.earnings_line_id → payroll.earnings_line enables auditors to trace which earnings line was corrected by a retroactive adjustment

Reference to the client invoice or credit memo generated as a result of this billing adjustment. Links the timesheet correction to the corresponding accounts receivable transaction in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.

Reference to the payroll run in which this adjustment was processed. Enables reconciliation between timesheet adjustments and payroll output records. Null until the adjustment is applied to payroll.

Identifier of the contingent worker whose timesheet is being adjusted. Enables worker-level payroll reconciliation, compliance reporting, and dispute resolution. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE canonical category.

Foreign key linking to joborder.rate_override. Business justification: Retroactive billing and payroll adjustments are frequently triggered by approved rate overrides. Staffing billing teams must link each retroactive adjustment to the specific rate_override that authori

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: Timesheet adjustments may be triggered by wage-hour violations discovered in DOL/state audits (unpaid OT, misclassified hours). Real business process: remediation tracking linking adjustments to viola

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Billing and pay adjustments for SOW workers must reference the SOW engagement for correct cost allocation and invoice correction. Finance teams require adjustment→SOW engagement linkage to reconcile S

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Billing and payroll adjustments in MSP programs must be attributed to the supplying vendor for vendor dispute resolution, chargeback processing, and supplier billing reconciliation. Without this FK, a

Reference to the original approved timesheet record that this adjustment corrects or amends. Core linkage enabling full audit trail from original submission to corrected values. Maps to the PARTY_REFERENCE canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the adjustment (e.g., ADJ-2024-000123). Used in dispute resolution, payroll reconciliation, and client billing communications. Maps to the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Current workflow lifecycle state of the adjustment record. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval and application to payroll and billing. Maps to the LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Categorical classification of the nature of the adjustment. Drives downstream payroll and billing processing logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours_correction|rate_correction|pay_type_reclassification|retroactive_change|break_correction|absence_correction — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]

Indicates whether this timesheet adjustment impacts client billable hours or bill rates, triggering a credit memo or supplemental invoice. False for adjustments that only affect worker pay (e.g., internal pay type reclassifications with no billing impact).

Indicates whether this timesheet adjustment impacts worker payable hours or pay rates, requiring a payroll correction or supplemental pay run. False for adjustments that only affect billing (e.g., bill rate corrections with no pay rate change).

The date and time when the approved adjustment was applied to client billing in Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Null until billing is updated. Confirms the client invoice or credit memo has been generated for the adjustment.

The date and time when the approved adjustment was applied to the payroll run in TempWorks Payroll. Null until the adjustment is processed. Confirms the adjustment has been actioned in the payroll system of record.

Indicates the approval tier required for this adjustment based on the adjustment type, dollar impact, or client contract requirements. Single-level approvals are handled internally; dual or client-required approvals involve additional stakeholders.
The date and time when the timesheet adjustment was approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved. Used for approval cycle time analytics and SLA compliance reporting.

The corrected gross client billing amount (in USD) after the adjustment. Drives credit memo issuance or supplemental invoice generation in Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.
The gross client billing amount (in USD) calculated from the original timesheet hours and bill rate before adjustment. Baseline for client invoice reconciliation and credit memo calculation.
The corrected client bill rate (per hour) in USD after the adjustment. Used when a rate correction changes the amount invoiced to the client. Drives credit memo or supplemental invoice generation.

The client bill rate (per hour) recorded on the original timesheet, in USD. Preserved for billing reconciliation when rate corrections affect client invoices. Represents the rate charged to the client before adjustment.

Indicates whether the client has formally approved the timesheet adjustment, required when the adjustment affects billable hours or bill rates under the clients Master Service Agreement (MSA). True when client sign-off is obtained.

The date and time when this timesheet adjustment record was first created in the system. Maps to the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage and audit trail.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this adjustment record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.

The corrected number of hours after the adjustment is applied. Replaces the original hours value for payroll processing and client billing. The difference between hours_adjusted and hours_original represents the net hours change.

The number of hours recorded on the original approved timesheet for the work date and pay type being corrected. Preserved as the pre-adjustment baseline for audit, dispute resolution, and reconciliation.

Indicates whether this adjustment applies to a prior pay period that has already been processed in payroll and/or billing. Retroactive adjustments require special handling in payroll tax recalculation (FICA, FUTA, SUTA) and may trigger amended invoices.

The corrected gross worker pay amount (in USD) after the adjustment is applied. Represents the authoritative payable amount for the adjusted period. The difference from pay_amount_original drives payroll debit or credit entries. Maps to MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.

The gross worker pay amount (in USD) calculated from the original timesheet hours and pay rate before adjustment. Serves as the financial baseline for payroll reconciliation and adjustment impact analysis.

The corrected worker pay rate (per hour) in USD after the adjustment. Used for rate correction and retroactive change adjustment types. Drives recalculation of worker gross pay for the affected period.
The worker pay rate (per hour) recorded on the original timesheet, in USD. Preserved for rate correction adjustments and retroactive pay rate changes. Sensitive compensation data.

The corrected pay type code after adjustment. Used when a pay type reclassification adjustment is applied (e.g., regular hours reclassified as overtime (OT) or double-time (DT)). Drives recalculation of worker pay and client bill amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|overtime|double_time|per_diem|pto|holiday|on_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The pay type code recorded on the original timesheet before adjustment (e.g., regular, overtime (OT), double-time (DT), per diem, paid time off (PTO), holiday, on-call). Preserved for full audit trail and reclassification tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|overtime|double_time|per_diem|pto|holiday|on_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Standardized reason code categorizing why the timesheet adjustment was initiated. Supports root-cause analysis, compliance reporting, and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_entry_error|system_error|missed_punch|rate_change|ot_reclassification|client_dispute|worker_dispute|manager_correction — promote to reference product]

Free-text narrative explanation provided by the requestor describing the specific circumstances requiring the timesheet adjustment. Supplements the reason code with contextual detail for audit and dispute resolution.
The business role of the individual who submitted the adjustment request. Provides context for approval routing and compliance audit without requiring a join to the user table.

Identifies the operational system of record that originated or triggered the timesheet adjustment (e.g., Kronos Workforce Ready, Bullhorn, Beeline VMS, TempWorks, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation across integrated systems.

The date and time when the timesheet adjustment request was formally submitted into the workflow system. Maps to the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for SLA tracking and audit trail.

The date and time when this timesheet adjustment record was last modified. Maps to the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks all changes for compliance and audit purposes.
The specific calendar date on which the hours being adjusted were originally worked. Enables day-level audit trail and supports overtime calculation corrections at the daily level.

Unique system-generated identifier for each timesheet approval workflow record. Serves as the primary key for this entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — tracks the multi-step approval lifecycle for a single timesheet submission.
Reference to the worker placement or assignment associated with this timesheet approval workflow. Ties the approval to the specific contingent worker assignment and client engagement.
Reference to the client organization for whom the contingent worker performed services. Used to route approval to the correct client contact and enforce client-specific SLA rules.

Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Client-side timesheet approvers are contacts; approval routing, SLA tracking, and audit trails require knowing which client contact approved hours. Critical for dispute resolution and compliance audit

Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Timesheet approval workflow completion triggers billing release (billing_released_flag exists on this product). Linking to the resulting invoice enables billing release audit trails — staffing billing

Reference to the payroll pay period to which the approved timesheet hours will be applied. Links the approval workflow to downstream payroll processing and ensures hours are captured in the correct pay cycle.

Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_run. Business justification: timesheet_approval_workflow has payroll_released_flag indicating when approval triggers payroll. Linking timesheet_approval_workflow.pay_run_id → payroll.pay_run enables tracing which approval workflo
Reference to the contingent worker (candidate) who submitted the timesheet being reviewed in this approval workflow. Supports worker-level audit trail and dispute resolution.

Foreign key linking to joborder.sla_commitment. Business justification: MSP/VMS programs measure timesheet approval cycle times against SLA commitments. timesheet_approval_workflow tracks sla_breach_minutes and sla_compliant_flag but has no FK to the governing sla_commitm

Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Timesheet approval workflows are measured against contractual SLAs (approval turnaround time, submission deadlines). The workflow already tracks sla_breach_minutes and sla_compliant_flag but has no FK

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Timesheet approval workflows for SOW workers must route to the correct SOW engagement approver, who may differ from standard assignment supervisors. MSP programs require this link to enforce SOW-speci

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Vendor SLA compliance reporting requires measuring approval turnaround time per supplier. Linking approval workflows to the supplying vendor enables SLA breach analysis by vendor, a standard KPI in MS
Reference to the parent timesheet record for which this approval workflow instance was initiated. Links the approval workflow to the specific worker time submission being reviewed.

Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: vms_program.timesheet_approval_cycle governs the approval workflow rules for VMS-managed programs. Linking timesheet_approval_workflow to vms_program enables VMS-driven approval routing, SLA complianc

The exact date and time when the approver performed the approval action at this workflow stage. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Critical for SLA compliance measurement and audit trail.

The specific action taken by the approver at this workflow stage. Distinct from approval_status in that it captures the discrete action event (e.g., delegated) rather than the resulting state. Used for audit trail and workflow analytics.
The interface or channel through which the approver submitted their approval action. Supports channel adoption analytics and helps identify approval bottlenecks by channel type.

The specific step in the multi-stage approval workflow at which this record applies. Stages include worker submission, supervisor review, client approval, back-office audit, and payroll release. Each stage represents a distinct checkpoint in the timesheet lifecycle.

Current lifecycle state of this approval workflow stage. Indicates whether the stage is awaiting action (pending), has been approved, rejected, escalated to a higher authority, auto-approved per system rules, or recalled by the submitter.

The functional role of the approver at this workflow stage. Distinguishes between worker self-submission, direct supervisor review, client manager approval, back-office audit, payroll admin release, and MSP coordinator oversight.

Identifier of the system-defined auto-approval rule that triggered automatic approval of this timesheet stage without manual intervention. Populated only when approval_action is auto_approved. Enables audit of automated decisions.

The hourly rate at which the client is billed for the workers time as defined in the client contract or VMS program. Used to validate that approved hours will generate the correct client invoice amount.

Indicates whether the approved timesheet hours have been released to the client billing system (Salesforce Revenue Cloud) for invoice generation. Enables reconciliation between approved hours and billed hours.
The date and time when this approval workflow record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and SOC 2 compliance purposes.

Indicates whether this timesheet approval workflow record is associated with a formal dispute raised by the worker, client, or staffing agency. When True, the record is flagged for dispute resolution review and may be excluded from standard payroll processing until resolved.

The case or ticket number associated with a formal timesheet dispute. Populated when dispute_flag is True. Used to link the approval workflow record to the dispute management process for resolution tracking.

Free-text or coded explanation for why this approval stage was escalated to a higher authority. Populated when approval_action is escalated. Supports escalation pattern analysis and workflow optimization.

Indicates whether this workflow record represents the final, fully-approved state of the timesheet across all required approval stages. When True, the timesheet is cleared for payroll processing and client billing. Enables efficient filtering for downstream payroll and billing feeds.

The source system reference identifier from Kronos Workforce Ready for the timesheet record associated with this approval workflow. Supports data lineage tracing and reconciliation with the system of record.
The hourly rate at which the contingent worker is compensated for approved hours. Used to validate payroll calculations downstream. The spread (bill_rate minus pay_rate) represents gross margin.

Indicates whether the approved timesheet hours have been released to the payroll processing system (TempWorks Payroll) for worker compensation. Distinct from final_approved_flag as it confirms the downstream handoff has occurred.

The total per diem dollar amount claimed by the worker on this timesheet. Populated when per_diem_claimed is True. Subject to IRS per diem rate limits and client contract terms. Feeds into billing and payroll calculations.

Indicates whether the worker claimed a per diem allowance (daily expense reimbursement for travel, meals, or lodging) on this timesheet. Per diem claims require separate approval validation against the assignment agreement.

Standardized code indicating the reason the timesheet was rejected at this approval stage. Used for root-cause analysis of rejection patterns and worker coaching. Populated only when approval_action is rejected. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours_discrepancy|missing_entries|unauthorized_overtime|unapproved_per_diem|policy_violation|duplicate_submission|other — promote to reference product]

Free-text narrative provided by the approver explaining the specific reason for rejecting the timesheet at this stage. Supplements the rejection_reason_code with contextual detail for dispute resolution and worker communication.

The number of times the worker has resubmitted this timesheet after a rejection. A high resubmission count indicates recurring errors or disputes and is used for quality-of-submission (QoS) analytics and worker coaching.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Breach Duration Minutes
description: The number of minutes by which the approval action exceeded the SLA deadline. Populated only when sla_compliant_flag is False. Zero or null when SLA was met. Enables severity-based SLA breach analysis and client reporting.

Indicates whether the approval action was completed within the SLA-defined deadline for this stage. True if the action_timestamp is on or before sla_due_timestamp; False if the SLA was breached. Supports SLA compliance reporting and client KPI dashboards.

The contractually or operationally defined deadline by which this approval stage must be completed per the applicable SLA. Derived from the SLA configuration for the client or program. Used to calculate SLA compliance.
Numeric ordering of this approval stage within the overall workflow. Enables reconstruction of the full approval chain in chronological order and supports multi-step workflow analytics.
The date and time when the worker originally submitted the timesheet that triggered this approval workflow. Used as the baseline for SLA elapsed time calculations and Time-to-Approve (TTA) reporting.

Total number of double-time (DT) hours recorded on the timesheet, applicable in jurisdictions or contracts where hours beyond a threshold (e.g., 12 hours/day or 7th consecutive day) are compensated at double the regular rate.

Total number of overtime (OT) hours recorded on the timesheet, typically hours worked beyond 40 per week or 8 per day per FLSA or applicable state law. Requires explicit approval and is a common rejection trigger.
Total number of straight-time (non-overtime) hours recorded on the timesheet being reviewed in this approval workflow. Used to validate hours against scheduled hours and client purchase order limits.
The date and time when this approval workflow record was last modified. Tracks the most recent state change for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data pipeline processing.

The external workflow identifier assigned by the Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this timesheet approval transaction. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the internal approval record and the clients VMS portal.
The last day of the work week covered by the timesheet being approved. Used to align the approval workflow with payroll processing cycles and client billing periods.

Externally-known business identifier for this approval workflow instance, used in communications, dispute resolution, and audit references. Typically formatted as a sequential or alphanumeric code (e.g., WF-2024-00123).
Primary key for approved_hours_summary
Reference to the active placement or assignment under which the worker performed the hours captured in this summary. Ties the hours to a specific job order and client engagement.
Reference to the client (end-customer) for whom the worker performed services during this pay period. Used to route billable amounts to the correct client invoice.

Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Approved hours summaries aggregate billing using rate schedules; invoicing systems pull contracted rates for billing calculations and client invoice generation. Real business process: invoice generati

Foreign key linking to timesheet.direct_deposit_setup. Business justification: The approved_hours_summary drives worker payment, and the direct_deposit_setup contains the banking instructions for that payment. Linking approved_hours_summary to direct_deposit_setup establishes wh

Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) that originated the workers assignment. Enables traceability from approved hours back to the originating staffing request.
Reference to the pay period calendar record that defines the start and end dates of the period covered by this approved hours summary.
Reference to the contingent worker whose approved hours are summarized in this record. Links to the worker master record in the Candidate/Worker domain.

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Approved hours summaries for SOW workers drive milestone billing and SOW budget consumption reporting. Finance teams require approved_hours_summary→SOW engagement linkage to generate SOW invoices and

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Approved hours summaries drive vendor billing and diversity spend reporting. Attributing each summary to the supplying vendor is required for MSP program spend analytics, vendor scorecards, and client
Reference to the source timesheet record from which this approved hours summary was derived. Provides traceability back to the raw time entry data in Kronos Workforce Ready.

Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Approved hours summaries are the primary payroll and billing output — they must be validated against the governing wage/hour determination (SCA, Davis-Bacon, state prevailing wage) to confirm pay rate
Free-text description of the reason for any post-approval amendment to this hours summary. Required when is_amended is True. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.

Current lifecycle state of the approved hours summary record within the timesheet approval workflow. approved indicates hours are cleared for billing and payroll; disputed indicates a client or worker challenge is open; amended indicates a post-approval correction was applied.

The exact date and time when the timesheet hours were formally approved by the authorized approver. Represents the principal business event timestamp that triggers downstream billing and payroll processing.

The hourly rate charged to the client for the workers regular hours during this pay period, as defined in the client MSA or VMS rate card. Expressed in USD per hour. Used to calculate total billable amount.
Indicates the current state of this approved hours summarys transmission to the client billing system (Salesforce Revenue Cloud). invoiced means a client invoice has been generated from this record.

The total employer burden rate applied to this workers payable wages for the pay period, expressed as a decimal percentage. Encompasses FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation (Workers Comp), and benefits costs. Used to calculate fully-loaded labor cost.
The date and time when this approved hours summary record was first created in the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements.
The hourly rate charged to the client for double-time (DT) hours during this pay period. Typically 2x the regular bill rate per the client MSA or applicable state labor law.

Total approved double-time (DT) hours worked during the pay period, applicable in jurisdictions or client agreements requiring 2x pay for hours beyond a daily or weekly threshold (e.g., California daily OT rules).

business_glossary_term: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Exemption Status
description: Indicates whether the worker is classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) for this assignment. Non-exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay at 1.5x for hours exceeding 40 per week.

The gross margin (Spread) for this pay period, calculated as total_billable_amount minus total_payable_amount minus burden costs. Represents the staffing agencys gross profit contribution from this worker-assignment combination before SG&A.
Total approved hours worked on designated company or client holidays during the pay period. Holiday hours may carry a premium bill rate and pay rate per the client MSA or worker agreement.

Indicates whether this approved hours summary record has been amended after initial approval (e.g., due to a timesheet correction, missed punch adjustment, or retroactive rate change). True triggers a reconciliation process in payroll and billing.

Boolean flag confirming that all hours in this summary have been formally approved by an authorized approver. True indicates the record is cleared for payroll and billing handoff. False indicates approval is still pending or was revoked.
The hourly rate charged to the client for overtime (OT) hours during this pay period. Typically 1.5x the regular bill rate, but may be negotiated differently in the client MSA.
Total approved overtime (OT) hours worked during the pay period, typically hours exceeding 40 per week for non-exempt workers under FLSA. Billed and paid at the OT multiplier rate (commonly 1.5x).
The last calendar date of the pay period covered by this approved hours summary. Defines the inclusive end boundary for all hours aggregated in this record.
The first calendar date of the pay period covered by this approved hours summary. Defines the inclusive start boundary for all hours aggregated in this record.

The hourly rate paid to the worker for regular hours during this pay period, as defined in the workers placement agreement. Expressed in USD per hour. Used to calculate total payable amount and gross margin (Spread).

Indicates the current state of this approved hours summarys transmission to the payroll processing system (TempWorks Payroll). confirmed means payroll has acknowledged receipt and the record is locked for the pay run.
The daily per diem allowance rate paid to the worker per approved per diem unit. Must not exceed IRS-published per diem limits to maintain tax-free treatment. Expressed in USD per day.

Number of per diem units (typically days) approved for the worker during the pay period. Per diem is a daily allowance paid to workers for meals, lodging, or incidentals when working away from their tax home. Not subject to payroll taxes when within IRS limits.
Total approved paid time off (PTO) hours for the worker during the pay period. PTO hours are payable to the worker but are typically not billable to the client unless specified in the MSA.

Total approved regular (straight-time) hours worked by the worker during the pay period, excluding overtime and double-time. Used as the base quantity for both client billing and worker payroll at the standard bill rate and pay rate respectively.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this approved hours summary was sourced or triggered. Kronos Workforce Ready is the primary source; Beeline VMS may be the source for MSP-managed programs; Manual indicates a staffing coordinator override entry.

Externally-visible business identifier for this approved hours summary record, formatted as AHS-{YYYY}-{WW}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in billing and payroll correspondence to reference a specific approved period.

Total gross amount to be invoiced to the client for this pay period, calculated as the sum of (regular_hours × bill_rate) + (ot_hours × ot_bill_rate) + (dt_hours × dt_bill_rate) + (holiday_hours × applicable holiday bill rate). Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice generation.

Sum of all approved hour types (regular + OT + DT + holiday) for the worker during the pay period. Represents the total time-based quantity used for workforce analytics and FTE calculations. Excludes PTO hours.

Total gross wages payable to the worker for this pay period, calculated as the sum of (regular_hours × pay_rate) + (ot_hours × ot_pay_rate) + (dt_hours × dt_pay_rate) + (holiday_hours × holiday pay rate) + (per_diem_units × per_diem_rate). Feeds TempWorks Payroll processing.
The date and time when this approved hours summary record was last modified. Tracks amendments such as retroactive adjustments or correction reprocessing.
The requisition or purchase order number assigned by the clients Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this workers engagement. Required on client invoices for MSP-managed programs.

Indicates whether the worker performed services on-site at the client location, fully remote, or in a hybrid arrangement during this pay period. Affects per diem eligibility and certain state tax obligations.

Classification of the workers tax and employment relationship for this assignment. W-2 indicates a staffing agency employee; 1099 indicates an independent contractor (IC); C2C indicates a corp-to-corp engagement. Drives payroll tax treatment and burden rate application.

The National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI) workers compensation class code assigned to the worker for this assignment. Determines the workers comp insurance premium rate applied within the burden rate calculation.

Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single shift-level or daily time entry record. Primary key for the time_entry product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — granular child record of a timesheet header representing one worked period.

Reference to the active placement or assignment under which this time entry is recorded. Ties the worked hours to the specific job order, client, and bill/pay rate agreement governing this engagement.

Foreign key linking to billing.bill_rate. Business justification: Each time entry is billed at a specific rate record. Linking to bill_rate enables rate audit and dispute resolution — staffing billing teams must verify which approved bill_rate record governed each t
Reference to the client (end-customer) at whose worksite the hours were worked. Used for client billing, SLA reporting, and workforce analytics segmented by client account.

Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Individual time entries inherit rate schedules from assignments; payroll and billing systems validate each entrys bill/pay rates against contracted schedules. Real business process: line-item rate va

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Individual time entries must validate worker credentials to determine billable/payable status. Expired credentials make hours non-billable in VMS programs. Critical for per-entry compliance auditing i

Foreign key linking to joborder.order_location. Business justification: Prevailing wage compliance (SCA/Davis-Bacon) and workers' comp classification require matching each time entry to the specific order location. order_location carries prevailing_wage_applicable, worker

Reference to the contingent worker whose time is being recorded. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE RESOURCE_REFERENCE category. Links to the worker master record for pay rate, classification, and compliance lookups.

Foreign key linking to timesheet.shift. Business justification: A time_entry is the granular daily/shift-level record of actual work performed, and it directly corresponds to a specific shift instance. The shift entity captures the scheduled and actual shift detai

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Individual time entries for SOW workers must reference the SOW engagement for deliverable-based billing and hours-against-budget tracking. Staffing operations teams use this to validate SOW milestone

Foreign key reference to the parent timesheet header record that groups this time entry within a pay period. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category. Required for payroll aggregation and client billing rollup.

Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Each time entry must comply with FLSA/state wage-hour rules. OT calculation, meal break compliance, and minimum wage validation depend on wage-hour determination. Real business process: compliant time

Reference to the physical client worksite location where the hours were worked. Required for workers compensation premium calculation (state and class code), OSHA recordkeeping, and multi-state payroll tax jurisdiction determination.

Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: time_entry.flsa_exempt is a denormalized classification attribute. Worker classification determines FLSA exemption status, pay type eligibility, and overtime applicability for each time entry. Payroll

Standardized code indicating the reason for any absence or non-worked time within this entry period (e.g., SICK, FMLA, JURY, BEREAVEMENT, NO_SHOW, WEATHER). Null when the worker was present for the full scheduled shift. Required for FMLA tracking and workforce attendance analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sick|fmla|jury_duty|bereavement|no_show|weather|personal|military — promote to reference product]

The actual clock-out date-time for the worked period. Paired with actual_start_time and break_duration_minutes to compute net hours worked. Required for FLSA §516 and California Labor Code §226 itemized wage statement backing data.

The actual clock-in date-time for the worked period as captured by Kronos (badge swipe, biometric, mobile punch, or manual entry). Used for attendance analysis, late-arrival tracking, and FLSA recordkeeping.
Date and time when this time entry was approved by the authorized approver. Distinct from submission and creation timestamps. Required for SLA measurement (time-to-approve) and FLSA §516 audit trail.

Total unpaid break time in minutes deducted from the gross shift duration to arrive at net hours worked. Includes all meal and rest break periods. Required for accurate net hours calculation and California Labor Code §512 meal period compliance.

Client or internal cost center code to which the labor hours are allocated for billing and financial reporting. Sourced from the clients organizational hierarchy in Beeline VMS or Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Enables cost allocation reporting in Oracle NetSuite ERP.

System timestamp when this time entry record was first created in the source system (Kronos Workforce Ready). Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Used for data lineage, ETL watermarking, and audit trail.

The calendar date on which the worked period occurred (yyyy-MM-dd). Serves as the primary business event date for this time entry. Used for daily labor reporting, schedule variance analysis, and FLSA workweek calculations. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE anchor date.

The method or system through which this time entry was captured: Kronos hardware clock (badge/biometric), Kronos mobile app, manual supervisor entry, VMS import from Beeline, or supervisor-entered correction. Supports audit trail requirements and data quality monitoring.

Current workflow lifecycle state of the time entry record. Controls whether the entry is editable, pending approval, approved for payroll/billing processing, rejected for correction, voided, or locked after payroll run. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT lifecycle dimension.

Indicates whether the hours in this time entry are billable to the client. False for internal training time, orientation hours, or non-billable absence periods. Drives inclusion/exclusion in Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice generation.

Indicates whether the hours in this time entry are payable to the worker. May differ from is_billable in cases such as orientation (payable but not billable) or disputed hours (billable but payment withheld pending resolution). Drives TempWorks Payroll gross pay inclusion.

Standardized job classification code identifying the role or position being performed during this time entry. Aligns with the job orders position code and is used for EEOC reporting, OFCCP compliance, and workforce analytics by job classification.

Source system identifier from Kronos Workforce Ready linking this time entry back to the originating raw punch event record. Enables drill-through from the Silver layer aggregated entry to the raw punch event for audit and dispute resolution. Null for manually entered records.

Classification of the type of work performed during this entry (e.g., Light Industrial, IT Professional, Healthcare, Administrative, Engineering). Used for workforce analytics, cost allocation, and Beeline VMS rate card alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: light_industrial|it_professional|healthcare|administrative|engineering|skilled_trades|clerical|professional — promote to reference product]

Sequential ordering number of this time entry within its parent timesheet header. Enables ordered display and processing of multiple daily entries within a single timesheet period. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.

Indicates whether the required meal break was taken in compliance with applicable state law (e.g., California Labor Code §512 requires a 30-minute unpaid meal period for shifts exceeding 5 hours). True = compliant; False = potential violation requiring premium pay or legal review.

System timestamp of the most recent modification to this time entry record. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Tracks corrections, retroactive adjustments, and re-approvals. Essential for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer pipeline.

Actual compensable hours worked during this entry period, computed as (actual_end_time − actual_start_time) − break_duration_minutes, expressed in decimal hours. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category. This is the authoritative hours figure fed to payroll and client billing. Subject to FLSA §516 recordkeeping.

The hourly pay rate (in USD) applicable to this time entry for worker compensation. Captured at the entry level to preserve the rate-in-effect at time of work, which may differ from the current placement rate due to retroactive adjustments or mid-period rate changes. Feeds TempWorks Payroll gross pay calculation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT monetary dimension.

Classification of the hours worked for compensation purposes: regular straight-time (REG), overtime at 1.5x (OT), double-time at 2x (DT), holiday premium, per diem allowance, or paid time off (PTO). Drives the pay rate multiplier applied in TempWorks Payroll and the bill rate applied in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.

Per diem allowance amount (in USD) associated with this time entry for travel, lodging, or meal reimbursement when the worker is on a remote or travel assignment. Tracked separately from wages for IRS per diem tax treatment. Zero if no per diem applies.

Number of paid time off (PTO) hours applied to this entry when the worker is absent but entitled to paid leave. Distinct from net_hours_worked (which reflects physically worked hours). Feeds PTO balance deduction in SAP SuccessFactors and payroll processing in TempWorks.

Free-text or coded reason provided by the approver when a time entry is rejected (entry_status = rejected). Documents the basis for rejection to guide worker or supervisor correction. Null when entry is not in rejected status.

Indicates whether all required paid rest breaks were provided in compliance with applicable state law (e.g., California IWC Wage Orders require a 10-minute paid rest period for every 4 hours worked). True = compliant; False = potential premium pay obligation.

Delta between planned hours (derived from scheduled_start_time and scheduled_end_time) and actual net_hours_worked for this entry. Positive value indicates overtime relative to schedule; negative indicates early departure or absence. Key KPI for workforce scheduling compliance and dispatch analytics.
The planned shift end date-time as defined in the workers schedule. Paired with scheduled_start_time to derive planned shift duration for schedule variance and workforce planning analytics.

The planned shift start date-time as defined in the workers schedule in Kronos Workforce Ready. Used to compute schedule variance (planned vs. actual hours delta) and to assess punctuality and dispatch compliance.

Alphanumeric code identifying the applicable shift differential pay rule (e.g., EVE15, NIGHT20, WKD10). Maps to the differential rate table in TempWorks Payroll to apply the correct premium multiplier to base pay rate. Distinct from shift_type which is a broad category.

Categorical classification of the shift period for differential pay and workforce scheduling analytics. Day, evening, night, weekend, and holiday shifts may carry different pay and bill rate differentials per the client MSA or worker agreement.

Two-letter US state code (e.g., CA, NY, TX) identifying the state where the work was physically performed. Determines applicable state wage and hour laws, state income tax withholding jurisdiction, SUTA rate, and state-specific break and overtime rules. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance.

Date and time when the worker or supervisor submitted this time entry for approval. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category. Used to measure submission timeliness against payroll cutoff deadlines and SLA compliance.
