Current workflow state of the timesheet through the submission and approval lifecycle. Controls downstream payroll processing and client billing eligibility. draft = worker has not yet submitted; submitted = awaiting supervisor/client approval; approved = authorized for payroll and billing; rejected = returned to worker for correction; pending_review = escalated for secondary review; voided = cancelled and excluded from processing.

Date and time when the timesheet was formally approved by the authorized approver (supervisor or client manager). Triggers eligibility for payroll processing and client billing. Null if not yet approved. Supports approval SLA measurement and audit compliance.

Hourly bill rate (in USD) charged to the client for regular straight-time hours during this pay period. Sourced from the assignment/VMS rate card. Used by Salesforce Revenue Cloud to generate client invoices. Classified confidential as client pricing data.

Total hours that are billable to the client for this pay period. Typically equals regular + OT + DT + holiday hours (excludes PTO and unpaid absences which are not client-billable). Authoritative input to Salesforce Revenue Cloud for client invoice generation. May differ from total_hours when non-billable paid time is present.

Total unpaid break time (meal periods) recorded during the pay period. Tracked to support state-mandated meal and rest break compliance (California IWC Wage Orders, Washington WAC 296-126-092, Oregon ORS 653.261). Ensures break deductions are correctly applied to gross hours to derive net payable hours.

System timestamp when the timesheet record was first created in Kronos Workforce Ready. Represents the audit record creation event. Required for FLSA §516 minimum 3-year retention compliance and SOC 2 Type II audit trail.

Total double-time hours worked during the pay period. Applicable under state-specific laws (e.g., California Labor Code §510 — hours over 12 in a day or all hours on the 7th consecutive day). Billed at the DT bill rate and paid at 2x the regular pay rate. Null or zero in jurisdictions without DT requirements.

Total approved expense reimbursements (in USD) for the pay period beyond per diem (e.g., mileage, tools, uniforms). Payable to the worker and potentially billable to the client per MSA terms. Tracked separately from wages for payroll and billing accuracy.

Workers FLSA classification for this assignment: exempt (not entitled to OT under FLSA salary/duties tests) or non_exempt (entitled to OT at 1.5x for hours over 40/week). Drives overtime eligibility logic in payroll processing. Subject to DOL enforcement and IRS worker classification rules.

Hours worked on designated company or client-observed holidays during the pay period. May be billed at a premium holiday bill rate and paid at a holiday pay rate per the assignment agreement or client MSA. Tracked separately for accurate payroll and billing rate application.

Flag indicating whether the timesheet has been formally disputed by the worker, client, or staffing firm. Triggers a dispute resolution workflow and places a hold on payroll processing and client billing until resolved. Supports dispute rate KPI and compliance audit.

Flag indicating whether the worker submitted the timesheet after the client or staffing firms defined submission deadline for the pay period. Used for late submission rate KPI reporting, worker compliance tracking, and payroll processing delay root cause analysis.

Native timesheet identifier from the Kronos Workforce Ready source system. Enables direct traceability from the Silver layer record back to the operational system of record for audit, reconciliation, and support purposes.

Flag indicating whether the worker signed a valid meal break waiver for this pay period, as permitted under California IWC Wage Orders for shifts of 6 hours or less, or mutual consent waivers for second meal periods. Required for California, Oregon, and Washington meal break compliance documentation. Null for non-applicable jurisdictions.

Hourly bill rate (in USD) charged to the client for overtime hours. Typically 1.5x the regular bill rate per the client MSA or VMS rate card. Used by Salesforce Revenue Cloud for OT billing line generation.

Total overtime hours worked during the pay period, typically hours exceeding 40 in a workweek under FLSA federal rules, or daily thresholds under state law (e.g., California 8-hour daily OT rule). Billed at the OT bill rate and paid at 1.5x the regular pay rate. Critical for FLSA §207 compliance and accurate payroll/billing.

Hourly pay rate (in USD) applied to overtime hours. Typically 1.5x the regular pay rate per FLSA §207. May vary by state law or assignment agreement. Used by TempWorks Payroll for OT gross wage calculation.

Frequency classification of the pay period covered by this timesheet. Determines payroll processing schedule and FLSA workweek overtime calculation methodology. weekly = 7-day period; biweekly = 14-day period; semi_monthly = twice per month; monthly = full calendar month.

Hourly pay rate (in USD) at which the worker is compensated for regular straight-time hours during this pay period. Sourced from the assignment agreement. Used by TempWorks Payroll to calculate gross wages. Subject to FLSA minimum wage compliance and state minimum wage laws. Classified confidential as worker compensation data.

Total per diem reimbursement amount (in USD) for the pay period, covering meals, lodging, and incidental expenses for workers on travel assignments. Non-taxable up to IRS GSA per diem rates. Payable to the worker but may or may not be billable to the client depending on the MSA. Tracked separately from wages for IRS tax treatment.

Hours of paid time off (PTO) used by the worker during the pay period. Includes vacation, sick leave, and other approved paid absences. Payable but not billable to the client. Tracked for PTO balance management and payroll cost allocation. Subject to state-mandated paid sick leave laws (e.g., California SB 3, Washington ESSB 5975).

Total straight-time (non-overtime, non-double-time) hours worked by the worker during the pay period. Represents the base billable and payable hours at the standard pay rate and bill rate. Core input to payroll gross pay calculation and client invoice generation. Subject to FLSA §207 threshold monitoring.

Free-text or coded reason provided by the approver when a timesheet is rejected. Captures the basis for rejection (e.g., hours discrepancy, missing punch, unauthorized OT, incorrect cost center). Supports worker correction guidance, rejection pattern analysis, and audit documentation.

Date and time when the worker electronically submitted the timesheet for approval. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the timesheet lifecycle. Used for SLA tracking (time-to-approve), late submission reporting, and audit trail under FLSA §516 recordkeeping requirements.

Human-readable, externally visible timesheet reference number used in worker communications, client invoices, and payroll remittances. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready and carried through to billing and payroll systems.

Sum of all compensable hours for the pay period: regular + OT + DT + holiday + PTO hours. Represents the total payable hours used as the basis for gross pay calculation. Excludes unpaid break hours and unpaid absences. Authoritative hours figure fed to TempWorks Payroll for payroll processing.

Hours of unpaid absence recorded during the pay period (e.g., unexcused absences, unpaid leave). Not billable to the client and not payable to the worker. Tracked for attendance management, assignment compliance monitoring, and potential assignment termination triggers.

System timestamp of the most recent modification to the timesheet record. Tracks any changes post-creation including corrections, re-submissions, or approval status changes. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer and audit compliance.

External timesheet identifier assigned by the clients Vendor Management System (e.g., Beeline VMS) when the client manages contingent labor through a VMS program. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the staffing firms Kronos records and the clients VMS approval records. Null for non-VMS-managed assignments.

Client-issued work order or purchase order number associated with the workers assignment for this pay period. Required on client invoices for accounts payable matching. Sourced from the clients procurement or VMS system. Null if the client does not require PO-based billing.

Classification of the workers engagement type for this timesheet period. Determines tax treatment, payroll processing path, and co-employment risk profile. w2_temp = W-2 temporary employee; w2_contract = W-2 contract worker; 1099_ic = Independent Contractor (1099); corp_to_corp = C2C vendor engagement; eor = Employer of Record arrangement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: w2_temp|w2_contract|1099_ic|corp_to_corp|eor — promote to reference product]
Unique identifier for the direct deposit setup record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire placements require direct deposit setup for the worker's first paycheck processed through the staffing firm during the guarantee period. Payroll operations need direct_deposit_setup→direct

Reference to the worker (candidate placed on assignment) for whom this direct deposit instruction is configured. Links to the worker master record in the placement or candidate domain.
Workers bank account number. Stored encrypted or tokenized. Masked in all user interfaces and reports to protect worker financial data. Required for ACH direct deposit transactions.
Last four digits of the bank account number. Used for display and worker verification purposes without exposing the full account number.
Type of bank account for deposit. Checking is most common for payroll; savings and prepaid card options are also supported.

Standard NACHA return code if the direct deposit transaction was returned by the receiving bank. Examples: R01 (insufficient funds), R02 (account closed), R03 (no account/unable to locate), R04 (invalid account number).

Method by which pay is allocated to this account. Full amount deposits entire net pay; fixed amount deposits a specific dollar value; percentage deposits a specified percentage of net pay. Supports split-deposit configurations.
Numeric value for the allocation. For fixed_amount type, this is the dollar amount (e.g., 500.00). For percentage type, this is the percentage (e.g., 25.00 for 25%). Null for full_amount type.
Name of the financial institution where the account is held. Used for worker reference and audit purposes.
Date when the worker provided consent for direct deposit enrollment. Captured during onboarding process for audit trail and compliance documentation.
Indicates whether the worker provided explicit consent for direct deposit enrollment. Required for compliance with electronic funds transfer regulations and company policy.
Timestamp when this direct deposit setup record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.
Date when this direct deposit instruction is no longer active. Null for currently active instructions. Used when worker changes banking details or ends assignment.
Date from which this direct deposit instruction becomes active and will be used for payroll processing. Allows for future-dated setup changes.

Indicates whether this is the workers primary direct deposit account. True for the main account; false for secondary split-deposit accounts. Only one account per worker should be marked primary at any given time.
Date when this direct deposit instruction was last used for an actual payroll transaction. Helps identify stale or unused banking setups for cleanup and security purposes.
Timestamp when this direct deposit setup record was last modified. Updated whenever any field changes. Audit field for change tracking and compliance.
Free-text field for additional notes or comments about this direct deposit setup. Used by HR staff to document special circumstances, worker requests, or troubleshooting details.
Date when the prenotification was successfully cleared by the bank, confirming the account is valid and ready for live payroll deposits. Typically 2-3 business days after prenote_sent_date.
Date when the prenotification (zero-dollar test transaction) was sent to the bank to verify account validity. Required by NACHA rules before live direct deposit can begin.

Order in which this deposit instruction is processed when multiple accounts are configured (split deposit). Lower numbers are processed first. Typically 1 for primary account, 2+ for secondary accounts.

Explanation if the direct deposit setup was rejected during prenote or live processing. Common reasons include invalid routing number, closed account, or account type mismatch. Populated from ACH return codes.
Nine-digit ABA routing transit number identifying the financial institution. Masked or tokenized in reporting layers for security. Required for ACH transactions.

Method by which the direct deposit information was captured. Worker self-service is most common during onboarding via SAP SuccessFactors portal. HR manual entry used for exceptions. Bulk import and API integration used for large-scale onboarding events.

Current verification and activation status of the direct deposit setup. Prenote (prenotification) is a zero-dollar ACH transaction sent to validate account details before live payroll. Active indicates the account is verified and in use. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|prenote_sent|prenote_cleared|active|rejected|suspended|inactive — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Number of unused PTO hours paid out to the worker upon separation. Used for payroll calculation and benefits reconciliation.

The date from which this labor category definition and its associated rates become effective. Supports rate versioning and historical rate lookups for timesheet processing and billing reconciliation.

The date on which this labor category definition and its associated rates expire. Null for open-ended categories. After this date, the category is no longer available for new timesheet entries unless renewed or superseded.

Indicates whether workers in this labor category are classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Non-exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay; exempt workers are not. Critical for overtime rule application and payroll compliance.

Flag indicating whether workers in this labor category may be engaged as independent contractors (1099) rather than W-2 employees. Drives worker classification compliance checks per IRS worker classification rules and DOL economic reality test.

Standardized job function code classifying the primary work function of this labor category (e.g., IT, Finance, Operations, Engineering, Healthcare). Aligns with O*NET or SOC occupational classification codes for workforce analytics and regulatory reporting.

business_glossary_term: Kronos Workforce Ready Labor Category ID
description: The native labor category identifier in Kronos Workforce Ready, the system of record for time and attendance. Used for system integration and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the Kronos source system.

Current lifecycle status of the labor category record. active indicates the category is available for use in timesheet entries and billing; inactive indicates it has been retired; pending indicates it is awaiting approval; deprecated indicates it has been superseded by a newer category.

Default markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate for this labor category. Represents the spread as a percentage of pay rate. Used in pricing models and profitability analysis. Markup = (Bill Rate - Pay Rate) / Pay Rate × 100.
Maximum allowable hourly bill rate for this labor category. Used as a ceiling in VMS rate card negotiations and client contract management. Bill rates above this ceiling require management approval.

Minimum allowable hourly pay rate for workers assigned to this labor category. Enforces compliance with applicable federal, state, or local minimum wage laws and prevailing wage requirements. Pay rates below this floor are rejected during payroll processing.

Flag indicating whether workers assigned to this labor category are eligible for per diem allowances (daily expense reimbursements for travel, lodging, or meals). Affects payroll processing and client billing for travel-based assignments.

Flag indicating whether this labor category is subject to prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act or Service Contract Act (SCA) for government or federally funded contracts. When true, the prevailing_wage_rate field applies.

The federally or state-mandated prevailing wage rate (in USD per hour) applicable to this labor category for government or public works contracts. Determined by DOL wage determinations under the Davis-Bacon Act or SCA. Null when prevailing_wage_indicator is false.

Code identifying the default bill/pay rate tier associated with this labor category. Rate tiers define pricing bands used across client contracts and VMS programs. Links to rate tier configuration in Beeline VMS and Salesforce Revenue Cloud.

business_glossary_term: Service Contract Act (SCA) Labor Category Code
description: The official Service Contract Act (SCA) labor category code or title as defined in the applicable DOL wage determination. Used to map internal labor categories to SCA-compliant job classifications for federal service contracts. Required for compliance reporting on government staffing contracts.

Proficiency or seniority tier associated with the labor category (e.g., entry, junior, mid, senior, lead, principal, executive). Used to differentiate bill/pay rate tiers and to support workforce planning analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry|junior|mid|senior|lead|principal|executive — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]

U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code mapped to this labor category. Used for EEOC reporting, DOL compliance, workforce analytics, and labor market benchmarking. Format: XX-XXXX (e.g., 15-1252 for Software Developers).

Flag indicating whether placements in this labor category are eligible for temp-to-perm conversion (contract-to-hire). When true, a conversion fee may apply upon permanent placement. Used in Bullhorn ATS/CRM for placement tracking and conversion fee calculation.

Flag indicating whether this labor category is subject to a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) or union work rules. When true, the union_code field identifies the applicable union. Affects pay rates, overtime rules, and benefit requirements.

Code identifying the labor union or collective bargaining agreement (CBA) applicable to this labor category. Null when union_applicable is false. Used to apply union-specific pay rules, benefit contributions, and work rules in payroll processing.
Timestamp when this labor category record was last modified. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.

NCCI (National Council on Compensation Insurance) workers compensation class code associated with this labor category. Determines the workers comp insurance rate applied to payroll for workers in this category. Used by TempWorks Payroll for burden rate calculation.

Unique system-generated identifier for the overtime and double-time calculation rule record. Primary key for the ot_rule entity. [ROLE: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this entity defines enumerated OT/DT calculation rules used as a reference by time entry and payroll processing; exempt from per-role minimums per canonical-attrs-enforced rule.]

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: OT rules implement state-specific compliance requirements (CA daily OT, NY spread-of-hours, etc.). Each OT rule must reference the governing state compliance rule. Real business process: jurisdiction-

Reference to the ot_rule_id of the newer rule that replaces this rule when rule_status is superseded. Enables rule version chain tracing for historical timesheet recalculation and compliance audits. Null if this rule has not been superseded.

Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: OT rules (daily/weekly thresholds, multipliers) are derived from and must be validated against wage/hour determinations (FLSA, SCA, state law). Compliance officers auditing OT rule configurations need

Specific statutory or regulatory citation governing this OT rule (e.g., FLSA §207, California Labor Code §510, Nevada Revised Statutes §608.018, Davis-Bacon Act 40 U.S.C. §3141). Used for compliance documentation, legal audit trails, and regulatory reporting to DOL and OFCCP.

Indicates whether this OT rule governs the classification of billable OT hours for client invoicing purposes (True), in addition to payable hours for worker compensation. When True, OT bill rate multipliers are applied in Salesforce Revenue Cloud for client billing. When False, the rule applies only to payroll (pay side) calculations.

Name or user identifier of the payroll manager, compliance officer, or HR administrator who approved this OT rule configuration for activation. Required for SOC 2 Type II change management controls and FLSA compliance audit trails. Populated when rule_status transitions to active.

Date on which this OT rule configuration was formally approved for activation by the authorized approver. Distinct from effective_date (when the rule starts being applied) and created_timestamp (when the record was created). Supports change management audit trails per SOC 2 Type II.

Multiplier applied to the standard bill rate to calculate the DT bill rate charged to the client for double-time hours (e.g., 2.00 means the client is billed at 2x the straight-time bill rate for DT hours). Null if the rule does not include a DT tier or if applies_to_billable_hours is False.

Multiplier applied to the standard bill rate to calculate the OT bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours (e.g., 1.50 means the client is billed at 1.5x the straight-time bill rate for OT hours). May differ from the pay-side OT multiplier based on client MSA terms. Null if applies_to_billable_hours is False.

Method used to calculate the regular rate of pay for OT purposes when a worker is on multiple concurrent assignments with different pay rates. weighted_average = FLSA §207(g)(1) weighted average of all rates; highest_rate = FLSA §207(g)(2) highest rate method; primary_assignment = rate of the primary assignment governs; not_applicable = worker is on a single assignment only.

Indicates whether unpaid meal/rest break deductions are applied before OT threshold calculation under this rule. True = break time is deducted from hours worked before OT is computed; False = all clocked hours count toward OT threshold without break deduction. Affects OT calculation in Kronos Workforce Ready.

Timestamp when this OT rule record was first created in the system (ISO 8601 format with timezone offset). Provides audit trail for rule configuration history and supports SOC 2 Type II compliance logging requirements.

Number of hours worked in a single workday beyond which double-time (DT) pay is triggered (e.g., 12.00 hours per California Labor Code §510). Hours worked above this threshold are compensated at the DT multiplier rate. Null if the rule does not include a daily DT threshold.

Number of hours worked in a single workday beyond which daily overtime pay is triggered (e.g., 8.00 hours per California Labor Code §510). Hours worked above this threshold and below the DT threshold are compensated at the OT multiplier. Null if the rule does not apply a daily OT threshold.

Pay rate multiplier applied to the workers regular rate of pay for hours classified as double-time (e.g., 2.00 for double-time per California Labor Code §510). Null if the rule does not include a DT tier. Used by TempWorks Payroll for DT wage calculation.

Date on which this OT rule becomes effective and begins to be applied to time entry calculations (inclusive). Used for date-range-based rule selection to ensure the correct rule version is applied to historical and current timesheets. Aligns with FLSA and state law effective dates.

Date on which this OT rule ceases to be effective (inclusive). Null for open-ended rules with no planned expiration. Used in conjunction with effective_date for date-range-based rule version management. When a rule is superseded, this date is set to the day before the new rules effective_date.

business_glossary_term: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Exemption Category
description: Specific FLSA exemption category when flsa_exemption_status is exempt or partial_exempt (e.g., executive, administrative, professional, computer_employee, highly_compensated, 207k_public_safety). Null for non-exempt workers. Required for FLSA compliance documentation and OFCCP audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive|administrative|professional|computer_employee|highly_compensated|207k_public_safety|outside_sales|seasonal — promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Exemption Status
description: FLSA overtime exemption classification applicable under this rule. non_exempt = worker is entitled to OT pay under FLSA; exempt = worker meets FLSA white-collar or other exemption criteria and is not entitled to OT; partial_exempt = specific exemption applies to some but not all OT provisions (e.g., §207(k) for law enforcement/fire). Drives OT eligibility in TempWorks Payroll.

Defines how holiday hours are treated for OT threshold calculation. count_as_worked = holiday pay hours count toward weekly OT threshold; exclude = holiday hours are excluded from OT threshold calculation; premium_separate = holiday premium is calculated separately and does not affect OT threshold. Configured per client MSA or state law.

Job category or occupational classification to which this OT rule is restricted (e.g., light_industrial, healthcare_rn, it_professional, clerical, all). Null or all indicates the rule applies across all job categories. Supports differentiated OT rules for prevailing wage or union-covered job categories.

Level of legal jurisdiction governing this OT rule. federal = applies nationwide under FLSA; state = applies within a specific U.S. state; local = applies within a specific municipality or county; contract = applies per client MSA or SOW terms regardless of geography.

Timestamp when this OT rule record was most recently updated (ISO 8601 format with timezone offset). Used to detect configuration changes, trigger downstream recalculation of affected timesheets, and support SOC 2 Type II audit logging.

Pay rate multiplier applied to the workers regular rate of pay for hours classified as overtime (e.g., 1.50 for time-and-a-half per FLSA §207). Used by TempWorks Payroll to calculate OT wages and by Salesforce Revenue Cloud to calculate OT bill amounts.

Indicates whether this OT rule incorporates prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act (for federal construction contracts) or the Service Contract Act (SCA) for government service contracts. True = prevailing wage OT rates apply; False = standard commercial OT rates apply. Critical for government staffing contract compliance.

DOL wage determination number associated with the prevailing wage schedule governing this OT rule (e.g., WD 2023-0001). Populated only when prevailing_wage_applies is True. Required for Davis-Bacon Act and SCA compliance documentation on government staffing contracts.

business_glossary_term: Paid Time Off (PTO) Counts Toward Overtime (OT) Flag
description: Indicates whether paid time off (PTO) hours are included in the weekly hours count for OT threshold purposes. True = PTO hours count toward the OT threshold (e.g., 32 hours worked + 8 PTO = 40 hours, no OT triggered); False = only actual hours worked count toward OT threshold per FLSA standard. Configurable per client MSA.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this OT/DT rule for reference in payroll configurations, VMS integrations, and client billing systems (e.g., FLSA_FED_STD, CA_510_DAILY, DAVIS_BACON_GOV). Used as the business key in Kronos Workforce Ready and TempWorks Payroll rule tables.

Detailed narrative description of the overtime rule, including the legal basis, applicable thresholds, and any special conditions (e.g., seventh-consecutive-day provisions, blended rate requirements, prevailing wage applicability). Supports compliance documentation and auditor review.

Human-readable name describing the overtime rule (e.g., Federal FLSA Standard 40-Hour Weekly OT, California Daily OT 8/12 Rule, Davis-Bacon Prevailing Wage OT). Used in payroll configuration UIs, compliance reports, and worker assignment documentation.

Numeric priority rank used for conflict resolution when multiple OT rules are applicable to the same time entry (e.g., a worker in California on a government contract may have federal FLSA, California state, and Davis-Bacon rules all applicable). Lower integer = higher priority. Ensures deterministic OT rule selection in Kronos Workforce Ready and TempWorks Payroll.

Current lifecycle status of the OT rule record. active = currently applied to time entry calculations; inactive = disabled and not applied; draft = under review before activation; superseded = replaced by a newer rule version but retained for historical reference.

Classification of the overtime rule by its governing authority or applicability scope. federal = FLSA-based federal standard; state = state-specific labor law (e.g., California, Nevada); client_specific = client-negotiated OT terms in MSA; prevailing_wage = Davis-Bacon Act or SCA government contract rules; blended = multi-assignment blended rate OT calculation.

business_glossary_term: Seventh Consecutive Day Double-Time (DT) Threshold Hours
description: On the seventh consecutive workday, the number of hours beyond which double-time pay applies (e.g., 8.00 hours per California Labor Code §510 — first 8 hours at OT, beyond 8 at DT). Null if seventh_day_ot_applies is False or if no DT tier applies on the seventh day.

business_glossary_term: Seventh Consecutive Day Overtime (OT) Applies Flag
description: Indicates whether this rule includes the seventh-consecutive-day OT provision, where all hours worked on the seventh consecutive day of a workweek are paid at OT rate (True) per California Labor Code §510. False for rules that do not include this provision.

Operational system of record from which this OT rule configuration was sourced or is mastered. kronos = Kronos Workforce Ready time and attendance; tempworks = TempWorks Payroll rule configuration; beeline = Beeline VMS rate and rule management; manual = manually entered by payroll or compliance team; bullhorn = Bullhorn ATS/CRM placement configuration.

Native identifier of this OT rule in the originating operational system (e.g., Kronos Workforce Ready pay rule ID, TempWorks Payroll OT rule code). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system for audit and troubleshooting purposes.

Two-letter U.S. state abbreviation (ISO 3166-2:US) identifying the state whose labor law governs this OT rule (e.g., CA for California Labor Code §510, NV for Nevada daily OT). Null for federal-level rules applicable nationwide. Used for multi-state OT rule resolution in Kronos Workforce Ready.

Indicates whether this OT rule is governed by or must comply with a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) or union contract. True = union OT provisions apply and may override standard FLSA or state rules; False = no union agreement governs this rule. Relevant for unionized light industrial and healthcare staffing placements.

Identifier or name of the collective bargaining agreement (CBA) that governs this OT rule when union_agreement_applies is True (e.g., SEIU Local 1199 CBA 2023-2026, Teamsters Local 25 CBA). Null when union_agreement_applies is False. Used for compliance documentation and payroll audit.

Number of hours worked in a workweek beyond which double-time pay is triggered (e.g., some state or client rules apply DT after 60 hours/week). Null if the rule does not apply a weekly DT threshold. Distinct from the daily DT threshold.

Number of hours worked in a workweek beyond which overtime pay is triggered (e.g., 40.00 hours per FLSA §207 federal standard). Hours worked above this threshold are compensated at the OT multiplier rate. Null if the rule does not apply a weekly OT threshold.

Worker tax classification to which this OT rule applies. w2_employee = applies only to W-2 employees on payroll; 1099_ic = applies to independent contractors (IC) per contract terms; all = applies regardless of worker classification. Aligns with IRS worker classification rules and FLSA co-employment considerations.

The designated first day of the workweek for purposes of weekly OT threshold calculation (e.g., sunday for a Sunday–Saturday workweek per FLSA §207). Workweek definition is fixed and regularly recurring per FLSA. Configured in Kronos Workforce Ready per client or assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sunday|monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Unique surrogate identifier for each pay period record in the staffing operation. Primary key for the timesheet_pay_period reference entity. [_canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this entity is a calendar/reference table defining pay period cycles; per-role minimums do not apply, but comprehensive coverage is still provided for operational completeness.]

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: State compliance rules mandate pay frequency (weekly in some states, bi-weekly in others) and pay period structure. Pay periods must reference the governing state rule for payroll compliance audits. a

Precise timestamp by which client supervisors or staffing coordinators must approve submitted timesheets. Drives SLA enforcement for approval workflows and escalation triggers in Kronos Workforce Ready. Supports on-time payroll processing and client billing cycle alignment.

The date on which client invoices are generated for billable hours worked during this pay period. May differ from check_date in cases where billing and payroll cycles are offset. Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice generation and Oracle NetSuite Accounts Receivable.

The standard calendar year (4-digit integer, e.g., 2024) to which this pay period belongs. Distinct from fiscal_year when the business operates on a non-calendar fiscal year. Used for W-2 and 1099 tax year alignment in TempWorks Payroll.

The date on which worker paychecks or direct deposit payments are issued for this pay period. Represents the actual pay date visible on W-2 wage statements and worker pay stubs. Used for tax reporting, FICA, FUTA, and SUTA liability calculations in TempWorks Payroll.

Business segment or client grouping for which this pay period definition is applicable (e.g., Healthcare, Light Industrial, IT Staffing, All). Supports multi-client staffing operations where different client verticals may operate on different pay cycles per MSA (Master Service Agreement) terms.

Timestamp when this pay period record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance. Populated automatically at record insertion and never updated.

Number of hours worked beyond which double-time (DT) pay rates apply for this pay period. Applicable in states such as California where DT is mandated after 12 hours in a workday or on the 7th consecutive workday. Supports multi-state payroll compliance in TempWorks Payroll.

business_glossary_term: Pay Period Definition Effective From Date
description: The date from which this pay period definition record became effective in the system. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking for pay period calendar changes (e.g., a client switching from weekly to bi-weekly pay cycles). Enables historical payroll reprocessing and audit compliance.

business_glossary_term: Pay Period Definition Effective Until Date
description: The date on which this pay period definition record ceases to be effective. Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned end date. Supports SCD Type 2 history tracking for pay period calendar changes and enables point-in-time payroll audit queries.

The last calendar date of the pay period. All timesheet hours submitted with a work date on or before this date are grouped into this pay period. Together with start_date, defines the authoritative time boundary for worker compensation and client billing.

The fiscal month (1–12) within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs. Supports monthly financial close processes in Oracle NetSuite ERP and monthly workforce analytics reporting in Power BI Analytics Platform.

The fiscal quarter (1–4) within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs. Used for quarterly payroll tax filings (941, SUTA), financial reporting, and KPI dashboards in Power BI Analytics Platform.

The sequential fiscal week number (1–53) within the fiscal year. Used for weekly labor analytics, overtime threshold tracking, and workforce scheduling reports in Kronos Workforce Ready and Power BI Analytics Platform. Particularly important for weekly pay period types.

The fiscal year (4-digit integer, e.g., 2024) to which this pay period belongs. Used for financial reporting, budget allocation, and year-end tax filings in Oracle NetSuite ERP. Supports multi-year analytics in Power BI Analytics Platform.

Indicates whether this pay period contains one or more observed holidays that affect standard workday counts, holiday pay eligibility, or payroll processing schedules. When True, payroll coordinators must review holiday pay rules and adjusted cutoff dates. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready holiday calendar.

Number of observed holidays falling within this pay period. Used to calculate adjusted workday counts, holiday pay liability, and per diem adjustments. Supports holiday pay eligibility rules for temporary workers under client MSA (Master Service Agreement) terms.

Indicates whether this pay period record is currently active and available for timesheet assignment and payroll processing. False records represent historical, future-dated, or deactivated periods. Used to filter active periods in Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling and TempWorks Payroll run selection.

Indicates whether this is the currently active pay period (i.e., todays date falls within start_date and end_date). Only one record per period_type should have this flag set to True at any given time. Used as a quick filter in operational dashboards and payroll processing queues.

Free-text field for payroll coordinators to document special instructions, exceptions, or contextual information for this pay period (e.g., Adjusted cutoff due to Thanksgiving holiday, Early check date for Christmas week). Supports operational communication and audit documentation.

Number of hours worked beyond which overtime (OT) pay rates apply for this pay period. Typically 40.00 hours for weekly FLSA overtime, but may vary by state law (e.g., California daily OT at 8 hours). Critical for payroll compliance and accurate OT cost calculations in TempWorks Payroll.

The deadline by which all timesheets must be submitted and approved for inclusion in this pay periods payroll run. Timesheets received after this date are held for the next pay period or processed as off-cycle adjustments. Operationally critical for TempWorks Payroll batch processing.

Code identifying the payroll processing provider or entity responsible for executing payroll for this pay period (e.g., TEMPWORKS, EOR_PARTNER_01). Relevant for EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements where a third-party provider processes payroll on behalf of the staffing firm.

Classifies the type of payroll run associated with this pay period. regular = standard scheduled payroll; off_cycle = unscheduled run for missed payments or terminations; supplemental = bonus or commission payments; correction = retroactive adjustment for prior period errors. Drives payroll processing logic in TempWorks Payroll.

Indicates whether per diem allowances are applicable and tracked within this pay period. When True, per diem expense records are expected to be submitted alongside timesheets. Relevant for travel-intensive placements (e.g., healthcare travel nurses, field technicians) where IRS per diem rates apply.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this pay period across systems (e.g., PP-2024-01-001). Used for cross-system reconciliation between Kronos Workforce Ready, TempWorks Payroll, and Salesforce Revenue Cloud billing cycles.

Human-readable label for the pay period (e.g., Week 1 Jan 2024, Bi-Weekly Period 3 - Q1 2024). Used in payroll reports, worker pay stubs, and client billing statements for clear period identification.

Sequential ordinal number of this pay period within its fiscal year and period type (e.g., 1st bi-weekly period = 1, 2nd = 2). Used for period-over-period comparison analytics, payroll run ordering, and audit trail sequencing.

Current lifecycle status of the pay period. open = timesheets can be submitted; processing = payroll run in progress; locked = no further timesheet changes permitted; closed = payroll finalized and disbursed; cancelled = period voided (rare, e.g., system error). Drives workflow gating in TempWorks Payroll and Kronos Workforce Ready.

Classification of the pay period cycle frequency. Weekly = 52 periods/year; bi_weekly = 26 periods/year; semi_monthly = 24 periods/year; monthly = 12 periods/year. Critical for multi-client operations where different clients may operate on different pay cycles. Drives payroll run scheduling in TempWorks Payroll.

business_glossary_term: Paid Time Off (PTO) Accrual Period Flag
description: Indicates whether PTO (Paid Time Off) accruals are calculated and posted during this pay period. Some staffing arrangements accrue PTO on a per-pay-period basis per state law (e.g., California AB 1522, New York Paid Sick Leave). Drives PTO accrual batch processing in TempWorks Payroll and SAP SuccessFactors.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this pay period definition originated. Kronos Workforce Ready is the primary source for time and attendance pay period calendars; TempWorks Payroll for payroll-specific period definitions; Beeline VMS for contingent workforce program pay cycles. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.

The native identifier of this pay period record in the originating source system (e.g., Kronos pay period ID, TempWorks period key). Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracing, and integration audit trails between the lakehouse silver layer and operational systems.

Expected number of standard straight-time hours for a full-time worker during this pay period (e.g., 40.00 for a weekly period, 80.00 for bi-weekly). Used as the baseline for overtime (OT) threshold calculations, FTE normalization, and payroll burden rate computations.

The first calendar date of the pay period. All timesheet hours submitted with a work date on or after this date are grouped into this pay period. Authoritative boundary for timesheet aggregation and payroll processing.

The IRS tax year (4-digit integer) to which payroll taxes for this period are reported. Typically matches calendar_year but may differ for periods spanning year-end (e.g., a bi-weekly period starting Dec 28 and ending Jan 10). Critical for W-2, 1099, FICA, FUTA, and SUTA tax filing accuracy.

Precise timestamp by which contingent workers must submit their timesheets for the pay period. Includes time-of-day precision to support automated reminder workflows in Kronos Workforce Ready and Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Distinct from payroll_cutoff_date which governs the payroll processing batch.

Total number of calendar days in this pay period (end_date minus start_date plus 1). Used for pro-ration calculations for partial-period placements, per diem allowance calculations, and FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) normalization in workforce analytics.

Number of standard business workdays (Monday–Friday, excluding holidays) within this pay period. Used for expected hours calculations, attendance rate reporting, and workforce scheduling in Kronos Workforce Ready. Supports SLA (Service Level Agreement) headcount compliance reporting.

Timestamp of the most recent update to this pay period record in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance. Updated automatically on every record modification.

Indicates whether this pay period is associated with a VMS (Vendor Management System) managed contingent workforce program (e.g., Beeline VMS). VMS programs may have distinct pay period calendars, approval workflows, and billing cycle alignment requirements separate from direct-client placements.

Indicates whether this pay period spans or falls at the calendar year-end boundary (e.g., a bi-weekly period crossing December 31 into January). When True, special year-end payroll processing rules apply including W-2 cutoff handling, FICA wage base reset, and FUTA/SUTA annual limit resets in TempWorks Payroll.
Unique surrogate identifier for the planned work schedule record in the Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling module. Primary key for the schedule data product.
Reference to the contingent worker placement assignment this schedule belongs to. Links the planned schedule to the active placement record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.

Foreign key linking to candidate.availability. Business justification: Staffing coordinators build schedules directly from a worker's availability record (preferred hours, shift, location). Linking schedule to the availability record that authorized the scheduling enable
Reference to the client organization at whose worksite the scheduled shift is to be performed. Links to the client master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.

Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Shift schedules are built using contracted rate cards; scheduling systems pull bill/pay rates from rate schedules when creating shifts for accurate cost forecasting. Real business process: shift costi

Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Worker schedules are tied to specific client sites — site-based scheduling drives badge requirements, dress code, parking, and location-specific OT rules. A staffing operations expert would expect sch
Reference to the job order (requisition) that originated the placement and governs the scheduled work. Links to the job order record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.

Foreign key linking to timesheet.ot_rule. Business justification: schedule currently has ot_rule_type as STRING and ot_threshold_hours as DECIMAL. Should FK to ot_rule.ot_rule_id for authoritative OT calculation rules including daily/weekly thresholds, multipliers,
Reference to the contingent worker whose planned work schedule is defined by this record. Links to the candidate/worker master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.

Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW workers have schedules tied to their engagement for workforce planning and deliverable timeline management. Workforce managers need schedule→SOW engagement linkage to track scheduled hours against

Reference to the on-site supervisor or manager responsible for overseeing the contingent worker during this scheduled shift. Used for escalation routing, timesheet approval workflows, and client relationship management.

Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Schedule creation must respect wage-hour determinations: OT thresholds, meal break requirements, rest periods, and spread-of-hours rules. Real business process: compliant shift scheduling to avoid vio

Reference to the physical worksite or client facility where the scheduled shift is to be performed. Used for geographic workforce distribution reporting, per diem eligibility determination, and site-level scheduling analytics.

Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Worker classification (W2 temp, 1099 IC, direct hire) determines scheduling constraints — ICs cannot be scheduled with behavioral control, W2 temps have overtime eligibility. Workforce planners buildi

The hourly rate charged to the client for the contingent workers time on this scheduled shift. Used for client invoicing in Salesforce Revenue Cloud and for spread/gross margin calculation. Confidential business financial data.
Indicates whether the contingent worker has acknowledged and confirmed the scheduled shift in Kronos Workforce Ready. Used for workforce readiness tracking and to reduce no-show risk.

Client or internal cost center code to which the scheduled labor hours are to be allocated. Used for client billing segmentation, internal labor cost allocation, and financial reporting in Oracle NetSuite ERP.
The date and time at which this schedule record was first created in the source system (Kronos Workforce Ready). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and schedule lead-time analytics.

The day of the week on which this shift is scheduled. Used for recurring schedule pattern analysis, weekend/weekday differential pay identification, and workforce scheduling analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Client department or business unit code associated with the scheduled shift. Used for client-side labor distribution reporting, workforce analytics, and billing breakdown by department.
The hourly rate charged to the client for double-time (DT) hours on this schedule. Used for accurate client invoicing when DT hours are incurred per the applicable rate agreement.

The hourly compensation rate applicable to double-time (DT) hours, typically triggered after a defined threshold of daily or weekly hours (e.g., 12+ hours/day in certain states). Used for payroll processing in TempWorks Payroll.

The calendar date on which this schedule definition expires or is superseded. Nullable for open-ended schedules that remain in effect until the assignment end date. Used for schedule versioning and historical comparison.

The calendar date from which this schedule definition becomes active and applicable to the workers assignment. Defines the beginning of the schedules validity window. Used for schedule versioning and historical comparison.

Total planned working hours per week for the worker on this assignment schedule. Used for FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) calculation, OT threshold monitoring, and workforce planning. Typically 40 hours for full-time contingent placements.

Indicates whether the hours on this scheduled shift are billable to the client. False for internal training shifts, orientation periods, or non-billable administrative time. Drives client invoicing logic in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.
Indicates whether this scheduled shift falls on a recognized holiday, which may trigger holiday premium pay rates per the client MSA (Master Service Agreement) or worker contract.
Indicates whether the contingent worker is eligible to receive per diem allowance for this scheduled shift based on travel distance, assignment location, and IRS tax home rules.

The native schedule record identifier as assigned by the Kronos Workforce Ready source system. Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage, and re-ingestion deduplication in the Databricks Silver Layer.
The date and time at which this schedule record was most recently updated in the source system. Used for change detection in incremental data loads, audit trail, and schedule modification tracking.

Free-text field for operational notes or special instructions associated with this scheduled shift, such as dress code requirements, access badge instructions, or client-specific directives. Not used for structured data.
The hourly rate charged to the client for overtime (OT) hours worked beyond the standard threshold on this schedule. Used for accurate client invoicing when OT hours are incurred.

The hourly compensation rate applicable to overtime (OT) hours worked beyond the standard threshold (typically 40 hours/week or 8 hours/day) on this schedule. Must comply with FLSA overtime premium requirements (minimum 1.5x regular pay rate).

The hourly compensation rate to be paid to the contingent worker for hours worked on this scheduled shift. Used for payroll processing in TempWorks Payroll and for spread/gross margin calculation. Confidential business financial data.

Daily allowance amount payable to the contingent worker for meals, lodging, or incidental expenses when the assignment requires travel or work away from the workers tax home. Governed by IRS per diem rules. Null if per diem is not applicable to this schedule.

The job title or role designation of the contingent worker for this scheduled shift, as defined in the job order. Used for workforce reporting, skill-based scheduling analytics, and compliance with EEOC job classification requirements.
The calendar date on which the schedule was published and made visible to the contingent worker. Used to measure schedule advance notice compliance and worker communication lead time.
Externally-known business identifier for the schedule record as assigned by Kronos Workforce Ready (e.g., SCH-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference and operational communication.

Current lifecycle state of the planned schedule record. Draft indicates the schedule is being built; published indicates it has been released to the worker; confirmed indicates worker acknowledgement; modified indicates a post-publish change; cancelled indicates the shift was removed; completed indicates the shift period has passed.

Total planned unpaid break duration in minutes for this shift as defined in the schedule template. Used to calculate net scheduled working hours and to validate compliance with mandatory rest period regulations.

Total number of planned working hours for this shift, excluding unpaid break time. Represents the expected billable and payable hours for the shift. Used as the baseline for actual vs. scheduled variance reporting and OT threshold monitoring.

The specific calendar date on which this scheduled shift is to occur. Distinct from shift_start_time in that it provides a day-level grain for schedule reporting, calendar views, and daily workforce planning without requiring timestamp precision.

The planned date and time at which the contingent workers shift is scheduled to end, expressed in local worksite time with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used in conjunction with shift_start_time to derive scheduled shift duration for variance analysis.

The planned date and time at which the contingent workers shift is scheduled to begin, expressed in local worksite time with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the baseline for actual time-entry variance analysis and OT calculation.

Classification of the scheduled shift by time-of-day or day-of-week category. Used for differential pay rate application, OT (Overtime) and DT (Double Time) rule triggering, and workforce analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: day|evening|night|weekend|holiday|on_call — promote to reference product if additional shift types are required]

Identifies the operational system of record from which this schedule record was sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across integrated systems in the Databricks Silver Layer.
The date and time at which the contingent worker confirmed the scheduled shift. Null if the worker has not yet confirmed. Used for advance notice compliance measurement and scheduling analytics.

Unique system-generated identifier for a specific scheduled or worked shift instance for a contingent worker. Primary key for the shift data product in the Kronos Workforce Ready time and attendance system.
Identifier of the active placement or assignment under which this shift is worked. Links the shift to the specific job order and client engagement governing the workers deployment.
Identifier of the client organization at whose worksite this shift is performed. Used for client-level labor reporting, billing, and SLA compliance tracking.

Identifier of the job order (requisition) associated with this shift. Connects the shift to the clients staffing request for labor analytics and billing reconciliation.
Identifier of the payroll pay period to which this shift belongs. Links the shift to the correct payroll processing cycle in TempWorks Payroll for wage calculation and tax filing.

Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_rate. Business justification: Each shift's payable hours must be priced against the authoritative pay_rate record for the assignment. Linking shift.pay_rate_id → payroll.pay_rate enables shift-level payroll cost validation and WC
Identifier of the contingent worker assigned to this shift. Links to the worker/candidate master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM and Kronos Workforce Ready.

Identifier of the parent timesheet record that aggregates this shift along with other shifts in the same submission period. Links shift-level data to the timesheet approval workflow.
Identifier of the specific physical worksite or location where the shift is performed. Supports multi-site client operations, workers compensation jurisdiction assignment, and OSHA compliance.

Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Worker classification governs whether a shift is billable, what overtime rules apply, and whether IC behavioral-control restrictions are triggered. Scheduling and billing systems must reference classi

Code indicating the reason for a missed or partial shift absence (e.g., NCNS for no-call-no-show, SICK, FMLA, PTO, JURY). Used for attendance tracking, FMLA compliance, and workforce reliability analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCNS|SICK|FMLA|PTO|JURY|BEREAVEMENT|MILITARY — promote to reference product]
The actual clock-out date and time recorded when the worker ended the shift. Sourced from Kronos punch data. Used for early departure tracking, schedule variance, and payable hours calculation.
The actual clock-in date and time recorded when the worker began the shift. Sourced from Kronos punch data. Used for tardiness tracking, schedule variance, and payable hours calculation.

Current state of the shift approval workflow. pending = awaiting supervisor review; approved = authorized for payroll and billing; rejected = returned for correction; disputed = worker or client contest; auto_approved = system-approved per configured rules.
Date and time when the shift was approved by the authorized approver. Used for SLA tracking on approval turnaround time and payroll cutoff compliance.

Total hours approved for client invoicing for this shift. May differ from worked_hours due to client-specific billing rules, approved adjustments, or non-billable time. Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud for invoice generation.

Total scheduled or actual break time in minutes deducted from the shift duration. Includes meal breaks and rest periods. Used for net hours calculation and compliance with mandatory break laws (e.g., FLSA, state labor codes).

Client or internal cost center code to which this shifts labor cost is allocated. Supports client-side cost allocation reporting, internal P&L tracking, and Oracle NetSuite ERP general ledger posting.

Date and time when this shift record was first created in the system, either by the scheduling engine or manual entry. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.
Client department or business unit code associated with this shift. Used for granular labor cost reporting to clients and workforce analytics by department.

Code identifying the pay differential applicable to this shift based on timing (e.g., evening, night, weekend, holiday). Drives premium pay rate calculations in TempWorks Payroll and corresponding bill rate adjustments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAY|EVE|NIGHT|WEEKEND|HOLIDAY|HAZARD|ONCALL — promote to reference product]

Hours worked beyond the double-time threshold (typically 12 hours/day or 7th consecutive day per California Labor Code and similar state laws) classified as double-time (DT) at 2x pay rate. Used for payroll and client billing at DT bill rate.

Indicates whether the workers clock-in/clock-out was verified within the approved geofence boundary of the worksite. Supports fraud prevention, buddy-punching detection, and client worksite compliance requirements.
Indicates whether this shift falls on a recognized company or federal holiday. Drives holiday pay premium calculations in TempWorks Payroll and holiday bill rate surcharges for client invoicing.
Indicates whether this shift falls on a Saturday or Sunday. Used to apply weekend shift differential pay codes and corresponding client bill rate adjustments per MSA terms.
Standardized job classification code for the role performed during this shift. Drives pay rate lookup, workers compensation classification, and EEOC/OFCCP labor category reporting.
Date and time when this shift record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest change for data lineage, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Free-text field for supervisor or worker notes related to this shift, such as reasons for schedule deviation, special instructions, incident observations, or client-specific comments. Used for operational context and dispute resolution.

Hours worked beyond the standard threshold (typically 40 hours/week or 8 hours/day depending on state law) classified as overtime (OT) at 1.5x pay rate. Critical for FLSA compliance, payroll cost management, and client billing at OT bill rate.

Total hours approved for worker compensation for this shift. May differ from worked_hours due to guaranteed minimum hours, rounding rules, or approved adjustments. Feeds TempWorks Payroll for worker pay calculation.

Dollar amount of per diem allowance payable to the worker for this shift. Applicable for travel assignments and remote deployments. Sourced from assignment-level per diem rate configuration. Feeds TempWorks Payroll.

Indicates whether the worker is eligible to receive a per diem allowance for this shift (e.g., for travel assignments, remote worksite deployments). Drives per diem payment processing in TempWorks Payroll.

Method or system through which the shifts time punches were captured. time_clock = physical Kronos terminal; mobile_app = Kronos mobile; web_portal = browser entry; supervisor_entry = manager-entered; vms = Beeline VMS submission; manual_adjustment = back-office correction.
Portion of worked hours classified as straight-time regular pay hours, not subject to overtime or double-time premiums. Used for payroll processing and client billing at the standard pay/bill rate.

Difference in minutes between actual worked hours and scheduled hours for this shift (actual minus scheduled). Positive values indicate overtime beyond schedule; negative values indicate early departure or missed time. Key metric for schedule adherence and labor analytics.
The planned end date and time of the shift as defined in the workforce schedule. Used alongside scheduled_start_time to determine planned shift duration and schedule adherence.

Total planned hours for the shift as defined in the workforce schedule, calculated from scheduled_start_time to scheduled_end_time minus planned break time. Used for headcount planning and FTE calculations.
The planned start date and time of the shift as defined in the workforce schedule. Used for schedule adherence analysis, variance calculation, and dispatch confirmation.

The calendar date on which the shift is scheduled or was worked. Serves as the principal business event date for the shift, used for daily labor reporting, payroll period alignment, and client billing.

Externally-known business identifier for this shift instance, typically generated by Kronos Workforce Ready. Used for cross-system reference, timesheet reconciliation, and worker/supervisor communication.

Current lifecycle state of the shift. scheduled = future shift confirmed; in_progress = worker clocked in; completed = shift ended and hours recorded; missed = worker did not appear; cancelled = shift removed before start; no_call_no_show = worker absent without notification.

Classification of the shift by its operational nature. regular = standard scheduled shift; overtime = OT-designated shift; holiday = worked on a recognized holiday; on_call = standby/on-call shift; split = non-contiguous shift segments; per_diem = per diem eligible shift.

Difference in minutes between actual_start_time and scheduled_start_time (actual minus scheduled). Positive values indicate tardiness; negative values indicate early arrival. Used for punctuality tracking and SLA compliance.

Total actual hours worked during the shift, calculated from actual_start_time to actual_end_time minus actual break time. Represents the gross worked duration before OT/DT classification. Feeds payroll and client billing.

NCCI or state-specific workers compensation classification code applicable to the work performed during this shift. Determines workers comp insurance premium calculation and is required for accurate burden rate costing.

The agreed-upon rate charged to the client per unit (hour, day, or project) for the specified job category and skill level. This is the authoritative rate used for invoice generation. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.

Current workflow state of the bill rate record. Pending = awaiting authorization; Approved = active and usable for invoicing; Rejected = declined by approver; Expired = past expiration_date; Superseded = replaced by a newer rate version.
Name or identifier of the internal manager or executive who authorized this bill rate. Required for audit trail and SOC 2 Type II compliance controls on financial data.
Date and time when the bill rate was formally approved. Provides a precise audit trail for rate authorization events, supporting SOC 2 Type II and internal financial controls.

The commercial billing model under which this rate is applied. Determines invoice generation logic: Time-and-Materials (T&M) bills actuals; Per Diem bills daily flat rates; Project-Based bills milestones; SOW (Statement of Work)-Based bills deliverables; Direct Placement bills one-time placement fees; Temp-to-Perm bills conversion fees.

Indicates whether employer burden costs (FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation, benefits) are factored into the bill rate amount. When True, the bill rate is all-inclusive; when False, burden is billed separately or absorbed by the staffing firm.
The employer burden rate percentage applied to the pay rate, covering FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation, and benefits costs. Used to calculate true gross margin when burden_included is True.
Date and time when this bill rate record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the record lifecycle, required for SOC 2 Type II compliance.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the bill rate amount (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Required for multi-currency client engagements and international staffing operations.
The negotiated bill rate for double-time hours (holiday, weekend, or contractually defined premium hours). Applied when timesheet entries are classified as DT per the client MSA terms.
The date from which this bill rate becomes active and applicable for invoice generation. Supports rate versioning and ensures correct rate application across the engagement lifecycle.
The date after which this bill rate is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended rate with no defined expiry. Used to enforce rate card validity windows and trigger rate renegotiation workflows.

business_glossary_term: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Exempt Flag
description: Indicates whether the job category associated with this bill rate is classified as FLSA-exempt (True) or non-exempt (False). Exempt positions are not subject to overtime billing rules; non-exempt positions trigger OT bill rates beyond 40 hours.

Gross margin expressed as a percentage of the bill rate: ((Bill Rate - Pay Rate - Burden) / Bill Rate) * 100. Represents the profitability of the engagement after direct labor costs. Key KPI for financial reporting and Power BI dashboards.

The percentage markup applied over the pay rate to arrive at the bill rate, expressed as ((Bill Rate - Pay Rate) / Pay Rate) * 100. Used for rate card negotiations, competitive benchmarking, and profitability analysis.
The ceiling rate above which this bill rate cannot be set, as defined by the client rate card or VMS program constraints. Used in rate cap enforcement for MSP/VMS-managed programs.

The floor rate below which this bill rate cannot be negotiated, as defined by the rate card or internal pricing policy. Enforces minimum margin thresholds during rate negotiations and VMS submissions.

Free-text field for capturing additional context about this bill rate, such as special negotiation terms, client-specific exceptions, or rate justification notes. Supports account management and audit review.

The negotiated bill rate for overtime hours (hours exceeding the FLSA standard 40-hour workweek threshold). Stored separately from the standard rate to support accurate invoice generation for OT hours on timesheets.

The corresponding pay rate for the worker associated with this bill rate. Stored alongside the bill rate to enable real-time spread and gross margin calculations without joining to the payroll domain.

Daily flat-rate allowance billed to the client for travel, lodging, or subsistence expenses associated with the worker assignment. Applicable for travel-intensive placements and project-based engagements.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this bill rate record within the rate card or billing system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Salesforce Revenue Cloud and Oracle NetSuite ERP.
Human-readable label for this bill rate record (e.g., Senior Java Developer - Standard, Registered Nurse - OT Rate). Used in rate card displays, client-facing documents, and reporting.

Indicates the origin of this bill rate: Manual = entered directly by a recruiter or account manager; VMS Imported = sourced from Beeline VMS; Rate Card = derived from the client rate card; MSA Negotiated = established during MSA contract execution; Market Benchmark = set based on SIA market data.

Classification of the bill rate by pay period or billing model. Standard = regular hours; OT (Overtime) = hours beyond FLSA threshold; DT (Double Time) = premium holiday/weekend rate; Per Diem = daily flat allowance; Blended = composite rate across multiple rate types; Project = fixed-fee project billing.

Sequential version number for this bill rate record, incremented each time the rate is renegotiated or updated. Supports rate history tracking and ensures the correct version is applied to invoices within the effective date range.

The experience or seniority tier for which this bill rate is negotiated. Drives rate differentiation within a job category on the client rate card. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry|junior|mid|senior|lead|principal|executive — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The native record identifier from the originating system of record (Salesforce Revenue Cloud or Oracle NetSuite ERP). Enables cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the Databricks Silver Layer.

The gross spread calculated as bill rate minus pay rate (Bill Rate - Pay Rate = Spread). Represents the raw revenue contribution before burden costs. A core profitability metric in staffing operations tracked per ASA and SIA standards.
Date and time when this bill rate record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.
Two-letter US state code for the work location associated with this bill rate. Drives state-specific tax, SUTA, and Workers Compensation rate calculations that affect gross margin.

The NCCI (National Council on Compensation Insurance) workers compensation class code applicable to this bill rates job category. Determines the workers comp premium rate included in burden calculations.
Unique surrogate identifier for the billing account record in the Staffing Hr data lakehouse. Primary key for the billing_account product.

Legal or trade name of the client entity to which invoices are issued. Reflects the billing entity name as it appears on invoices and AR records. May differ from the CRM client profile name if the billing entity is a subsidiary or parent.

Externally-known, human-readable billing account number assigned to the clients financial billing entity. Used on invoices, statements, and AR correspondence. Sourced from Salesforce Revenue Cloud or Oracle NetSuite ERP customer record.

Current lifecycle state of the billing account. Controls whether new invoices can be generated and whether payments are being accepted. on_hold indicates a credit or collections hold. suspended indicates a temporary freeze pending resolution.

Classification of the billing account by the nature of the client engagement model. Drives billing rules, markup structures, and invoice formats. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_client|msp_program|vms_program|peo_client|eor_client|rpo_client — promote to reference product]
Primary street address line of the billing entity. Used on invoice headers, statements, and AR correspondence. Must match the legal billing address on the Master Service Agreement (MSA).
Secondary address line for the billing entity (suite, floor, department, PO Box). Supplementary to billing_address_line1 for complete invoice address formatting.
City of the billing entitys legal billing address. Used on invoice headers and for state/local tax jurisdiction determination.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the billing entitys legal billing address (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Determines currency, tax regime, and regulatory compliance requirements including GDPR for international clients.

Frequency at which invoices are generated and issued to this billing account. Aligns with the timesheet submission and payroll cycle cadence. Weekly is most common for temporary staffing; monthly for SOW and project-based engagements.

Primary billing model applied to this account. Determines how invoices are calculated and structured. time_and_materials is standard for temporary staffing (hourly bill rate × hours). sow_based applies to Statement of Work (SOW) engagements. direct_placement_fee applies to permanent placement.
ZIP or postal code of the billing entitys legal billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction lookup and invoice address formatting.
Two-letter US state or territory code of the billing entitys legal billing address. Drives state tax applicability, SUTA jurisdiction, and invoice compliance requirements.

Indicates whether multiple job orders or work locations should be consolidated into a single invoice per billing cycle, rather than generating separate invoices per placement or location. Common for enterprise MSP clients.
Timestamp when the billing account record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail creation event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Maximum outstanding AR balance (in account currency) that Staffing Hr will extend to this billing account before requiring payment or placing the account on hold. Enforced during invoice generation and placement approval workflows.

Current credit standing of the billing account as assessed by the AR/credit team. Determines whether new placements and invoices can be extended to this client. probationary indicates reduced credit terms are in effect.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which invoices are denominated and payments are received for this billing account (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Drives multi-currency AR processing and FX conversion in Oracle NetSuite ERP.

Number of days from invoice date within which the client must pay to qualify for the early payment discount percentage. For example, 10 in a 2/10 Net 30 arrangement. Null if no early payment discount applies.

Percentage discount offered to the client if payment is received within the early payment discount window (e.g., 0.0200 = 2% discount). Supports 2/10 Net 30 and similar early payment incentive structures common in staffing billing.

Date on which the billing account became active and eligible to receive invoices. Marks the start of the billing relationship with this client entity. Used for AR aging calculations and contract alignment.

The clients own internal account or vendor number assigned to Staffing Hr within the clients ERP or VMS system (e.g., Beeline VMS supplier ID, client SAP vendor number). Used for cross-system reconciliation and remittance matching.
Oracle NetSuite ERP General Ledger (GL) account code to which AR transactions for this billing account are posted. Ensures correct revenue and receivables classification in the financial statements.

Preferred method by which invoices are delivered to the clients AR department. vms_portal indicates delivery through the clients Vendor Management System (VMS) such as Beeline. edi indicates Electronic Data Interchange transmission.
