Full name of the primary on-site contact person (e.g., hiring manager, facility supervisor) responsible for coordinating worker assignments, timesheets, and day-to-day staffing operations at this location.
Direct phone number for the primary on-site contact. Used by staffing coordinators for dispatch coordination, worker check-in issues, and urgent placement communications.

Human-readable name of the physical or virtual work site (e.g., Chicago Distribution Center, Austin HQ – Tower 2, Remote – Pacific Time Zone). Used in assignment dispatch, job orders, and worker communications.

Two-letter ISO 3166-2 state or province code where the work site is located. Drives workers comp classification, SUTA (State Unemployment Tax Act) assignment, FLSA overtime rules, and OSHA jurisdiction.

IANA time zone identifier for the work site (e.g., America/Chicago, America/Los_Angeles). Used by Kronos Workforce Ready for shift scheduling, timesheet cutoff calculations, and overtime rule application.

Timestamp of the most recent update to this location record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and Silver layer incremental processing.

NCCI (National Council on Compensation Insurance) four-digit classification code assigned to the work site based on the nature of work performed. Determines workers comp premium rates for temporary workers placed at this location.

Two-letter state code that governs the workers compensation insurance policy for workers deployed at this site. May differ from the physical state for multi-state operations or border locations. Critical for TempWorks Payroll workers comp classification.
Unique surrogate identifier for the rate card record in the Staffing Hr lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the client account for whom this rate card is negotiated. Links the rate card to the client master record in the client domain.

business_glossary_term: Client Approved By Contact Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Rate cards have client_approved_by field storing approver name as string. Adding contact_id FK normalizes this to reference the authoritative contact record who approved the rate card. One rate card a

Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Rate cards have location_state and location_country fields indicating geographic applicability. Adding location_id FK normalizes this to reference the authoritative location record, enabling location-

Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: Rate cards can be associated with managed programs (RPO/MSP) in addition to VMS programs. This allows tracking negotiated rates specific to managed workforce programs. One rate card for one managed pr

Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Rate cards reference MSA terms for markup caps, payment terms, and liability provisions. Essential for rate card approval workflow and compliance verification. Replaces denormalized msa_reference_numb

Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Rate cards often tied to specific SOWs for project-based staffing engagements. Required for SOW-specific pricing enforcement, billing validation, and scope-of-work rate compliance in statement-of-work

External identifier of the VMS program (e.g., Beeline program code) under which this rate card is managed. Used to reconcile rate card data between the Staffing Hr lakehouse and the clients VMS platform.

Current workflow state of the rate card in the approval lifecycle. Controls whether the rate card may be used for job order creation and invoice generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|rejected|expired|superseded — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]
Name or identifier of the internal approver (e.g., Account Manager, VP of Sales) who authorized this rate card version. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.
Date on which the rate card was formally approved by the authorized approver. Used for audit trail, compliance reporting, and rate card lifecycle management.
Native rate card identifier from the Beeline VMS system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Staffing Hr lakehouse and the clients Beeline VMS program.
Negotiated hourly bill rate charged to the client for double-time (DT) hours, applicable in jurisdictions or client agreements requiring double-time pay (e.g., California labor law, holiday work).
Negotiated hourly bill rate charged to the client for overtime (OT) hours, typically hours worked beyond 40 per week. Governed by FLSA overtime provisions and client MSA terms.
Negotiated hourly bill rate charged to the client for straight-time (regular) hours worked. This is the primary pricing field used in invoice generation and revenue recognition.

Indicates whether the employer burden costs (FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation, benefits) are already embedded in the bill rate or markup percentage. True = burden is included in the stated rates; False = burden is calculated separately.

Employer burden rate as a percentage of pay rate, covering statutory costs including FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation, and applicable benefits. Used when burden_rate_included is True to document the embedded cost component.

Negotiated fee percentage charged to the client when a temporary or contract worker is converted to a permanent (direct hire) employee (Temp-to-Perm conversion). Expressed as a percentage of the workers annualized salary.
Timestamp when this rate card record was first created in the Staffing Hr lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 compliance requirements.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary rate values on this rate card are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency client programs.
Negotiated fee percentage charged to the client for direct hire (permanent placement) engagements under this rate card. Expressed as a percentage of the placed candidates first-year base salary.
Date on which the rate card becomes active and may be applied to job orders, submittals, and invoices. Aligns with the MSA or VMS program agreement start date.
Date on which the rate card ceases to be valid for new transactions. After this date, a new version must be approved before job orders can reference this rate schedule.
Multiplier applied to the regular bill rate for hours worked on client-designated holidays (e.g., 1.5 = time-and-a-half, 2.0 = double time). Null if no holiday premium is negotiated.

Negotiated markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 45.00 = 45%). Captures the agreed margin structure for this job category and skill level.
Ceiling bill rate above which the staffing firm may not price a submittal for this job category and skill level. Enforces client budget caps and VMS program rate compliance.
Floor bill rate below which the staffing firm may not price a submittal for this job category and skill level. Enforces margin floor compliance during job order creation and candidate pricing.
Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or exceptions associated with this rate card line (e.g., client-specific rate exceptions, program notes, negotiation history summaries).

Recommended or maximum hourly pay rate for the worker under this rate card line. Used as a pricing guardrail during candidate submittal to ensure spread and margin targets are maintained. Not a contractual pay commitment.

Daily allowance rate for travel, lodging, and meals applicable to workers on this rate card line when deployed away from their primary work location. Null if per diem is not applicable for this engagement.
Human-readable name or label for the rate card, typically describing the client program, engagement type, or labor category scope (e.g., Acme Corp IT Contingent 2024).
Externally-known business identifier for the rate card, used in client communications, VMS integrations, and invoice references. Sourced from Beeline VMS or Salesforce Revenue Cloud.

business_glossary_term: Salesforce Revenue Cloud Price Book ID
description: Native price book entry identifier from Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse rate card and the Salesforce billing and invoicing configuration.

business_glossary_term: Spread Amount (Bill Rate Minus Pay Rate)
description: Calculated difference between the regular bill rate and the pay rate guidance (Spread = Bill Rate - Pay Rate). Represents the gross dollar margin per hour before burden costs. Stored for operational reference and reporting; not recomputed at query time.
Timestamp when this rate card record was last modified in the Staffing Hr lakehouse. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data freshness monitoring.
Sequential version number of the rate card, incremented each time the rate schedule is renegotiated or amended. Enables tracking of rate card history and supersession.
Unique identifier for the managed workforce program record. Primary key.
Reference to the client account that owns this managed program.

Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: A managed program (RPO, MSP, or hybrid) has a designated primary client-side contact who serves as the program sponsor or relationship owner. Linking managed_program to client_contact via client_conta

Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Managed programs governed by MSAs that define program scope, SLA terms, and fee structures. Essential for program governance, rate card inheritance, and SLA enforcement. Replaces denormalized msa_refe
Self-referencing FK on managed_program (parent_managed_program_id)

Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Managed programs often have specific SOWs defining deliverables, headcount commitments, and payment terms. Required for scope management, deliverable tracking, and billing reconciliation. Replaces den
Current number of active worker placements under this managed program.
Current number of active job requisitions under this managed program.
Estimated annual staffing spend for this managed program in the program currency.
Indicates whether background checks are mandatory for all placements under this managed program.
Fee percentage charged when a temporary worker is converted to permanent employment under this managed program.
Timestamp when this managed program record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for financial transactions under this managed program.

business_glossary_term: Data Privacy Addendum On File Flag
description: Indicates whether a data privacy addendum (GDPR, CCPA, or other) is on file for this managed program.
Fee percentage of first-year salary charged for direct placement services under this managed program.
Indicates whether drug screening is mandatory for all placements under this managed program.
Date when the managed program becomes active and binding.
Indicates whether an Employer of Record arrangement is required for workers under this managed program.
Indicates whether E-Verify employment eligibility verification is mandatory for all placements under this managed program.
Date when the managed program contract ends. Nullable for open-ended programs.
Defines the decision-making and oversight structure for the managed program.
Indicates whether independent contractors (1099 workers) are permitted under this managed program.
Date of the most recent formal performance review or QBR for this managed program.
Maximum allowable markup percentage (bill rate minus pay rate divided by pay rate) for suppliers participating in this managed program.
Timestamp when this managed program record was last modified.
Management fee percentage charged by the MSP for program administration and oversight.
Indicates whether a signed Non-Disclosure Agreement is on file for this managed program.
Scheduled date for the next formal performance review or QBR for this managed program.
Free-text notes and additional context about the managed program.
Indicates whether the managed program requires a dedicated on-site coordinator at the client location.
Frequency of formal performance reviews and Quarterly Business Reviews (QBR) for this managed program.
Human-readable name of the managed workforce program.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the managed program, used in client communications and contracts.
Detailed description of the services, job categories, and geographic coverage included in the managed program.
Current lifecycle status of the managed program.

Classification of the managed program model. RPO (Recruitment Process Outsourcing), MSP (Managed Service Provider), Hybrid (combination), VMS (Vendor Management System), or On-Site (dedicated on-site team).

Method by which requisitions are distributed to suppliers in the managed program. Waterfall (sequential by tier), Broadcast (all suppliers simultaneously), Hybrid (combination), or Manual (case-by-case).
Service level tier defining performance targets and response commitments for the managed program.
Number of supplier tiers configured in the managed program for requisition routing and vendor management.
Target percentage of requisitions successfully filled under this managed program, as defined in the SLA.
Target percentage of submitted candidates that meet client quality standards, as defined in the SLA.
Target number of days to fill a requisition under this managed program, as defined in the SLA.
Target number of days from offer acceptance to candidate start date under this managed program, as defined in the SLA.
Reason for termination or suspension of the managed program, if applicable.

Unique surrogate identifier for the program supplier association record. Primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer. Entity role: JUNCTION (extended) — links a staffing supplier/vendor to a specific VMS program and carries its own participation terms, tier assignments, and performance metrics.
Reference to the client account that owns and manages this VMS contingent workforce program. Establishes the demand-side context for the supplier participation record.

Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Suppliers enrolled in a managed program must comply with a specific credential package defining BGC, drug screen, and training requirements for their workers. MSP program management requires linking e

Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: Suppliers can participate in managed programs (RPO/MSP) in addition to VMS programs. This allows tracking supplier participation across both VMS and managed program types. One supplier association to

Foreign key linking to contract.nda. Business justification: Supplier NDA compliance per program enrollment is a standard staffing procurement requirement. Normalizing nda_on_file (boolean) to a direct NDA FK enables expiration tracking, document retrieval, and

Reference to the staffing supplier/vendor master record in the vendor domain. Vendor identity and master data are owned by the vendor domain; this product owns only the program-specific participation terms.

Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Each supplier enrolled in a managed program operates under a specific vendor agreement governing rates, compliance, and terms. Normalizing replaces denormalized msa_on_file/msa_effective_date/msa_expi
Reference to the VMS contingent workforce program this supplier is enrolled in. Links to the client-managed program record within the Beeline VMS platform.

Current number of active worker assignments placed by this supplier within the program at the time of the last data refresh. Provides a real-time headcount view of the suppliers workforce contribution to the program. Sourced from Beeline VMS.

Comma-delimited list of job category codes or names that this supplier is authorized to fill within the program (e.g., IT, Finance, Engineering, Light Industrial). Restricts the suppliers submission eligibility to approved labor categories only.

Average number of calendar days from requisition release to candidate placement for this supplier within the program. A core SLA and KPI metric used to evaluate supplier responsiveness and efficiency. Sourced from Beeline VMS supplier scorecard.

business_glossary_term: Beeline Vendor Management System (VMS) Supplier Program ID
description: The externally-known unique identifier assigned by Beeline VMS to this supplier-program enrollment record. Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration with the source VMS platform.

Certification and compliance status of the supplier within the program. Reflects whether the supplier has met all program-required compliance obligations including insurance certificates, diversity certifications, background check protocols, I-9 compliance, and contractual requirements.

The timestamp when this program supplier association record was first created in the system. Represents the audit creation time in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary rate fields for this supplier-program record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency VMS programs for multinational clients.

The specific type of diversity certification held by this supplier, if applicable. Used for OFCCP compliance reporting and corporate diversity spend analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MBE|WBE|SDVOSB|HUBZone|LGBTBE|DVBE|none — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]

Indicates whether this supplier holds a recognized diversity certification (e.g., MBE, WBE, SDVOSB, HUBZone). Many enterprise VMS programs track diversity supplier participation for OFCCP compliance and corporate diversity spend reporting.
The date on which the supplier was formally enrolled and activated within the VMS program. Marks the effective start of the suppliers participation terms and eligibility to receive requisitions.

The date on which the suppliers participation in the VMS program is scheduled to end or was terminated. Null for open-ended enrollments. Used for contract lifecycle management and program renewal tracking.

Percentage of placements made by this supplier within the program that ended prematurely (before the scheduled assignment end date). High fall-off rates indicate poor candidate quality or fit. Used in tier review and supplier performance management.

Percentage of requisitions assigned to this supplier that were successfully filled within the program period. Calculated as filled requisitions divided by total requisitions routed to the supplier. Key KPI for supplier performance evaluation and tier assignment.
The expiration date of the suppliers most recently verified insurance certificate on file for this program. Used to trigger renewal alerts and compliance reviews before coverage lapses.

Indicates whether the suppliers required insurance certificates (general liability, workers compensation, professional liability) have been verified and are current within the program. Required for program participation and compliance.
The date of the most recent formal performance review conducted for this supplier within the program. Used to track review cadence and ensure timely tier reassessment based on performance metrics.

The maximum markup percentage this supplier is permitted to apply above the pay rate when billing within this program. Enforced by the VMS platform at submission time. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 35.00 = 35%). Represents the Spread/Markup ceiling negotiated for this supplier within the program.

The maximum bill rate (in the programs currency) that this supplier is authorized to submit for any candidate within this program. Acts as a rate ceiling enforced by the VMS platform at submission time. Distinct from the markup cap percentage.
The scheduled date for the next formal performance review of this supplier within the program. Supports proactive program management and ensures regular supplier evaluation cadence.

Indicates whether the supplier has completed all required onboarding steps for this program, including document submission, compliance verification, system access provisioning, and training acknowledgments. Prerequisite for receiving live requisitions.

Composite performance score for this supplier within the program, calculated by the VMS platform based on fill rate, time-to-fill (TTF), quality of submission (QoS), assignment completion rate, and fall-off rate. Typically expressed on a 0–100 scale. Used for tier review and program renewal decisions.

The worker classification type(s) this supplier primarily places within the program (W-2 employees, 1099 independent contractors, Corp-to-Corp, or Employer of Record). Relevant for IRS worker classification compliance and co-employment risk management.
Email address of the primary contact person at the supplier organization for this program. Used for requisition notifications, compliance alerts, and program communications via Beeline VMS.

Full name of the primary contact person at the supplier organization responsible for managing this program relationship. This is the supplier-side account manager or program coordinator for the VMS engagement.

Numeric sequence position determining the order in which this supplier receives requisition notifications within the program tier. Lower numbers receive requisitions first. Used to implement tiered routing logic (e.g., preferred suppliers receive reqs before approved suppliers).

business_glossary_term: Response Time Service Level Agreement (SLA) Hours
description: The maximum number of hours within which this supplier is contractually required to respond to a new requisition notification within the program. Defines the SLA commitment for supplier responsiveness. Tracked against actual response times for SLA compliance reporting.

Maximum number of candidate submittals this supplier is allowed to make per open requisition within the program. Controls Quality of Submission (QoS) and prevents requisition flooding. Enforced by Beeline VMS at the time of submittal.

Current participation status of the supplier within this specific VMS program. Active suppliers can receive and respond to requisitions; suspended suppliers are temporarily blocked; removed suppliers have been permanently disqualified from the program.

Tier classification of the supplier within the VMS program, determining routing priority for requisitions. Preferred suppliers receive first-look on new requisitions; approved suppliers are standard participants; backup suppliers are used when preferred/approved cannot fill; probationary suppliers are under performance review.

The date on which the supplier was suspended or removed from the program. Populated when supplier_status transitions to suspended or removed. Used for compliance audit trails and program governance reporting.

Free-text description of the reason for supplier suspension or removal from the program, if applicable. Populated when supplier_status is suspended or removed. Supports audit trail and program governance documentation.

Total number of candidate submittals made by this supplier within the program in the current calendar year. Used to assess supplier engagement level and submission volume relative to program activity.

The timestamp when this program supplier association record was most recently modified. Tracks the last update event for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

The supplier code or identifier assigned by the clients VMS platform (Beeline) to this supplier within the program. Used as the operational reference key in VMS-generated reports, requisition routing, and timesheet approvals.
Unique surrogate identifier for this rate card line item record

business_glossary_term: Rate Card Line - Rate Card Id
description: Foreign key linking to the client rate card under which this pricing line is defined

business_glossary_term: Rate Card Line - Job Category Id
description: Foreign key linking to the job category for which this rate card line establishes pricing
Negotiated hourly bill rate charged to the client for double-time hours for this job category under this rate card
Negotiated hourly bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours for this job category under this rate card
Negotiated hourly bill rate charged to the client for straight-time hours for this job category under this rate card
Indicates whether employer burden costs are included in the bill rate for this job category under this rate card
Employer burden rate as a percentage of pay rate for this job category under this rate card
Negotiated fee percentage charged when a temporary worker in this job category converts to permanent under this rate card
Negotiated fee percentage charged for direct hire placement in this job category under this rate card
Date on which this rate card line becomes active and may be applied to job orders and submittals for this job category

Classification of the staffing engagement model for which this rate applies. Distinguishes between contract/temporary, temp-to-perm (Contract-to-Hire), direct placement, Statement of Work (SOW), and per diem arrangements. [Moved from rate_card: Engagement type (contract, temp-to-perm, direct hire) varies by job category line within a rate card. A single rate card can have different engagement types for different job categories, so this belongs in the association.]
Date on which this rate card line ceases to be valid for new transactions for this job category

Specific job title or position designation for which this rate card line applies (e.g., Software Engineer, Data Analyst, Forklift Operator). Drives rate lookup during job order creation and candidate submittal pricing. [Moved from rate_card: Job title is specific to the job category being priced, not to the rate card as a whole. This belongs in the association or should be derived from the job_category reference.]
Negotiated markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate for this job category under this rate card
Ceiling bill rate above which the staffing firm may not price a submittal for this job category under this rate card
Floor bill rate below which the staffing firm may not price a submittal for this job category under this rate card

Number of hours per week (or per day, per overtime_threshold_period) after which overtime bill rates apply. Defaults to 40 hours per week per FLSA; may differ for daily OT jurisdictions (e.g., California 8-hour daily OT rule). [Moved from rate_card: Overtime threshold may vary by job category within a rate card (e.g., exempt vs. non-exempt roles) and belongs in the association.]

Period over which the overtime threshold hours are measured. daily applies in jurisdictions with daily OT rules (e.g., California); weekly applies under standard FLSA rules. [Moved from rate_card: Overtime threshold period may vary by job category within a rate card and belongs in the association.]
Recommended or maximum hourly pay rate for workers in this job category under this rate card, guiding recruiter compensation negotiations
Daily allowance rate for travel, lodging, and meals applicable to workers in this job category under this rate card

Experience or seniority tier associated with the job title on this rate card line. Differentiates rate tiers within the same job title (e.g., Junior vs. Senior Software Engineer). [Moved from rate_card: Skill level is specific to the job category line item within the rate card, not to the rate card header. This attribute describes the pricing tier for a specific job category and belongs in the association.]
Calculated difference between the regular bill rate and the pay rate guidance for this job category under this rate card

Tax and employment classification of the worker under this rate card line. Determines payroll tax treatment, burden rate applicability, and compliance obligations. W-2 (employee), 1099 (independent contractor), Corp-to-Corp, or Employer of Record (EOR). [Moved from rate_card: Worker classification (W2, 1099, etc.) is specific to the job category line item, not the rate card header. Different job categories within the same rate card may have different worker classifications.]

Unique surrogate primary key for each SLA measurement record. Identifies a single periodic performance measurement instance against a defined SLA configuration. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete business event capturing actual vs. target SLA performance for a reporting window.

Reference to the client (employer) account for whom this SLA measurement is being evaluated. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE linking this measurement to the demand-side client entity in the client domain.

Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: SLA measurements can apply to managed programs (RPO/MSP) in addition to VMS programs. This allows tracking SLA performance for managed workforce programs. One measurement for one managed program.
Reference to the SLA configuration record that defines the metric type, target thresholds, and measurement rules governing this measurement. Links the measurement to its governing SLA definition.

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: SLA measurements frequently track supplier-specific metrics (fill rate, time-to-fill, quality of submission) within VMS/MSP programs. Linking measurements to suppliers enables performance reporting, s

The program identifier from the Beeline VMS or equivalent VMS platform for clients operating under a VMS/MSP (Managed Service Provider) program. Enables correlation of SLA measurements with VMS-tracked requisitions and submittals.

A composite numeric score (0–100) representing the overall health of the client account based on this measurement periods SLA performance. Derived from weighted SLA compliance results and used for account risk segmentation and QBR preparation.

The contractual bonus dollar amount earned by over-performance in this measurement period, as calculated per the bonus formula in the SLA configuration. Populated only when bonus_triggered is True. Used for billing adjustments and incentive tracking.

Boolean flag indicating whether the SLA over-performance in this measurement period has triggered a contractual bonus or incentive clause. True = bonus applies; False = no bonus. Drives positive billing adjustments in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.

Indicates whether a higher or lower measured value represents better performance for this metric type. higher_is_better applies to fill_rate, qos, retention_rate; lower_is_better applies to TTF and TTS (fewer days is better). Required to correctly interpret variance sign for compliance determination.
The timestamp when this SLA measurement record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to penalty_amount and bonus_amount fields (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency client engagements in international staffing operations.

Free-text description of the clients stated reason for disputing this SLA measurement result. Populated only when measurement_status is disputed. Supports dispute resolution workflows and MSA compliance documentation.

The date on which a disputed SLA measurement was formally resolved between the staffing firm and the client. Populated only when a dispute has been closed. Used to track dispute resolution cycle times and SLA governance compliance.

Number of requisitions excluded from the SLA measurement scope due to agreed exclusion criteria (e.g., client holds, force majeure, late job order submissions). Provides transparency into the effective denominator used in SLA calculations.

Describes any agreed-upon exclusions applied to the measurement scope (e.g., client-caused delays, force majeure, requisitions excluded per MSA terms). Populated when certain requisitions or placements were removed from the SLA calculation denominator.

Number of placements that ended prematurely (fall-offs) during the measurement period before the expected assignment end date. High fall-off counts negatively impact retention rate SLA metrics and may trigger penalty clauses.

Number of requisitions that were successfully filled (placement confirmed) within the measurement period. Used as the numerator in fill rate calculations and as a direct performance indicator for talent acquisition effectiveness.

The actual fill rate for the measurement period expressed as a percentage (0–100), calculated as fill_count divided by req_count multiplied by 100. Measures the staffing firms ability to fulfill client demand within the period.

The date on which this SLA measurement record was locked and finalized for reporting. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for the finalization lifecycle event. Once finalized, the record is immutable for audit and billing purposes.

Boolean flag indicating whether the measured performance met or exceeded the SLA target for this measurement period. True = SLA met (compliant); False = SLA missed (breach). Primary compliance indicator used in account health scoring and QBR reporting.

The actual observed performance value for the SLA metric during the measurement period. Interpretation depends on sla_metric_type: for TTF/TTS this is average calendar days; for fill_rate/qos/retention_rate this is a percentage (0–100); for submittal_ratio this is a ratio. This is the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT for this transaction.

Free-text field for account managers or operations staff to document contextual notes about this measurement record, such as explanations for SLA misses, data quality caveats, client-agreed exclusions, or force majeure circumstances affecting performance.

Externally-known business identifier for this measurement record, used in client-facing QBR (Quarterly Business Review) reports, penalty/bonus calculations, and audit trails. Format: SLA-MEAS-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.

The operational system of record from which the measurement data was sourced. Identifies data lineage for audit and reconciliation purposes. manual indicates data was entered directly by an account manager outside of automated system feeds.

Current lifecycle state of the SLA measurement record. draft indicates data is being collected; pending_review awaits account manager sign-off; finalized is locked for reporting; disputed is under client challenge; voided has been invalidated.

The contractual penalty dollar amount triggered by this SLA breach, as calculated per the penalty formula in the SLA configuration. Populated only when penalty_triggered is True. Used for billing credit adjustments and financial reporting.

Boolean flag indicating whether the SLA breach in this measurement period has triggered a contractual penalty clause as defined in the MSA or SLA addendum. True = penalty applies; False = no penalty. Drives billing adjustments in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.

The last calendar date of the reporting window for which SLA performance is being measured. Defines the close of the measurement interval and determines which requisitions, placements, and timesheets fall within scope.

The first calendar date of the reporting window for which SLA performance is being measured. Combined with period_end_date, defines the exact scope of the measurement interval (weekly, monthly, quarterly, etc.).
Categorizes the cadence of the measurement window. Determines how frequently SLA compliance is evaluated and reported to the client. Drives QBR preparation and penalty/bonus calculation cycles.

Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement record falls within a QBR reporting period and should be included in the formal Quarterly Business Review package presented to the client. True = include in QBR; False = standard operational measurement only.

The actual QoS rate for the measurement period, expressed as a percentage (0–100). Typically calculated as the ratio of client-accepted submittals to total submittals, or interview-to-submittal ratio. Measures the quality and relevance of candidate submittals.

Total number of open requisitions (job orders) that fell within the measurement period scope and were included in the SLA calculation. Provides the denominator context for fill rate and submittal ratio calculations.

The actual assignment retention rate for the measurement period expressed as a percentage (0–100). Measures the proportion of placed workers who remained on assignment through the expected end date, excluding early terminations and fall-offs.

The specific KPI (Key Performance Indicator) being measured in this record. TTF = Time to Fill; TTS = Time to Start; fill_rate = percentage of requisitions filled; QoS = Quality of Submission rate; submittal_ratio = submittals per req; retention_rate = assignment retention. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ttf|tts|fill_rate|qos|submittal_ratio|retention_rate|on_time_start|absence_rate — promote to reference product]
Total number of candidate submittals made to the client across all in-scope requisitions during the measurement period. Used in QoS (Quality of Submission) and submittal ratio calculations.

The contractually agreed performance threshold for the SLA metric as defined in the SLA configuration. Represents what was promised to the client in the MSA (Master Service Agreement) or SLA addendum. Used as the benchmark for compliance determination.

business_glossary_term: Time to Fill (TTF) Actual Average Days
description: The actual average number of calendar days from requisition open date to confirmed placement (fill) across all in-scope requisitions during the measurement period. Core TTF KPI metric used in SLA compliance evaluation and client QBR reporting.

business_glossary_term: Time to Start (TTS) Actual Average Days
description: The actual average number of calendar days from requisition open date to the workers first day on assignment across all in-scope placements during the measurement period. TTS measures end-to-end speed including onboarding and credentialing.
The timestamp when this SLA measurement record was last modified. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and Silver layer delta processing.

The variance expressed as a percentage of the target value ((measured_value - target_value) / target_value * 100). Enables normalized comparison of SLA performance across different metric types and clients regardless of unit scale.

The arithmetic difference between measured_value and target_value (measured_value minus target_value). Positive variance indicates over-performance; negative variance indicates under-performance. Used to quantify the degree of SLA compliance or breach for penalty/bonus triggers.

Unique surrogate identifier for the VMS program record in the Staffing Hr lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running, binding contingent workforce program administered through a VMS platform.
Reference to the client account that owns and administers this VMS program. Links the program to the authoritative client identity record in the client domain.

Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: A VMS program has a designated client-side program manager or VMS administrator contact who is the primary liaison for the contingent workforce program. Linking vms_program to client_contact via clien

Percentage fee charged to the client when a temporary worker placed through this VMS program converts to a permanent (direct hire) employee. Governs temp-to-perm and contract-to-hire financial terms within the program.

Timestamp when this VMS program record was first created in the Staffing Hr lakehouse. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Sourced from Beeline VMS integration load.

description: Indicates whether a Data Privacy Addendum (DPA) or data processing agreement has been executed for this VMS program. Required for programs subject to GDPR, CCPA, or other data privacy regulations where worker personal data is shared with the clients VMS platform.
Indicates whether a pre-placement drug screening is required for all workers entering this VMS program. Impacts onboarding timelines and compliance with client safety and OSHA requirements.

Indicates whether the VMS program mandates use of an Employer of Record (EOR) arrangement for worker engagement. When true, Staffing Hr or a designated EOR entity assumes employer obligations including payroll, benefits, and compliance.

Name of the Managed Service Provider (MSP) that administers the VMS program on behalf of the client (e.g., Allegis Global Solutions, Pontoon, Workforce Logiq). Null when program_type is self-managed. Critical for understanding the intermediary controlling requisition flow and supplier tiering.
Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) has been executed and is on file for this VMS program. Required for programs involving access to sensitive client data or proprietary information.

Indicates whether the VMS program requires Staffing Hr to provide a dedicated on-site coordinator or account manager at the client location. Impacts staffing firm resource allocation and cost structure for program participation.

Maximum allowable markup percentage for overtime (OT) hours billed through this VMS program. May differ from the standard markup cap. Governs gross margin on OT billing and ensures FLSA-compliant rate submissions.

Indicates whether per diem expense reimbursements are permitted for workers placed through this VMS program. Relevant for travel-intensive assignments and impacts payroll burden calculations in TempWorks.

Indicates whether a Purchase Order (PO) number is required on all invoices submitted through this VMS program. Impacts invoice processing workflow and accounts receivable management in Oracle NetSuite ERP.

Operational model under which the VMS program is administered. MSP-managed indicates a third-party Managed Service Provider controls supplier relationships and requisition flow; self-managed means the client administers directly; hybrid combines both models. CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.

Rule or logic governing how new requisitions are distributed to suppliers within this VMS program (e.g., sequential by tier, broadcast to all tier-1, round-robin, category-based routing). Determines how Staffing Hr receives job orders and the competitive dynamics of the program.

SLA tier classification assigned to this VMS program, reflecting the contractual service commitment level. Higher tiers carry stricter TTF, TTS, and QoS requirements. Aligns with the SLA tier defined in the client MSA.

Maximum number of candidate submittals Staffing Hr is permitted to submit per requisition within this VMS program. Enforced by the VMS platform. Impacts sourcing strategy and Quality of Submission (QoS) focus.

URL of the VMS supplier portal through which Staffing Hr accesses requisitions, submits candidates, and manages worker records for this program. Operational reference for recruiter and account management teams.

Target number of calendar days from requisition open date to placement confirmation as defined in the VMS program SLA. Key performance indicator (KPI) for program compliance and client satisfaction measurement.

Maximum number of hours from requisition release to candidate submittal allowed under this VMS programs SLA. Drives internal sourcing urgency and recruiter workflow prioritization. Measured in business hours unless otherwise specified.

Frequency at which worker timesheets are submitted and approved within this VMS program. Drives Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling, payroll processing cycles in TempWorks, and billing cadence in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.

Percentage fee charged by the VMS platform or MSP to Staffing Hr on each placement or billing transaction within this program. Deducted from the spread and directly reduces gross margin. Expressed as a percentage of bill rate (e.g., 2.50 = 2.5%).

Name of the VMS technology platform through which this contingent workforce program is administered. Determines integration protocols, API endpoints, and data exchange formats used by Staffing Hr to receive requisitions and submit candidates.

Workers Compensation insurance classification code applicable to the primary labor category within this VMS program. Drives burden rate calculations, insurance premium allocation, and payroll processing in TempWorks.

Composite weighted performance score for the vendor across all KPI dimensions for the evaluation period. Expressed as a numeric value on a 0–100 scale. Drives tier reclassification and program allocation decisions.

Name or identifier of the MSP program manager or client stakeholder who approved this allocation assignment. Provides an audit trail for allocation governance and change management.
The date on which this allocation was formally approved by the MSP program manager or client stakeholder. Establishes the governance record for allocation activation.

Indicates whether job orders matching this allocations job category are automatically distributed to this vendor by Beeline VMS without manual MSP intervention. When True, the VMS auto-routes requisitions based on tier and allocation percentage. When False, manual distribution is required.

Indicates whether the vendor has completed all required compliance verifications (insurance certificates, E-Verify enrollment, background check program agreement, OFCCP compliance attestation) to be active under this program allocation.
The date on which the vendors compliance credentials were last verified and confirmed as meeting program requirements. Used to track compliance currency and trigger re-verification workflows.
The timestamp when this program allocation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and SOC 2 compliance.

The date on which this vendor program allocation becomes active and the vendor is eligible to receive job order distributions under the specified terms. Aligns with MSP program cycle start or tier reclassification decision date.

Indicates whether this vendor holds exclusive rights to fill requisitions in the specified job category within this VMS program. When True, no other vendor may receive job order distributions for this category. Typically reserved for niche or specialized labor categories.

The date on which this vendor program allocation expires and the vendor is no longer eligible to receive job order distributions under the specified terms. Nullable for open-ended allocations. Typically aligned with program review cycles or scorecard-driven reclassification events.

The number of business days this vendor has exclusive or priority access to a new requisition before it is released to lower-tier vendors. Enforces preferred supplier first-look access per MSP program rules. Applicable only to Tier 1 and Preferred vendors.

Indicates whether this vendor is authorized to supply Independent Contractors (ICs) under this program allocation. When True, the vendor may submit 1099 or Corp-to-Corp workers. Requires additional IC compliance validation per IRS worker classification rules.

The maximum hourly bill rate the vendor is authorized to charge the client for placements made under this allocation and job category. Enforced by Beeline VMS rate card controls. Expressed in the programs operating currency (USD).

The maximum number of candidate submittals this vendor is permitted to have active simultaneously for a single requisition within this program and job category. Controls submission quota per Beeline VMS program configuration to enforce Quality of Submission (QoS) standards.

Free-text field for MSP program managers to capture contextual notes about this allocation, such as rationale for tier assignment, special client instructions, or conditions attached to the allocation. Not used for structured data.

Numeric rank of this vendor within its assigned tier for the job category. Lower values indicate higher priority for job order distribution when multiple vendors share the same tier. Used by Beeline VMS to sequence first-look access among same-tier suppliers.

The target number of calendar days from requisition approval to worker start date for placements made under this allocation. Represents the Time to Start (TTS) SLA commitment for this vendor-program-job category combination.

The timestamp when this program allocation record was most recently modified. Tracks the last change event for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.

The native allocation or supplier program enrollment reference number assigned by Beeline VMS for this vendor-program-job category combination. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the Beeline VMS operational system.

The primary federal or state law or regulation governing this determination (e.g., FLSA, Davis-Bacon Act, Service Contract Act, California Labor Code §510, New York Minimum Wage Order). Supports multi-jurisdiction compliance tracking.

Assessed level of co-employment or joint employment risk for this worker engagement based on the degree of client control over the worker. High or critical risk may require EOR arrangement or contract restructuring to mitigate joint employer liability.

Free-text field for compliance officer annotations, determination rationale, exceptions, or special circumstances relevant to this wage and hour determination. Supports DOL audit documentation and FLSA litigation defense narrative.
Timestamp when this wage and hour determination record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements for DOL, IRS, and SOC 2 Type II compliance.

The principal business event date on which the wage and hour determination was formally made. This is the operative date for compliance purposes, DOL audit readiness, and FLSA litigation defense — distinct from record creation timestamp.

Externally-known business identifier for this determination record, used in DOL audit correspondence, compliance officer sign-off documentation, and FLSA litigation defense filings. Format: WHD-YYYY-NNNNNN.

Current lifecycle state of the wage and hour determination record. draft indicates initial assessment in progress; pending_review awaits compliance officer sign-off; approved is finalized and operative; superseded replaced by a newer determination; appealed under formal challenge; closed no longer active.

Indicates whether this determination record has been flagged as part of an active or anticipated DOL Wage and Hour Division audit. True triggers enhanced documentation retention and compliance officer review.

The official U.S. Department of Labor wage determination number (from SAM.gov or DOL Wage Determinations Online) applicable to this engagement under Davis-Bacon or Service Contract Act. Null if prevailing wage does not apply.

The daily or weekly hour threshold beyond which double-time (DT) pay is required. Applicable in states such as California where hours over 12 in a day or over 8 on the 7th consecutive day trigger DT. Null if DT does not apply to this engagement.

Date on which this wage and hour determination expires or is superseded. Null indicates the determination remains in effect indefinitely. Used to manage determination versioning and ensure current applicability.
Date from which this wage and hour determination becomes operative for the worker engagement. Pay rates, overtime thresholds, and classification rules apply starting this date.

The specific FLSA white-collar or other exemption category applied when the worker is classified as exempt. none applies to non-exempt workers. Required for DOL audit documentation and FLSA litigation defense to demonstrate the basis for exemption. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive|administrative|professional|computer_employee|outside_sales|highly_compensated|motor_carrier|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

FLSA overtime exemption classification for the worker. exempt workers are not entitled to overtime pay; non_exempt workers must receive 1.5x pay for hours over 40/week; highly_compensated_exempt meets the HCE threshold; partial_exempt applies to specific industry exemptions (e.g., motor carrier). Core field for FLSA litigation defense.

The job title of the worker at the time of this determination. Used to validate FLSA exemption basis (e.g., executive, professional), prevailing wage job classification, and pay equity peer group comparisons.
Indicates whether this determination record is subject to a litigation hold due to active or threatened FLSA litigation. Records under litigation hold must not be modified or deleted.

The minimum wage rate (per hour, USD) applicable to this worker based on federal, state, or local law — whichever is highest. Used to validate that pay_rate_validated meets or exceeds the legal floor. Supports state-specific minimum wage compliance tracking.

The jurisdiction (federal, state, or local municipality) whose minimum wage rate was applied as the floor for this determination. Examples: Federal, California, New York City. Supports multi-jurisdiction compliance reporting.

The pay rate multiplier applied to hours worked beyond the OT threshold. Standard FLSA rate is 1.5x (time-and-a-half); double-time (DT) states or contracts may require 2.0x. Used in payroll processing and timesheet OT rule configuration.

The weekly (or daily, for states like California) hour threshold beyond which overtime pay is required for this worker. Standard federal FLSA threshold is 40 hours/week; California requires daily OT after 8 hours. Drives timesheet OT calculation rules.

Result of the pay equity review for this worker engagement. compliant indicates no pay disparity found; gap_identified flags a potential pay equity issue requiring remediation; remediated indicates a gap was corrected; not_applicable when no pay equity review was required.

Indicates whether a pay equity assessment was conducted as part of this determination. True triggers documentation of the pay equity analysis outcome. Supports EEOC and OFCCP pay equity compliance reporting.

The compensation structure type for the validated pay rate. hourly for time-based workers; salary for exempt salaried employees; daily for day-rate engagements; piece_rate for output-based pay; per_diem for daily allowance-based arrangements. Affects overtime calculation methodology.

The workers pay rate (per hour) that was validated against applicable minimum wage, prevailing wage, or FLSA salary threshold requirements as part of this determination. Expressed in USD per hour. Core field for DOL audit readiness and pay equity assessments.

Indicates whether a prevailing wage requirement (Davis-Bacon Act for federal construction contracts or Service Contract Act for federal service contracts) applies to this worker engagement. True triggers prevailing wage rate validation.

Specifies the prevailing wage framework applicable to this engagement. davis_bacon for federal construction projects; service_contract_act for federal service contracts; state_prevailing_wage for state-mandated prevailing wage laws; none when no prevailing wage applies.

The business event or reason that initiated this wage and hour determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_placement|rate_change|law_change|periodic_review|dol_audit|client_request|worker_complaint — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the workers salary meets the current FLSA minimum salary threshold required for white-collar exemption ($684/week as of 2024 federal standard). True is required for exempt classification under executive, administrative, or professional exemptions.
Date on which the compliance officer formally approved and signed off on this wage and hour determination. Required for DOL audit trail documentation.

Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification code for the workers role. Used for prevailing wage determination lookups (Davis-Bacon, Service Contract Act wage schedules), EEOC occupational category reporting, and OFCCP compliance.
The operational system of record from which this determination was originated or validated. Supports data lineage tracking across the lakehouse silver layer.
Timestamp when this wage and hour determination record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and silver layer incremental processing.

Two-letter US state code where the worker performs services. Determines applicable state minimum wage, state overtime rules, state prevailing wage laws, SUTA jurisdiction, and workers compensation requirements.
Unique system-generated identifier for each worker classification determination record. Primary key for the worker_classification data product.
Reference to the client account associated with this worker engagement and classification determination. Relevant for co-employment and joint employment risk assessment.

Indicates whether the state-level ABC Test (stricter than IRS Common Law Test) applies to this classification determination based on the work jurisdiction. True = ABC Test governs; False = IRS Common Law or DOL Economic Realities Test applies.

Outcome of the ABC Test evaluation where applicable. Pass = worker qualifies as independent contractor under all three prongs; Fail = worker must be classified as W-2 employee; Not Applicable = ABC Test does not govern this jurisdiction.

Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the degree of behavioral control the staffing firm or client exercises over the worker, per the IRS Common Law Test. Higher scores indicate greater control and stronger W-2 classification support.
Externally-known unique business identifier for this classification determination record, used in audit correspondence, legal filings, and IRS inquiries. Format: WC-YYYY-NNNNNN.

Current lifecycle status of this classification determination. Pending = under review; Active = in force; Superseded = replaced by a newer determination; Appealed = under formal challenge; Revoked = withdrawn due to error or legal order.

The determined classification type for the worker engagement. W2 = W-2 employee (temporary or permanent); 1099_IC = Independent Contractor (1099-MISC); 1099_NEC = Non-Employee Compensation contractor; C2C = Corp-to-Corp engagement; EOR = Employer of Record arrangement. This is the SSOT for IC vs. W-2 decisions.

The worker classification type explicitly requested by the client in the job order, MSA, or SOW. May differ from the compliance-determined classification. Used to track client preference vs. legal determination and flag misalignment.

Free-text field for compliance officers or legal counsel to document additional observations, risk factors, remediation actions, or contextual information relevant to this classification determination. Retained for audit trail.
Timestamp when this worker classification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field required for DOL, IRS, and SOC 2 compliance.

The legal or regulatory test framework used to arrive at the classification determination. Behavioral Control = IRS test on instruction/training; Financial Control = IRS test on investment/profit/loss; Relationship Type = IRS test on written contracts/benefits/permanency; ABC Test = state-level test (e.g., California AB5); Economic Realities = DOL FLSA test; Multi-Factor = combination of tests applied.
The date on which the formal worker classification determination was made and recorded. This is the principal business event date for this record.
The date from which this classification determination is binding and in effect for the worker engagement. May differ from determination_date if retroactive or prospective.
The date on which this classification determination expires or is superseded. Null for open-ended classifications. Populated when a new determination replaces this one or the engagement ends.

Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the degree of financial control the staffing firm or client exercises over the worker, per the IRS Common Law Test. Considers investment in tools, opportunity for profit/loss, and services available to the general market.

Indicates whether a signed Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is on file for this worker engagement. Tracked as part of the compliance documentation package for the classification record.

Indicates whether a signed Non-Compete Agreement is on file for this worker. Relevant to classification because non-compete clauses are more consistent with employee relationships and may affect IC classification defensibility.

Indicates whether the final classification determination overrides the system-recommended or compliance-officer-recommended classification. True = an override was applied; False = determination follows standard process. Triggers mandatory documentation and senior review.

Free-text explanation documenting the business or legal justification for overriding the standard classification recommendation. Required when override_flag is True. Retained for audit trail and IRS inquiry response.

The prior classification type for this worker on this engagement, captured when a reclassification event occurs. Enables audit trail of classification changes and supports IRS back-period liability analysis.
Date on which a workers classification was changed from a prior type to the current classification_type. Null if this is the original classification with no prior change.

The event or authority that triggered a reclassification of the worker. Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the nature of the relationship between the worker and the engaging party, per the IRS Common Law Test. Considers written contracts, employee benefits, permanency of relationship, and integral business activity.

description: Full name of the compliance officer, HR attorney, or legal counsel who reviewed and approved this classification determination. Required for audit trail and regulatory defense.

The operational system of record from which this classification determination originated or was last updated. Supports data lineage and reconciliation across Bullhorn ATS/CRM, SAP SuccessFactors, TempWorks Payroll, Beeline VMS, or manual entry.

The IRSs official determination result from Form SS-8 review. Employee = IRS determined worker is an employee; Independent Contractor = IRS confirmed IC status; Pending = IRS review in progress; Withdrawn = SS-8 filing was withdrawn.

The scheduled or actual start date of the worker assignment or placement. Compliance clearance must be achieved prior to this date. Used to flag placements where clearance was obtained after assignment start.

The date on which the background check screening was completed and a final result was returned by Sterling Background Check. Used to assess BGC turnaround time and compliance with client SLA requirements.

Status of the background check (BGC) screening for this placement as reported by Sterling Background Check. clear indicates no disqualifying findings. adverse_action indicates findings requiring pre-adverse and adverse action notices per FCRA. waived indicates client or internal authorization to proceed without BGC.

Count of individual compliance check items that are currently in a failed or pending status that would block placement activation. A value of 0 with check_status = cleared confirms full compliance. Used in real-time compliance dashboards to prioritize remediation.

Overall lifecycle status of the placement compliance gate-check. cleared indicates all required checks passed and placement may go active. failed blocks placement from generating billable hours. waived indicates authorized exception granted. expired indicates a previously cleared check has lapsed and requires renewal.

Classifies the nature of the compliance gate-check. initial applies to new placements, extension to assignment extensions, reactivation to returning workers, annual_renewal for ongoing long-term assignments, client_change when a worker transfers to a new client.

Name or identifier of the authorized approver who granted a compliance waiver for this placement (if applicable). Required when check_status = waived or overall_compliance_result = conditional_pass. Provides audit trail for waiver accountability.
The date on which a granted compliance waiver expires. After this date, the outstanding compliance item must be completed or the placement may be suspended. Drives automated compliance expiry alerts.

Documented business justification for any compliance waiver granted for this placement. Required for audit trail integrity. Examples include client-approved exceptions, emergency placements with post-start completion commitments, or regulatory exemptions.

Aggregate risk rating assigned to the audit based on the severity and volume of findings. Rolled up from individual finding risk ratings. Drives escalation protocols and executive reporting. Values: low, medium, high, critical.

Indicates whether attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine has been asserted over audit materials. When True, certain audit documents are restricted from disclosure. Relevant for regulatory audits where legal exposure exists.

Indicates whether this audit contains one or more findings that were also identified in a prior audit cycle (repeat findings). Repeat findings signal systemic control failures and typically result in elevated regulatory scrutiny and penalties.
The date on which the formal audit report (draft or final) was issued to stakeholders. Triggers the remediation clock for findings with regulatory deadlines.

Number of records, transactions, or workers included in the audit sample. Applicable when audit_methodology is statistical_sample or targeted_sample. Used to calculate deficiency rates and statistical confidence levels.

Secondary classification providing additional specificity within the audit type. For example, within a payroll_tax_audit: FICA, FUTA, SUTA, or worker classification (W-2 vs 1099/IC). Enables granular reporting and trend analysis by compliance sub-domain.

Number of client accounts impacted by this regulatory violation. Relevant for systemic violations spanning multiple client engagements (e.g., widespread I-9 non-compliance, multi-site OSHA violations). Supports enterprise-wide compliance risk assessment.

Required for tracking formal enforcement actions and agency correspondence. Nullable for internally-discovered violations not yet reported to an agency.

Indicates whether this violation involves or creates co-employment or joint employment risk between the staffing firm and the client. Triggers enhanced legal review and client notification protocols. Critical for MSP/VMS program compliance and liability management.

Timestamp when the regulatory violation record was first created in the compliance system. Supports audit trail requirements mandated by DOL, OSHA, and IRS regulatory frameworks. Distinct from discovery_date which reflects the real-world event time.

Indicates whether this violation constitutes or involves a personal data breach requiring notification under GDPR Article 33, CCPA, or FCRA. Triggers the 72-hour GDPR breach notification clock and state-specific breach notification timelines.

The calendar date on which the regulatory violation was first identified or discovered. Serves as the principal business event date for the violation record. Critical for calculating regulatory response deadlines, statute of limitations tracking, and trending analysis.

The mechanism by which the regulatory violation was identified: internal audit, external complaint, agency notice, whistleblower report, client notification, or self-assessment. Critical for root cause analysis and improving proactive compliance controls.
